User Story: As a developer, I want to create a decentralized identifier using the did:peer method variant 4, so that I have a portable, self-contained DID with embedded cryptographic material.
Expected Behaviour: DIDManager.createDIDPeer() generates an Ed25519 or configured defaultKeyType keypair, creates a did:peer using @aviarytech/did-peer library's createNumAlgo4() with Multikey verification method, resolves it to a full DID document, ensures verificationMethod entries have controller field, sets authentication and assertionMethod to the first verification method ID. Optionally returns keypair if returnKeyPair=true. See DIDManager.ts:26-83.
Edge Cases: Empty verification methods array requires fallback to set authentication/assertionMethod; No keypair provided uses configured defaultKeyType from config; Missing controller field on verification methods is automatically populated
Notes: Deps: @aviarytech/did-peer (createNumAlgo4, resolve functions), KeyManager for key generation, OriginalsConfig.defaultKeyType

User Story: As a developer, I want to migrate an existing DID document to the did:webvh method, so that I can use web-based verification of my decentralized identity.
Expected Behaviour: DIDManager.migrateToDIDWebVH() takes a DIDDocument and optional domain. Derives a stable slug from the original DID's method/suffix (stripping invalid characters), validates domain (supporting localhost, IP addresses, and DNS for production), constructs did:webvh:domain:slug format, returns new document with updated ID. See DIDManager.ts:85-129.
Edge Cases: Domain with port number (e.g., localhost:3000) is validated and supported; Non-standard DID methods fallback to last segment as suffix; Domain characters not matching [a-z0-9._-] are replaced with '-'; IP addresses and localhost bypass strict DNS validation
Notes: Deps: OriginalsConfig.webvhNetwork (optional, defaults to DEFAULT_WEBVH_NETWORK), getNetworkDomain() for default domain lookup

User Story: As a developer, I want to migrate an existing DID document to the did:btco method anchored to a satoshi number, so that I can leverage Bitcoin's immutability for my identity.
Expected Behaviour: DIDManager.migrateToDIDBTCO() validates satoshi parameter (0 to MAX_SATOSHI_SUPPLY), determines Bitcoin network from webvhNetwork config or explicit config, extracts public key from first verification method (multikey format), calls createBtcoDidDocument() with key material, preserves service endpoints. Returns minimal document if no key material available. See DIDManager.ts:131-191.
Edge Cases: Satoshi validation fails for negative, non-numeric, or > MAX_SATOSHI_SUPPLY values; Unable to decode first verification method's public key results in minimal document without keys; Network is derived from webvhNetwork config (takes precedence over network config); Service endpoints from original document are carried forward
Notes: Deps: OriginalsConfig.webvhNetwork (optional, takes precedence), OriginalsConfig.network (fallback), validateSatoshiNumber() and MAX_SATOSHI_SUPPLY constants, multikey.decodePublicKey() for key extraction, createBtcoDidDocument() function

User Story: As a developer, I want to resolve a DID from any supported method (did:peer, did:webvh, did:btco), so that I can fetch and validate DID documents uniformly.
Expected Behaviour: DIDManager.resolveDID() dispatches to method-specific resolver based on DID prefix. did:peer uses @aviarytech/did-peer.resolve(), did:btco uses BtcoDidResolver, did:webvh uses didwebvh-ts.resolveDID(). Returns DIDDocument or null on fatal error. Handles resolution failures gracefully by returning minimal document with just @context and id. See DIDManager.ts:193-240.
Edge Cases: did:btco variants: did:btco: (mainnet), did:btco:test: (legacy), did:btco:sig: (signet); did:peer resolution fails -> returns minimal document with just id/context; did:webvh resolution fails -> returns minimal document; Unknown method prefix -> returns minimal document; RPC URL defaults to http://localhost:3000 if not configured
Notes: Deps: @aviarytech/did-peer (resolve function), didwebvh-ts (resolveDID function), BtcoDidResolver class, OrdinalsClient for Bitcoin resolution, OrdinalsClientProviderAdapter, OriginalsConfig.bitcoinRpcUrl

User Story: As a developer, I want to validate that a DID document conforms to the W3C specification, so that I can ensure data integrity in my application.
Expected Behaviour: DIDManager.validateDIDDocument() returns true if document has a non-empty @context array and an id field. See DIDManager.ts:242-244.
Edge Cases: Null or undefined document returns false; @context must be an array (not string or object); id field must exist and be a string
Notes: Deps:

User Story: As a developer, I want to create a did:webvh identifier with cryptographic signing, optionally using an external key management system, so that I can integrate with services like Turnkey while maintaining control over my keys.
Expected Behaviour: DIDManager.createDIDWebVH() dynamically imports didwebvh-ts, accepts domain (defaults from webvhNetwork config), generates Ed25519 keypair or uses provided keypair, creates Multikey verification method, instantiates OriginalsWebVHSigner adapter, calls createDID() with signer/verifier/updateKeys/verificationMethods, validates result, optionally saves log to disk. External signer mode requires verificationMethods and updateKeys. See DIDManager.ts:270-387.
Edge Cases: externalSigner provided without verificationMethods/updateKeys throws error; External signer doesn't get keypair returned (returns empty keypair instead); outputDir provided -> log saved to did.jsonl in domain-prefixed directory structure; didwebvh-ts module export validation fails -> throws error; Domain defaults to network domain if not provided
Notes: Deps: didwebvh-ts (createDID, prepareDataForSigning functions), KeyManager.generateKeyPair('Ed25519'), OriginalsWebVHSigner adapter class, Ed25519Signer for internal signing, multikey.encodeMultibase() for proof encoding, OriginalsConfig.webvhNetwork

User Story: As a developer, I want to update a did:webvh document by appending a new log entry, so that I can change service endpoints or other metadata while preserving the history.
Expected Behaviour: DIDManager.updateDIDWebVH() imports didwebvh-ts.updateDID, accepts current log and updates object, creates internal or external signer, merges updates with current document (preserving DID id), calls updateDID(), validates result, optionally saves updated log. See DIDManager.ts:394-483.
Edge Cases: Current log must contain at least one entry (accessed via [length-1]); Updates object merged with current doc; DID id cannot be changed; Signer can be external (with sign/getVerificationMethodId) or keypair (with privateKey/publicKey); Verifier optional; if not provided, signer used as verifier for keypair mode; outputDir provided -> updated log saved to disk
Notes: Deps: didwebvh-ts (updateDID, prepareDataForSigning functions), OriginalsWebVHSigner adapter, Ed25519Signer, saveDIDLog() method for persistence

User Story: As a developer, I want to rotate the signing keys for a did:webvh identifier, so that I can refresh my cryptographic material while maintaining identity continuity.
Expected Behaviour: DIDManager.rotateDIDWebVHKeys() delegates to WebVHManager.rotateDIDWebVHKeys() which appends a new log entry that replaces verification method and updateKey with new keypair, signed by current keypair. Current keypair must be authorized in previous entry's updateKeys. New keypair becomes both verification method and updateKey for next rotation. Optionally saves updated log. See WebVHManager.ts:556-568.
Edge Cases: Current keypair must be authorized by previous entry's updateKeys (validation delegated to didwebvh-ts); newKeyPair optional; fresh Ed25519 pair generated if omitted; outputDir provided -> updated log saved to disk; Rotation fails if current keypair not authorized -> didwebvh-ts.updateDID() throws
Notes: Deps: WebVHManager.rotateDIDWebVHKeys(), WebVHManager.appendKeyChange() private method, didwebvh-ts (updateDID, prepareDataForSigning), KeyManager.generateKeyPair('Ed25519'), OriginalsWebVHSigner adapter

User Story: As a developer, I want to recover a did:webvh after key compromise, so that I can regain control and issue a W3C KeyRecoveryCredential documenting the event.
Expected Behaviour: DIDManager.recoverDIDWebVH() delegates to WebVHManager.recoverDIDWebVH() which performs key rotation with compromised keypair, then generates KeyRecoveryCredential documenting recovery reason, compromised verification methods, new verification method. Credentials conform to W3C VC spec with issuer=did, type=[VerifiableCredential, KeyRecoveryCredential]. See WebVHManager.ts:575-609.
Edge Cases: recoveryKeyPair optional; fresh Ed25519 pair generated if omitted; Compromised key used to authorize recovery entry (must be in previous updateKeys); credentialSubject.recoveryReason hardcoded as 'key_compromise'; previousVerificationMethodIds extracted from latest log entry; newVerificationMethod falls back to did:key: format if not found in log; outputDir provided -> updated log saved to disk
Notes: Deps: WebVHManager.recoverDIDWebVH(), WebVHManager.appendKeyChange() for key rotation portion, WebVHManager.extractVerificationMethodIds() helper, KeyManager.generateKeyPair('Ed25519'), Current date/time for issuanceDate and recoveredAt

User Story: As a developer, I want to persist did:webvh logs to disk and load them back, so that I can manage DID history across application restarts.
Expected Behaviour: saveDIDLog() parses did:webvh:domain:path1:path2 format, validates all path segments (no '.', '..', separators, absolute paths), sanitizes domain, constructs baseDir/did/domain/path/did.jsonl, verifies output path stays within baseDir (defense in depth), creates directories, writes JSONL (one JSON entry per line). loadDIDLog() reads file, splits on newlines, parses each line as JSON DIDLogEntry. See DIDManager.ts:519-585.
Edge Cases: DID path segments validated individually to prevent directory traversal; Domain sanitized: lowercase, non-[a-z0-9._-] chars replaced with '_'; Path resolution verified to stay within baseDir (resolved paths checked); Empty log file parsing results in empty array; JSONL format: newline-delimited JSON, not array; Resolved paths checked with path.relative() to catch '..' escape attempts
Notes: Deps: fs.promises.mkdir(), writeFile(), readFile(), path module for joining, resolving, checking absolute paths, isValidPathSegment() private method for segment validation

User Story: As a developer, I want to generate keypairs of various types (ES256K, Ed25519, ES256), so that I can support different cryptographic algorithms for DIDs.
Expected Behaviour: KeyManager.generateKeyPair() accepts KeyType ('ES256K', 'Ed25519', 'ES256'), generates random private key using @noble crypto library (secp256k1 for ES256K, ed25519 for Ed25519, p256 for ES256), derives public key, encodes both as multibase strings, returns KeyPair. See KeyManager.ts:30-61.
Edge Cases: Ed25519 uses async getPublicKeyAsync() method; ES256K and ES256 use synchronous getPublicKey() with 'true' flag for compression; All keys encoded via multikey.encodePrivateKey/encodePublicKey; Unsupported type throws exhaustiveness error
Notes: Deps: @noble/secp256k1 (for ES256K), @noble/ed25519 (for Ed25519), @noble/curves/p256 (for ES256), multikey.encodePrivateKey() and encodePublicKey()

User Story: As a developer, I want to rotate keys in a DID document, so that I can refresh authentication and assertion methods while preserving the document history.
Expected Behaviour: KeyManager.rotateKeys() updates DID document by marking all existing verification methods with 'revoked' timestamp, generating new key ID (didId#keys-index), creating new verification method with public key, updating authentication/assertionMethod to reference only new key, preserving multikey and security contexts, preserving keyAgreement/capabilityInvocation/capabilityDelegation/service. See KeyManager.ts:63-122.
Edge Cases: No existing verification methods -> empty verificationMethod array in result; Missing contexts -> multikey and security contexts are added; Existing keyAgreement/capabilityInvocation/capabilityDelegation/service preserved as-is; New key index calculated from existing verification methods length
Notes: Deps: multikey encoding for public key, Current timestamp generation via Date.toISOString()

Feature Name: Recover From Key Compromise in DID Document
User Story: As a developer, I want to recover a DID document after detecting key compromise, so that I can mark compromised keys and issue a recovery credential for audit purposes.
Expected Behaviour: KeyManager.recoverFromCompromise() detects key type from first existing verification method (falls back to Ed25519), generates new keypair of same type, marks all existing verification methods with 'compromised' timestamp, collects previous method IDs, creates new verification method at higher index, updates authentication/assertionMethod to new key, generates W3C KeyRecoveryCredential documenting event, preserves optional fields. See KeyManager.ts:124-219.
Edge Cases: No existing verification methods -> defaults to Ed25519 and generates new pair; Failed key type detection from first method -> fallback to Ed25519; Missing contexts -> multikey and security contexts added; previousVerificationMethodIds may be sparse (some IDs might be missing); New key index calculated from existing verification methods length
Notes: Deps: multikey.decodePublicKey() to detect key type, KeyManager.generateKeyPair() for new key generation, Current timestamp generation, fromMultikeyType() helper for type conversion

User Story: As a developer, I want to encode raw key bytes as multibase strings and decode them back, so that I can work with keys in different representations.
Expected Behaviour: KeyManager.encodePublicKeyMultibase() takes Buffer and KeyType, converts to Uint8Array, calls multikey.encodePublicKey(). decodePublicKeyMultibase() takes multibase string, calls multikey.decodePublicKey(), returns { key: Buffer, type: KeyType }. See KeyManager.ts:221-236.
Edge Cases: Invalid multibase string throws error; Null/undefined encoded string throws 'Invalid multibase string' error; Type must be in MultikeyType enum ('Secp256k1', 'Ed25519', 'P256'); Conversion fails for unsupported key types
Notes: Deps: multikey.encodePublicKey() and decodePublicKey(), toMultikeyType() and fromMultikeyType() converters

User Story: As a developer, I want to resolve a did:btco DID by fetching inscriptions from a satoshi number, so that I can retrieve DID documents anchored on Bitcoin.
Expected Behaviour: BtcoDidResolver.resolve() parses did:btco format (supports did:btco:, did:btco:reg:, did:btco:sig:), calls provider.getSatInfo(satNumber) to get inscription IDs, iterates each inscription to fetch content via configurable fetchFn (default: global fetch), checks content for 'BTCO DID:' prefix matching expected DID, validates metadata as DID document, detects deactivation via '🔥' character, returns latest valid document or error result. See BtcoDidResolver.ts:76-207.
Edge Cases: Multiple inscriptions on same satoshi -> returns latest valid document; Inscription marked with '🔥' -> treated as deactivated (didDocument=null); Content fetch timeout (configurable, default 10s) -> AbortController used; Metadata validation fails -> error recorded, continues to next inscription; No provider supplied -> returns 'noProvider' error result; No inscriptions found -> returns 'notFound' error result; HTTP error during fetch -> caught and recorded in inscriptionData.error
Notes: Deps: ResourceProviderLike (provider interface with getSatInfo, resolveInscription, getMetadata), Configurable fetchFn (default: global fetch), AbortSignal.timeout() for fetch timeout, Optional options.fetchFn and options.timeout per-call override

User Story: As a developer, I want to create a did:btco DID document anchored to a satoshi number, so that I can prepare documents for Bitcoin inscription.
Expected Behaviour: createBtcoDidDocument() validates satoshi number, determines DID prefix based on network (mainnet/regtest/signet), builds verification method with public key encoded as multibase, creates document with W3C contexts, sets authentication and assertionMethod to verification method ID. See createBtcoDidDocument.ts:33-59.
Edge Cases: Satoshi validation enforces 0 <= satNumber <= MAX_SATOSHI_SUPPLY; Network determines prefix: mainnet='did:btco:', regtest='did:btco:reg:', signet='did:btco:sig:'; Verification method fragment always '#0'; Controller defaults to DID itself if not provided in params
Notes: Deps: validateSatoshiNumber() utility, multikey.encodePublicKey() for key encoding, getDidPrefix() helper for network-specific prefix

User Story: As a developer, I want to cache resolved DID documents with time-to-live (TTL) expiration and LRU eviction, so that I can reduce resolution latency and RPC calls.
Expected Behaviour: DIDCache stores entries in-memory with configurable TTL (default 24h) and max size (default 1000). Expired entries return null. LRU eviction removes least recently used unpinned entry when full. Pinned entries never expire/evict. Optional pluggable storage for persistence. Metrics collector tracks cache hits/misses. See DIDCache.ts:51-279.
Edge Cases: Accessing expired entry removes it and returns null; Setting new entry when full evicts LRU unpinned entry; Pinned entries cannot be evicted (throws if all entries pinned); Unpinning entry restarts TTL countdown from current time; Storage adapter handles get/set persistence independently; load() called at startup to hydrate cache from storage
Notes: Deps: DIDCacheStorage interface (optional) for persistence, MetricsCollector interface (optional) for metrics, DIDDocument type for cached documents

User Story: As a developer, I want to pin important DID documents so they never expire or get evicted, so that I can ensure critical identities remain available.
Expected Behaviour: DIDCache.pin() marks entry as pinned (or caches new one if not present), never expires, never evicted. Pinning without document requires existing cache entry. unpin() restarts TTL countdown from current time. isPinned() returns boolean, listPinned() returns all pinned DIDs. See DIDCache.ts:148-201.
Edge Cases: Pinning non-cached DID without document throws error; Pinning new document accepts optional DIDDocument parameter; Unpinning non-existent DID silently returns (no error); Unpinning entry immediately makes it subject to expiration; Pinned entry remains in accessOrder set for consistency
Notes: Deps: Internal cache Map and accessOrder Set, Optional DIDCacheStorage for persistence

User Story: As a developer, I want to verify Ed25519 signatures in DID documents, so that I can validate cryptographic proofs for did:webvh and other methods.
Expected Behaviour: Ed25519Verifier.verify() takes signature/message/publicKey as Uint8Array, handles 33-byte keys by stripping version byte, delegates to @noble/ed25519.verifyAsync(), returns boolean. getVerificationMethodId() returns stored ID, getPublicKey() returns stored key, getPublicKeyMultibase() returns base64url-encoded key with 'z' prefix. See Ed25519Verifier.ts:8-68.
Edge Cases: Public key can be 32 or 33 bytes (33-byte form has version byte stripped); Invalid key length (not 32 or 33) returns false with console.error; Verification error caught, logged, and returns false; getVerificationMethodId() returns undefined if not set in constructor; getPublicKeyMultibase() returns undefined if publicKey not set
Notes: Deps: @noble/ed25519.verifyAsync() for signature verification, Buffer for base64 encoding

User Story: As a developer, I want to adapt the OrdinalsClient to provide satoshi/inscription data for DID resolution, so that I can resolve did:btco identifiers using a consistent interface.
Expected Behaviour: OrdinalsClientProviderAdapter implements ResourceProviderLike interface, delegates getSatInfo/getMetadata to OrdinalsClient, implements resolveInscription by fetching from base URL. Configurable fetch function, timeout (default 10s), accepts config string or object. See providers/OrdinalsClientProviderAdapter.ts:10-65.
Edge Cases: Constructor accepts baseUrl as string or OrdinalsClientProviderConfig object; baseUrl trailing slash stripped before constructing URL; Missing baseUrl throws 'requires a baseUrl' error; HTTP error logged as warning but rethrown; AbortSignal.timeout() used for fetch abort; Default fetch function is global fetch if not configured
Notes: Deps: OrdinalsClient for getSatInfo/getMetadata, Configurable fetchFn (default: global fetch), AbortSignal.timeout() for timeout handling

User Story: As a content creator, I want to create verifiable credentials that record resource lifecycle events (creation, update, migration) so that I have an auditable, cryptographically-signed proof of asset history.
Expected Behaviour: CredentialManager.issueResourceCredential() creates unsigned VerifiableCredential with type ResourceCreated, captures resource metadata (id, type, hash, contentType, creator, createdAt) in credentialSubject, includes Originals context for term definitions. CredentialManager.issueResourceUpdateCredential() records version changes with previousHash, newHash, fromVersion, toVersion. CredentialManager.issueMigrationCredential() captures layer transitions with optional Bitcoin metadata (transactionId, inscriptionId, satoshi). See CredentialManager:547-571, 601-631, 658-693.
Edge Cases: Credential chain with previousCredential metadata may have mismatched chain links due to missing hash computation — chaining is optional but if specified, links must be validated later via verifyCredentialChain(); CreatedAt timestamp defaults to current ISO time if not provided in resource; Migration credential's targetDid is optional; if omitted, sourceDid is used as the subject id
Notes: Deps: CredentialManager instance with OriginalsConfig, DIDManager for optional verification method resolution, SHA-256 hashing for credential digests, Issuer DID for credentialSubject.id

User Story: As an issuer, I want to sign a credential with my private key to prove I issued it, supporting multiple key types and verification methods so that verifiers can cryptographically validate the proof.
Expected Behaviour: CredentialManager.signCredential() accepts unsigned credential, multibase-encoded private key, and verification method ID. If DIDManager present and verification method is a DID URL, attempts Data Integrity proof via Issuer class with eddsa-rdfc-2022 cryptosuite. Falls back to legacy signing: computes credential digest via computeCredentialDigest(), signs with configured key type (ES256K, Ed25519, or ES256), encodes proofValue as multibase, returns credential with DataIntegrityProof. See CredentialManager:196-243, Issuer:60-93.
Edge Cases: If DIDManager present and DID resolution fails, silently falls back to legacy local signer; Issuer must specify secretKeyMultibase for Data Integrity path; missing key throws error; Only Ed25519 is supported for eddsa-rdfc-2022 cryptosuite; other key types trigger error; proofValue is base64url multibase for legacy path, base58btc multibase for EdDSA Data Integrity
Notes: Deps: DIDManager (optional, for DID resolution), Signer implementations (ES256KSigner, Ed25519Signer, ES256Signer), DataIntegrityProofManager for eddsa-rdfc-2022, RDF canonicalization for document and proof config

User Story: As an issuer with a hardware wallet or third-party signing service (e.g., Turnkey), I want to sign credentials without exposing my private key to the SDK so that I maintain security while the SDK handles credential construction.
Expected Behaviour: CredentialManager.signCredentialWithExternalSigner() creates unsigned credential copy, builds proof structure with type DataIntegrityProof and eddsa-rdfc-2022 cryptosuite, calls external signer's sign() method passing unsigned document and proof structure, receives proofValue from signer, returns signed credential with proof attached. See CredentialManager:251-286.
Edge Cases: External signer must return exactly a proofValue in its response; manager does not validate signature independently; External signer's getVerificationMethodId() is trusted; no validation of signer identity against credential issuer; If signer fails, exception propagates to caller without fallback
Notes: Deps: ExternalSigner interface implementation (e.g., hardware wallet driver, Turnkey API client), No local cryptography needed

User Story: As a verifier, I want to verify that a credential's proof is valid and matches the issuer's key so that I can trust the credential data was not modified since issuance.
Expected Behaviour: CredentialManager.verifyCredential() checks if DIDManager present and proof has cryptosuite field indicating Data Integrity proof; if so, delegates to Verifier.verifyCredential(). Otherwise, extracts proofValue and verificationMethod from legacy proof, decodes multibase proofValue, computes credential digest, resolves verification method key via did:key extraction or DID document resolution, verifies signature using configured Signer, returns boolean. See CredentialManager:288-342, Verifier:24-52.
Edge Cases: did:key verification method is self-certifying; key embedded in DID must match credential issuer DID; Bare multibase public keys (not bound to a DID) are not trusted as root of trust; If DID resolution fails, credential fails verification rather than accepting bare key; Proof missing or malformed returns false without exception
Notes: Deps: DIDManager for optional DID resolution, Signer for cryptographic verification, RDF canonicalization for digest computation

User Story: As a verifier, I want to verify a credential's signature AND check if it has been revoked or suspended so that I can be sure the credential is currently valid.
Expected Behaviour: CredentialManager.verifyCredentialWithStatus() first verifies signature via verifyCredential(). If credential has credentialStatus of type BitstringStatusListEntry, checks status using StatusListManager.checkStatus(). Returns object with verified (signature valid), revoked (status purpose is revocation and bit is set), suspended (status purpose is suspension and bit is set), and errors array listing all issues. See CredentialManager:354-402.
Edge Cases: If credential has no credentialStatus, status checking is skipped (not an error); If statusListCredential not provided but credentialStatus present, error is recorded but signature verification result is still returned; Status purpose must match between entry and list; mismatch throws error during checkStatus()
Notes: Deps: StatusListManager for status bit checking, Status list credential (W3C Bitstring Status List format)

User Story: As an issuer, I want to revoke, suspend, or unsuspend credentials and check their status so that I can manage the lifecycle of credentials without reissuing them.
Expected Behaviour: CredentialManager.revokeCredential() extracts BitstringStatusListEntry from credential, validates statusPurpose is 'revocation', calls StatusListManager.setStatus() to set bit to true, returns updated status list credential. CredentialManager.suspendCredential() is similar but requires statusPurpose 'suspension'. CredentialManager.unsuspendCredential() clears the suspension bit (sets to false). CredentialManager.checkRevocationStatus() returns StatusCheckResult with isSet, statusPurpose, statusListIndex. CredentialManager.isRevoked() is convenience method returning boolean. See CredentialManager:1100-1197.
Edge Cases: Revocation is irreversible; once set, cannot be unset; Suspension is reversible via unsuspendCredential(); Credential without credentialStatus throws error; Wrong status purpose (e.g., trying to revoke a suspension credential) throws error with descriptive message
Notes: Deps: StatusListManager for bit manipulation, BitstringStatusList encoding/decoding (GZIP + base64url)

Feature Name: Allocate Status Entries for New Credentials
User Story: As an issuer preparing to issue credentials, I want to allocate a status entry (bit position) in a status list so that I can embed it in the credential and manage its revocation later.
Expected Behaviour: StatusListManager.allocateStatusEntry() takes status list credential URL, bit index (non-negative integer), and statusPurpose ('revocation' or 'suspension'), returns BitstringStatusListEntry object with id, type, statusPurpose, statusListIndex (as string), and statusListCredential URL. Manager does not check if index is already used or within list capacity; caller responsible for tracking allocations. See StatusListManager:115-131.
Edge Cases: Index is not validated against actual list capacity at allocation time; overflow is caught during setStatus(); Same index can be allocated multiple times (no deduplication tracking); statusListIndex is serialized as string per W3C spec
Notes: Deps: No external dependencies; pure data structure construction

User Story: As an issuer, I want to create a W3C-compliant status list credential that can hold revocation/suspension bits for multiple credentials so that I can manage a pool of credentials efficiently.
Expected Behaviour: StatusListManager.createStatusListCredential() takes options (id URL, issuer DID, statusPurpose, optional length), creates VerifiableCredential with type ['VerifiableCredential', 'BitstringStatusListCredential'], credentialSubject with type 'BitstringStatusList', and encodedList (GZIP-compressed, base64url-encoded bitstring). Default length is 131072 bits (minimum per W3C spec to preserve privacy). Returns unsigned credential. See StatusListManager:77-105.
Edge Cases: Length < 131072 throws error immediately; Returned credential is unsigned; caller must sign it; All bits are initially 0 (no credentials revoked/suspended); encodedList uses multibase 'u' prefix (base64url)
Notes: Deps: Node.js zlib for GZIP compression/decompression, Base64url encoding/decoding

User Story: As an issuer, I want to revoke or suspend multiple credentials in a single operation so that I can efficiently update the status list without reconstructing it multiple times.
Expected Behaviour: StatusListManager.batchSetStatus() takes status list credential and array of [index, value] tuples, decodes bitstring, applies all bit changes (value true = set, false = clear), re-encodes, returns new credential with updated encodedList and fresh issuanceDate. See StatusListManager:235-276.
Edge Cases: Each index is validated individually; if any is invalid, entire batch fails; No partial updates; either all succeed or all fail; Issuance date is updated to current time even if same bitstring
Notes: Deps: StatusListManager for decoding/encoding, Bitstring validation

Feature Name: Query Status List Capacity and Utilization
User Story: As an issuer, I want to check how many credential slots are available in a status list and how many have been used so that I can plan credential allocation.
Expected Behaviour: StatusListManager.getCapacity() decodes bitstring and returns total number of bit positions (bitstring.length * 8). StatusListManager.getSetCount() counts how many bits are currently set (revoked or suspended). See StatusListManager:281-304.
Edge Cases: Capacity is computed from encoded bitstring length, not the original length parameter; Set count includes all set bits; does not distinguish between revocation and suspension
Notes: Deps: GZIP decompression for bitstring decoding

User Story: As an issuer, I want to link credentials together in a chain so that I can create an auditable sequence of events (e.g., creation → update → migration) with cryptographic verification of linkage.
Expected Behaviour: CredentialManager methods issueResourceCredential(), issueResourceUpdateCredential(), issueMigrationCredential(), issueOwnershipCredential() accept optional chainOptions with previousCredentialId and previousCredentialHash. If provided, adds previousCredential metadata to credentialSubject. CredentialManager.verifyCredentialChain() takes array of credentials, verifies each individually via verifyCredential(), then validates chain links by comparing previousCredential.id with previous credential's id and previousCredential.hash with computed hash of previous credential. Returns validation result with errors and chainLength. See CredentialManager:764-809, 846-900.
Edge Cases: Chain verification requires all credentials present in order; missing intermediate credentials cannot be detected; Hash mismatch indicates chain broken (credential modified after issuance); First credential in array should have no previousCredential; Hash computation uses SHA-256 of RDF-canonicalized credential
Notes: Deps: RDF canonicalization for consistent credential hashing, SHA-256 hashing

User Story: As an issuer, I want to sign a credential with BBS+ so that holders can later derive selective disclosure proofs revealing only chosen fields while hiding others.
Expected Behaviour: CredentialManager.prepareSelectiveDisclosure() takes credential, SelectiveDisclosureOptions (mandatoryPointers array, optional selectivePointers, optional BBS+ privateKey/publicKey). Validates all pointers are JSON Pointer format (start with /). If privateKey provided, imports BBSCryptosuiteManager, calls createProof() with mandatoryPointers to sign credential with bbs-2023 cryptosuite. Returns credential with BBS+ proof and pointer metadata. Without privateKey, returns credential with metadata only (no cryptographic proof). See CredentialManager:914-975.
Edge Cases: At least one mandatory pointer required; error if empty array; Invalid JSON Pointer format (not starting with /) throws error with details; If no privateKey, credential returned as-is (no proof added); Mandatory pointers are included in every derived proof; cannot be hidden
Notes: Deps: BBSCryptosuiteManager for BBS+ proof creation, Multikey encoding/decoding for key format conversion

User Story: As a holder, I want to derive a selective disclosure proof from a BBS+ credential so that I can present only certain fields to a verifier without revealing everything.
Expected Behaviour: CredentialManager.deriveSelectiveProof() takes credential with BBS+ proof and array of fieldsToDisclose (JSON Pointers). Validates each field pointer format. Extracts all field paths from credential, determines hidden fields as those not in fieldsToDisclose. If proof.cryptosuite === 'bbs-2023', calls BBSCryptosuiteManager.deriveProof() which generates BBS+ selective disclosure proof revealing only disclosed fields. Returns credential with derived proof plus disclosedFields and hiddenFields arrays. Without BBS+ proof, returns credential and field lists (no cryptographic derivation). See CredentialManager:989-1036.
Edge Cases: Invalid JSON Pointer in fieldsToDisclose throws error; Hidden fields include those with null/undefined values and nested objects; Mandatory pointers (from base proof) are always disclosed regardless of fieldsToDisclose; If no BBS+ proof, derivation is skipped but field metadata returned
Notes: Deps: BBSCryptosuiteManager for BBS+ derivation, Field path extraction via recursive traversal

Feature Name: Access Credential Fields via JSON Pointer
User Story: As a developer, I want to retrieve a credential field value by JSON Pointer path so that I can programmatically access nested fields without manual object traversal.
Expected Behaviour: CredentialManager.getFieldByPointer() takes credential and JSON Pointer string (e.g., '/credentialSubject/name'), validates pointer starts with /, splits on '/', handles JSON Pointer escape sequences (~0 for ~, ~1 for /), traverses credential object depth-first, returns value at path or undefined if path not found. See CredentialManager:1067-1086.
Edge Cases: Missing intermediate object returns undefined (no error); Null/undefined in chain returns undefined; Escape sequences must be decoded: ~1 before ~0; Empty pointer '/' is invalid (throws error)
Notes: Deps: None; pure traversal logic

User Story: As a holder, I want to create a verifiable presentation containing one or more credentials so that I can present them as a bundle to a verifier.
Expected Behaviour: CredentialManager.createPresentation() takes array of VerifiableCredentials and holder DID, returns VerifiablePresentation with @context ['https://www.w3.org/2018/credentials/v1'], type ['VerifiablePresentation'], holder, and verifiableCredential array. Presentation is unsigned; caller must sign it via Issuer.issuePresentation(). See CredentialManager:404-414.
Edge Cases: Empty credentials array is allowed (no validation); Presentation does not include credential proofs; only credential references; Returned presentation has no proof; must be signed separately
Notes: Deps: None; pure data structure construction

Feature Name: Issue Presentations with Data Integrity Proofs
User Story: As a holder, I want to sign a verifiable presentation with my key so that I can prove I hold the credentials and present them to a verifier.
Expected Behaviour: Issuer.issuePresentation() takes unsigned presentation, IssueOptions (proofPurpose, optional documentLoader, challenge, domain), verifies verification method exists via document loader, validates secretKeyMultibase present (throws error if missing), infers key type from publicKeyMultibase (must be Ed25519), creates proof via DataIntegrityProofManager.createProof() with eddsa-rdfc-2022 cryptosuite, returns signed presentation. Challenge and domain are included in proof for challenge-response and domain binding. See Issuer:95-133.
Edge Cases: Only Ed25519 keys supported; other types throw error; Missing secretKeyMultibase throws error (required for signing); Challenge/domain are optional but included in proof if provided; Proof purpose typically 'authentication' for presentations
Notes: Deps: DIDManager for verification method resolution, DataIntegrityProofManager for proof creation, Ed25519 cryptography

User Story: As a verifier, I want to verify a presentation's proof and all contained credentials so that I can trust the holder's submission.
Expected Behaviour: Verifier.verifyPresentation() validates presentation structure (has @context, type, proof), loads all contexts via document loader, verifies each credential in verifiableCredential array recursively, then verifies presentation's proof using DataIntegrityProofManager.verifyProof(). Returns VerificationResult with verified boolean and errors array. All credentials must verify for presentation to verify. See Verifier:188-210.
Edge Cases: If any credential fails verification, entire presentation fails; Missing proof on presentation fails immediately; Empty verifiableCredential array is allowed; Presentation proof is verified independently of credential proofs
Notes: Deps: Verifier.verifyCredential() for credential verification, DataIntegrityProofManager for presentation proof verification

User Story: As a verifier, I want to verify a credential's proof and automatically check its revocation status during verification so that I can be confident the credential has not been revoked.
Expected Behaviour: Verifier.verifyCredential() checks if option checkStatus !== false (default true) and credential has credentialStatus and statusListResolver configured. If so, calls checkCredentialStatus() which fetches status list credential via statusListResolver callback, checks status via StatusListManager.checkStatus(). If revoked or suspended, returns verified: false with appropriate error. See Verifier:24-52, 58-85.
Edge Cases: If no statusListResolver configured, status check is skipped (not an error); If statusListResolver fails to fetch status list, returns verified: false; checkStatus: false in options disables status checking; Non-BitstringStatusListEntry status types are silently ignored
Notes: Deps: StatusListResolver callback for fetching status list credentials, StatusListManager for status bit checking

User Story: As a multi-party organization, I want to require M signatures from N authorized signers before a credential is valid so that I can enforce multi-party approval workflows.
Expected Behaviour: MultiSigManager.signCredentialMultiSig() validates policy (m <= n, required >= 1, no duplicate signers, timelock valid). Collects available signers from privateKeys and externalSigners maps, filters to policy-authorized signers, signs with first `required` signers using signWithPrivateKey() or signWithExternalSigner(). Returns credential with array of proofs (one per signer). If fewer authorized signers available than required, throws error. See MultiSigManager:131-178.
Edge Cases: Policy validation fails if required > total or required < 1; Duplicate verification methods in signerVerificationMethods throws error; Timelock constraint outside signing window throws error; Signing uses first `required` available signers in iteration order (non-deterministic if Map order matters)
Notes: Deps: MultiSigPolicy with threshold and signer list, Private keys or ExternalSigner instances for each participant, Signer implementations for signature generation

User Story: As a verifier, I want to verify that a multi-sig credential meets its threshold requirements so that I can trust the credential has sufficient authorization.
Expected Behaviour: MultiSigManager.verifyMultiSig() validates policy, extracts proofs array from credential, verifies each proof using verifyProof() which decodes proofValue, computes digest, extracts public key from verification method (did:key format only), verifies signature. Tracks unique signers (rejects duplicate proofs from same signer). Returns MultiSigVerificationResult with verified (validSignatures >= required), validSigners, invalidSigners, timelockValid, and errors array. See MultiSigManager:187-254.
Edge Cases: Duplicate proofs from same signer are ignored (counted once, error logged); Only did:key verification methods are supported for key extraction; Non-did:key verification methods return false for that proof; If any unauthorized signer present, marked as invalidSigner but verification continues; Timelock validation happens before proof verification
Notes: Deps: MultiSigPolicy for threshold definition, Signer implementations for verification, did:key resolution (no DID document fetch needed)

User Story: As an organization using escrow, I want to verify a credential meets both the threshold and escrow agent authorization requirements so that I can ensure controlled release.
Expected Behaviour: MultiSigManager.verifyEscrow() validates EscrowPolicy (which extends MultiSigPolicy with escrowAgent and releaseConditions), calls verifyMultiSig() to check threshold. If escrowSignatureRequired is true, additionally checks that escrowAgent is in validSigners. Returns MultiSigVerificationResult with verified set to false if escrow agent signature missing. See MultiSigManager:260-276.
Edge Cases: escrowSignatureRequired true but escrowAgent not in validSigners results in verified: false; escrowSignatureRequired false allows credential to be valid without escrow agent; Escrow agent signature counts toward threshold if included in signerVerificationMethods
Notes: Deps: MultiSigManager.verifyMultiSig() for base verification, EscrowPolicy definition

User Story: As a corporation, I want to verify that credentials are signed by representatives of all mandatory roles so that I can enforce role-based signing requirements.
Expected Behaviour: MultiSigManager.verifyCorporate() validates CorporatePolicy (extends MultiSigPolicy with roles Map and mandatoryRoles array), calls verifyMultiSig(). For each mandatory role, checks if at least one signer with that role has valid signature. If any mandatory role has no valid signature, sets verified: false and adds error. See MultiSigManager:282-307.
Edge Cases: No mandatory roles specified means role validation is skipped; Multiple signers can have same role; only one needs valid signature per role; A signer can satisfy the threshold even without all roles present; mandatoryRoles must be a non-empty subset of assigned roles
Notes: Deps: MultiSigManager.verifyMultiSig() for base verification, CorporatePolicy definition

User Story: As a workflow coordinator, I want to create a session for collecting signatures from multiple parties over time so that I can manage asynchronous signing without holding the credential in memory.
Expected Behaviour: MultiSigManager.createSession() creates MultiSigSession with unique id, stores in sessions Map, returns session object with id, policy, document (unsigned credential copy), empty contributions array, timestamps (createdAt, expiresAt defaults to policy.timelockEnd or 24h from now), status 'collecting'. addContribution() adds signature to session, validates signer is authorized and not duplicate, updates status to 'threshold_met' when contributions >= required. finalizeSession() checks threshold met, extracts proofs from contributions, returns signed credential with proof array. See MultiSigManager:318-419.
Edge Cases: Session expires if current time > expiresAt; contributions after expiry throw error; Duplicate contribution from same signer throws error; finalizeSession() requires threshold met; error if not enough contributions; Sessions stored in memory; lost on manager instance destruction
Notes: Deps: MultiSigPolicy for validation, SignatureContribution structure

User Story: As a participant in a multi-sig session, I want to create my signature contribution so that I can participate in async signature collection without the session holder having my key.
Expected Behaviour: MultiSigManager.createContribution() takes sessionId, privateKeyMultibase, verificationMethod, looks up session, validates verificationMethod is authorized by session policy, computes credential digest, signs via signWithPrivateKey(), returns SignatureContribution with proof, signerIndex (position in policy.signerVerificationMethods), and signedAt timestamp. MultiSigManager.createContributionWithExternalSigner() is similar but uses ExternalSigner.sign(). See MultiSigManager:438-489.
Edge Cases: Session lookup fails if sessionId not found; Verifier method not in policy throws error; Signature created at contribution time; clock skew not handled; Contribution contains all proof details including verificationMethod
Notes: Deps: MultiSigManager session tracking, Signer implementations

User Story: As a developer, I want to compute a consistent hash of a credential so that I can use it to chain credentials together for provenance verification.
Expected Behaviour: CredentialManager.computeCredentialHash() canonicalizes credential to RDF-N-Quads format using canonicalizeDocument(), computes SHA-256 hash of canonical bytes, returns hex-encoded hash string. Hash is deterministic; same credential always produces same hash regardless of JSON key order. See CredentialManager:832-838.
Edge Cases: Credential with proof is hashed including proof (caller should remove proof first if only data hash needed); Canonical form may differ between implementations; hashes not portable across systems; Empty or null fields are included in hash per canonicalization rules
Notes: Deps: RDF canonicalization (JSON-LD to N-Quads), SHA-256 hashing

User Story: As a Bitcoin-anchored asset issuer, I want to create a credential recording ownership transfer so that I have a verifiable record of who owned the asset at each point in time.
Expected Behaviour: CredentialManager.issueOwnershipCredential() creates unsigned VerifiableCredential with type 'OwnershipTransferred', captures assetDid, previousOwner (DID or address), newOwner (Bitcoin address), transactionId (Bitcoin tx hash), optional satoshi inscription number and transferReason. Adds to credentialSubject and chains to previous credential if chainOptions provided. See CredentialManager:720-750.
Edge Cases: previousOwner and newOwner can be DIDs, Bitcoin addresses, or other formats (no validation); transactionId must be provided (no default; caller error if missing); satoshi and transferReason are optional per details parameter
Notes: Deps: CredentialManager for chaining support, Optional: Bitcoin transaction validation (not performed by manager)

User Story: As a developer, I want to register verification methods (keys and their metadata) with the document loader so that they can be resolved during credential signing and verification.
Expected Behaviour: verificationMethodRegistry is a module-level Map storing verification method documents by ID. registerVerificationMethod() takes VM object with id property, stores in registry keyed by id. DocumentLoader.load() checks registry first before DID resolution when loading a did:* URL with fragment. Allows offline key registration without DID document fetch. See documentLoader.ts:76-79, 25-65.
Edge Cases: Registry is global per module instance; shared across all document loaders; Registering same ID twice overwrites previous entry; Registry entries not persisted; cleared when module unloads; Fragment resolution from registry bypasses DID document parsing
Notes: Deps: Module-level registry Map

User Story: As a system, I want contexts to be available offline so that credential canonicalization and verification work without network access to W3C or Originals context URLs.
Expected Behaviour: DocumentLoader loads preloaded contexts from PRELOADED_CONTEXTS object instead of fetching from network. Contexts include W3C credentials v1, v2, security data-integrity, and Originals custom context. If context not in preloaded set, throws error. Preloading ensures all term definitions are available during safe-mode RDF canonicalization. See documentLoader.ts:9, 18-22.
Edge Cases: Unknown context URLs throw 'Document not found' error; Preloaded contexts are bundled at build time; stale if context specs change; No network fallback if preloaded context is missing
Notes: Deps: PRELOADED_CONTEXTS mapping (from serialization utils)

User Story: As a credential system, I want to use EdDSA (Ed25519) signatures with Data Integrity proofs so that I can create tamper-evident, verifiable credentials compatible with W3C standards.
Expected Behaviour: EdDSACryptosuiteManager.createProof() creates proof config with cryptosuite 'eddsa-rdfc-2022', canonicalizes document and proof config separately using JSON-LD canonicalization, hashes both to create digest (proofConfigHash || documentHash), signs digest with Ed25519 private key, returns proof with proofValue as multibase base58btc (z-prefixed). EdDSACryptosuiteManager.verifyProof() reconstructs digest identically and verifies signature against public key. See cryptosuites/eddsa.ts:18-60.
Edge Cases: Private key can be 32 or 64 bytes; 64-byte keys are truncated to last 32 bytes; proofValue must start with 'z' prefix for base58btc; other formats rejected; Proof context must match document context for canonicalization equivalence; Challenge and domain fields are optional but included in proof if provided
Notes: Deps: Ed25519 signing/verification (@noble/ed25519), JSON-LD canonicalization (jsonld library or polyfill), SHA-256 hashing

Feature Name: BBS+ Proof Creation and Selective Disclosure
User Story: As an issuer, I want to create BBS+ proofs that enable selective disclosure so that holders can reveal only certain credential fields without showing everything to a verifier.
Expected Behaviour: BBSCryptosuiteManager.createProof() converts credential fields to BBS+ messages using credentialToMessages(), signs all messages with private key using BbsSimple.sign(), serializes base proof value with signature, header, public key, HMAC key, and mandatory pointers. createProof() returns proof with cryptosuite 'bbs-2023'. deriveProof() reconstructs original messages, filters by selectivePointers, calls BbsSimple.createProof() to generate proof of selective disclosure, returns credential with only disclosed fields and derived proof. See cryptosuites/bbsCryptosuite.ts:83-157, 212-293.
Edge Cases: Mandatory pointers are always disclosed in derived proofs; cannot be hidden; Public key can be derived from private key if not explicitly provided; Derived proof includes only disclosed fields in returned document; Field extraction is depth-first recursive; arrays treated as objects
Notes: Deps: BbsSimple for low-level BBS+ signing/proof generation, BBSCryptosuiteUtils for serialization/parsing, Multikey for key format handling, JSON-LD canonicalization

User Story: As a developer, I want to create an Issuer instance from a verification method so that I can sign credentials and presentations using Data Integrity proofs.
Expected Behaviour: Issuer constructor takes DIDManager and VerificationMethodLike (id, controller, publicKeyMultibase, secretKeyMultibase, type). issueCredential() takes unsigned credential and IssueOptions (proofPurpose, optional documentLoader, challenge, domain). Ensures @context includes secure context for proof terms, infers key type from publicKeyMultibase via multikey.decodePublicKey(), validates Ed25519 only, calls DataIntegrityProofManager.createProof() with eddsa-rdfc-2022. Returns credential with proof. See Issuer:49-133.
Edge Cases: Key type inference fails if multikey decode fails; defaults to Ed25519; Non-Ed25519 keys throw error (only eddsa-rdfc-2022 supported); Missing secretKeyMultibase throws error; @context is preserved if present; defaults to W3C v2 + Originals if missing
Notes: Deps: DIDManager for document loading, Multikey for key type inference, DataIntegrityProofManager for proof creation

User Story: As a developer, I want to create a new digital asset with a cryptographic event log at the peer layer, so that I can establish initial provenance and ownership.
Expected Behaviour: OriginalsCel.create(name, resources) generates a did:peer identifier, creates a 'create' event with asset metadata, signs it, and returns an EventLog with the event appended. Throws error if layer is not 'peer' (per OriginalsCel.ts:205-216).
Edge Cases: Empty or non-string asset name throws error; Resources array is required and validated; Non-peer layer layers cannot create directly - must create at peer first; Signer function must be valid function returning DataIntegrityProof with required fields
Notes: Deps: PeerCelManager.create(), createEventLog algorithm, @aviarytech/did-peer library, @noble/ed25519 for key generation

User Story: As an asset owner, I want to update an asset's metadata while maintaining cryptographic proof of the change, so that I can track modifications over time.
Expected Behaviour: OriginalsCel.update(log, data) appends an 'update' event to the log with cryptographic chaining to the previous event via previousEvent hash field. New timestamp is added to update data. Returns new EventLog without mutating input (per updateEventLog.ts:50-99 and OriginalsCel.ts:239-265).
Edge Cases: Empty event log throws error; Deactivated logs cannot be updated; Update data automatically gets updatedAt timestamp; PeerCelManager applies updates directly; WebVH/BTCO logs use peer manager's update algorithm; Non-object data is wrapped in { value: data }
Notes: Deps: updateEventLog algorithm, computeDigestMultibase() for hash chain, Layer manager's update method

User Story: As a verifier, I want to verify the complete integrity of an event log including all proofs and hash chain linkage, so that I can confirm asset provenance.
Expected Behaviour: OriginalsCel.verify(log, options?) validates: (1) each event has proof array, (2) proof structures are valid, (3) hash chain previousEvent references are correct. Returns VerificationResult with verified flag, per-event details, and error list (per verifyEventLog.ts:20-150+).
Edge Cases: First event must not have previousEvent field; Subsequent events must have previousEvent matching previous event's digest; Custom verifier can be provided via options to override default proof validation; Default verifier checks proof structure but not cryptographic validity (requires verifier function); Invalid hash chain breaks verification chain at that point
Notes: Deps: verifyEventLog algorithm, computeDigestMultibase() for hash verification, Optional custom verifier function from options

User Story: As an asset publisher, I want to migrate my asset from peer layer to did:webvh layer with HTTP witness attestations, so that my asset becomes discoverable and publicly verifiable.
Expected Behaviour: OriginalsCel.migrate(log, 'webvh', options?) adds an 'update' event with WebVHMigrationData containing targetDid, sourceDid, domain, migratedAt. Generates did:webvh DID from domain. Optionally adds witness proofs from configured services. Returns updated EventLog (per WebVHCelManager.ts:127-203).
Edge Cases: Can only migrate from 'peer' to 'webvh'; Cannot migrate webvh to webvh (already at target); Cannot migrate from btco (final layer); Domain is required - from options or config; Deactivated logs cannot be migrated; Migration event replaces DID with did:webvh format; Witnesses are optional but commonly added at this layer
Notes: Deps: WebVHCelManager.migrate(), updateEventLog algorithm, witnessEvent algorithm (if witnesses configured), WitnessService implementations (optional)

User Story: As an asset owner seeking maximum immutability, I want to migrate my asset to did:btco layer anchored on Bitcoin, so that provenance is permanently recorded on blockchain.
Expected Behaviour: OriginalsCel.migrate(log, 'btco') creates update event with BtcoMigrationData, mandatorily inscribes to Bitcoin via BitcoinWitness, extracts txid/inscriptionId, generates did:btco DID, updates migration data with Bitcoin details. Returns EventLog with witness proof (per BtcoCelManager.ts:131-254).
Edge Cases: Can only migrate from 'webvh' to 'btco'; Cannot migrate from 'peer' directly to 'btco' (must go through webvh); Bitcoin witness is REQUIRED (not optional like WebVH); BTCO is final layer - cannot migrate further; Deactivated logs cannot be migrated; BitcoinManager must be configured with ordinals provider; Optional feeRate can be specified for inscription transaction; Did:btco DID derived from inscriptionId if available, otherwise fallback to txid
Notes: Deps: BtcoCelManager.migrate(), BitcoinManager for ordinals inscription, BitcoinWitness service (mandatory), updateEventLog algorithm, witnessEvent algorithm

User Story: As a developer, I want to derive the current state of an asset from its event log without replaying every update, so that I can quickly access metadata.
Expected Behaviour: OriginalsCel.getCurrentState(log) replays all events in order: starts with create event state, applies each update event, marks deactivated if deactivate event present. Returns AssetState with current did, name, layer, resources, timestamps, and metadata. Throws error if log is empty or first event is not 'create' (per PeerCelManager.ts:294-370).
Edge Cases: Empty log throws error; First event must be 'create' type; Migration events update did and layer fields; Update events merge data into metadata dict for unknown fields; Deactivate event sets deactivated flag and stores reason in metadata.deactivationReason; Resources array is copied (not referenced) from create event; Layer detection uses latest migration event's layer value
Notes: Deps: Layer manager's getCurrentState() method (delegates by layer), Event iteration and field extraction

User Story: As an asset owner, I want to permanently seal an asset log by marking it deactivated, so that no further changes can be made.
Expected Behaviour: deactivateEventLog(log, reason, options) validates log is not already deactivated, appends a 'deactivate' event with reason and deactivatedAt timestamp, links to previous event via hash chain, returns new EventLog with deactivation event. No further events can be added to deactivated logs (per deactivateEventLog.ts:51-99+).
Edge Cases: Already deactivated log throws error; Empty log throws error; Reason parameter is required; deactivatedAt timestamp is auto-generated; Deactivation creates final hash chain link
Notes: Deps: deactivateEventLog algorithm, computeDigestMultibase() for hash chain, Signer function

User Story: As a developer, I want to compute a CEL-compliant digest for external content, so that I can create external references.
Expected Behaviour: computeDigestMultibase(content: Uint8Array) computes SHA-256 hash and encodes as Multibase base64url-nopad (prefix 'u'). Returns digest string suitable for ExternalReference.digestMultibase (per hash.ts:27-33).
Edge Cases: Content must be Uint8Array; Empty content produces valid digest; Same content always produces same digest; Digest encoding uses 'u' prefix per CEL spec
Notes: Deps: @noble/hashes/sha2.js for SHA-256, multibase.encode() for base64url encoding

User Story: As a verifier, I want to verify that external content matches a digest, so that I can confirm content integrity.
Expected Behaviour: verifyDigestMultibase(content, digest) computes digest of content and compares with provided digest string. Returns true if match, false if mismatch or error (per hash.ts:49-60).
Edge Cases: Invalid digest format returns false (not throw); Content mismatch returns false; Any exception during verification returns false; Empty content can be verified
Notes: Deps: computeDigestMultibase(), @noble/hashes/sha2.js

User Story: As a developer, I want to create a reference to external content with integrity hash, so that I can link assets to resources.
Expected Behaviour: createExternalReference(content, mediaType, urls?) computes digestMultibase from content, returns ExternalReference with digestMultibase, mediaType, and optional url array (per ExternalReferenceManager.ts:36-56).
Edge Cases: URLs array is optional; Empty URLs array is not included in result; mediaType is required; Content must be Uint8Array
Notes: Deps: computeDigestMultibase()

User Story: As a verifier, I want to confirm that content matches a reference, so that I can validate external resources.
Expected Behaviour: verifyExternalReference(ref, content) computes digest of content and compares with ref.digestMultibase. Returns true if match, false otherwise (per ExternalReferenceManager.ts:81-87).
Edge Cases: Content mismatch returns false; Invalid digest format returns false; Any error during verification returns false
Notes: Deps: verifyDigestMultibase()

User Story: As a publisher using WebVH, I want to add HTTP-based third-party witness attestations to my events, so that additional parties can verify provenance.
Expected Behaviour: HttpWitness.witness(digestMultibase) POSTs digest to configured endpoint, receives WitnessProof response with witnessedAt timestamp and proof fields. Throws HttpWitnessError on network error or non-OK response (per HttpWitness.ts:104-150+).
Edge Cases: Network timeout after 30 seconds (configurable); Non-OK HTTP status throws error with status code; Invalid JSON response throws error; Missing witnessedAt in response throws error; Custom fetch implementation can be provided for testing
Notes: Deps: WitnessService interface, fetch API (global or custom), Remote HTTP witness endpoint

User Story: As an asset owner, I want to anchor my event on Bitcoin via ordinals inscription, so that provenance is permanently timestamped on blockchain.
Expected Behaviour: BitcoinWitness.witness(digestMultibase) creates witness data, inscribes via BitcoinManager, extracts txid/inscriptionId/satoshi, returns BitcoinWitnessProof with Bitcoin details and witnessedAt timestamp. Throws BitcoinWitnessError if inscription fails (per BitcoinWitness.ts:99-160+).
Edge Cases: Invalid multibase prefix throws error; BitcoinManager inscription fails throws error; No inscriptionId in response uses txid fallback; Fee rate is optional - BitcoinManager estimates if not provided; Block height may be undefined if not yet confirmed
Notes: Deps: BitcoinManager for ordinals provider, WitnessService interface, Bitcoin network access

User Story: As a publisher, I want to add witness proofs from services to events without replacing existing proofs, so that multiple attestations accumulate.
Expected Behaviour: witnessEvent(event, witness) computes event digest, calls witness.witness(digest), validates returned WitnessProof, appends to event.proof array. Returns new LogEntry with witness proof added (immutable operation) (per witnessEvent.ts:52-87).
Edge Cases: Event must have at least one existing proof; Witness service failure throws error; Multiple witnesses can be called sequentially; Proof array grows with each witness; Event itself is not mutated (new object returned)
Notes: Deps: WitnessService interface, computeDigestMultibase() for event digest, Specific witness implementation (HttpWitness, BitcoinWitness, etc)

User Story: As a developer, I want to export an event log as JSON for storage or transmission, so that I can persist provenance data.
Expected Behaviour: serializeEventLogJson(log) recursively sorts all object keys for deterministic output, returns formatted JSON string with 2-space indentation (per json.ts:51-58).
Edge Cases: Null or undefined log throws error; Keys are sorted recursively at all nesting levels; Array elements are not reordered (only object keys sorted); Formatting uses 2-space indentation for readability
Notes: Deps: JSON.stringify with custom replacer

User Story: As a developer, I want to load an event log from JSON, so that I can work with persisted provenance data.
Expected Behaviour: parseEventLogJson(json) validates JSON syntax, reconstructs EventLog structure, validates each LogEntry and Proof, throws error if validation fails (per json.ts:173-209).
Edge Cases: Invalid JSON syntax throws error; Missing required fields throws error; Invalid proof structure throws error; Invalid entry type throws error; previousEvent must be string if present; Empty string input throws error
Notes: Deps: JSON.parse, Validation functions

User Story: As a developer optimizing for bandwidth, I want to export an event log as compact CBOR binary, so that I can reduce transmission size.
Expected Behaviour: serializeEventLogCbor(log) encodes EventLog to CBOR binary format using encode() utility, returns Uint8Array. CBOR is typically 30-50% smaller than JSON (per cbor.ts:125-131).
Edge Cases: Null or undefined log throws error; CBOR encoding is deterministic; Binary output is not human-readable; All data types are preserved in CBOR
Notes: Deps: cbor encode utility

User Story: As a developer, I want to load a CBOR-encoded event log, so that I can work with compact binary provenance data.
Expected Behaviour: parseEventLogCbor(cbor) validates Uint8Array input, decodes CBOR, validates EventLog structure and all LogEntry/Proof fields, throws error if validation fails (per cbor.ts:149-189).
Edge Cases: Non-Uint8Array input throws error; Empty CBOR data throws error; Invalid CBOR encoding throws error; Missing required fields throws error; All validation rules same as JSON parsing
Notes: Deps: cbor decode utility

User Story: As a developer, I want direct access to peer layer operations without the unified API, so that I can have fine-grained control over did:peer assets.
Expected Behaviour: PeerCelManager provides create(), update(), getCurrentState() methods for Layer 0 assets. Generates did:peer identities, manages asset state replay, enforces peer-layer constraints (per PeerCelManager.ts:72-372).
Edge Cases: Cannot create at non-peer layer; DID generation uses did:peer numalgo 4 format; Creator DID equals asset DID at peer layer; No witness support at this layer; Direct access bypasses OriginalsCel routing
Notes: Deps: @aviarytech/did-peer for did:peer generation, @noble/ed25519 for key generation, createEventLog, updateEventLog algorithms

User Story: As a publisher, I want direct access to webvh layer operations, so that I can manage domain-based assets with witness services.
Expected Behaviour: WebVHCelManager provides migrate() and getCurrentState() methods for Layer 1 assets. Accepts domain, optional witness services, generates did:webvh DIDs, optionally adds witness proofs (per WebVHCelManager.ts:67-361).
Edge Cases: Cannot create at webvh layer (only migrate); Domain validation enforces standard domain format; Witnesses are optional (default empty array); did:webvh DID derives from source DID and domain; Migration event stores domain for resolution
Notes: Deps: WitnessService implementations, updateEventLog, witnessEvent algorithms

User Story: As an asset owner seeking immutability, I want direct access to btco layer operations, so that I can anchor assets on Bitcoin.
Expected Behaviour: BtcoCelManager provides migrate() and getCurrentState() methods for Layer 2 assets. Requires BitcoinManager, mandatorily adds Bitcoin witness via ordinals, generates did:btco DIDs, stores txid/inscriptionId in migration event (per BtcoCelManager.ts:73-408).
Edge Cases: Cannot create at btco layer (only migrate from webvh); BitcoinManager must be configured with ordinals provider; Bitcoin witness is REQUIRED (not optional); Fee rate is optional - BitcoinManager estimates if not provided; did:btco derived from inscriptionId or txid; Cannot migrate further from btco (final layer)
Notes: Deps: BitcoinManager for ordinals provider, BitcoinWitness (mandatory), updateEventLog, witnessEvent algorithms

User Story: As a developer, I want a single unified API for all layer operations, so that I can work with assets across peer/webvh/btco layers transparently.
Expected Behaviour: OriginalsCel class accepts layer type and signer, internally routes create/update/migrate/verify/getCurrentState calls to appropriate layer manager. Determines current layer from event log and adapts operations accordingly (per OriginalsCel.ts:95-460).
Edge Cases: Configuration is layer-specific and optional per layer; Empty log defaults to 'peer' layer; Layer detection iterates events to find current layer; Domain extraction needed for webvh operations; WebVH manager created fresh each call (supports dynamic domains); Invalid layer name throws error
Notes: Deps: PeerCelManager, WebVHCelManager, BtcoCelManager, All layer-specific algorithms, verifyEventLog for verification

User Story: As a developer, I want to derive complete asset state by replaying events, so that I can query current properties without external sources.
Expected Behaviour: AssetState type captures derived state with: did, name, layer, resources, creator, createdAt, updatedAt, deactivated flag, metadata dict. getCurrentState() replays all events, applies updates in order, stores unknown fields in metadata (per types.ts:126-146).
Edge Cases: Resources array is copied from create event; Migration events update did and layer; Unknown fields from update events stored in metadata; Deactivate event sets flag and stores reason in metadata.deactivationReason; updatedAt tracks latest modification timestamp; metadata dict accumulates non-standard fields
Notes: Deps: Event log iteration, Layer manager getCurrentState()

User Story: As a witness provider, I want to implement a pluggable witness interface, so that I can provide custom attestation services.
Expected Behaviour: WitnessService interface defines single async witness(digestMultibase: string) method returning WitnessProof. Implementations (HttpWitness, BitcoinWitness, custom) follow this contract. Service records digest at point in time and returns proof with witnessedAt timestamp (per WitnessService.ts:37-51).
Edge Cases: witness() method must not mutate digest parameter; Must return WitnessProof (has witnessedAt field); witnessedAt should be ISO 8601 timestamp; Service failures throw appropriate error subclass; Multiple witnesses can be chained in sequence
Notes: Deps: WitnessProof type from types.ts

User Story: As a developer, I want to understand the proof structure, so that I can create and validate proofs correctly.
Expected Behaviour: DataIntegrityProof interface defines: type='DataIntegrityProof', cryptosuite (e.g., 'eddsa-jcs-2022'), created (ISO timestamp), verificationMethod (DID URL), proofPurpose (e.g., 'assertionMethod'), proofValue (multibase-encoded signature). WitnessProof extends with witnessedAt field (per types.ts:1-52).
Edge Cases: type must always be 'DataIntegrityProof'; cryptosuite identifies algorithm (eddsa-jcs-2022, bitcoin-ordinals-2024, etc); verificationMethod is DID URL locating public key; proofValue is multibase-encoded (typically 'z' or 'u' prefix); WitnessProof is DataIntegrityProof + witnessedAt
Notes: Deps: Proof types from types.ts

Feature Name: Create CEL Asset with Initial Event
User Story: As a developer, I want to create a new CEL (Cryptographic Event Log) asset with an initial event, so that I can register digital assets with provenance tracking.
Expected Behaviour: createCommand (create.ts) validates required --name and --file flags, reads file content into Uint8Array, determines MIME type from extension (create.ts:48-76), generates or loads Ed25519 key pair, creates a signer function, creates PeerCelManager, calls manager.create(), and serializes output to JSON (default) or CBOR format. When key is generated, writes to file with mode 0600 and logs details to stderr. Output goes to file (--output) or stdout (create.ts:282-305).
Edge Cases: Missing --name or --file parameters returns error; Non-existent file returns file not found error; Invalid format flag (not json/cbor) returns validation error; Key file parse errors handled for both JSON and raw multibase formats; Ed25519 key type validation ensures only Ed25519 keys are accepted (create.ts:124-126); CBOR output to stdout is base64-encoded since binary cannot be streamed (create.ts:300-301)
Notes: Deps: @noble/ed25519 - async key generation and signing, fs - file I/O operations, path - MIME type detection from file extension, PeerCelManager - layer/PeerCelManager, serializeEventLogJson, serializeEventLogCbor - serialization functions, multikey - Ed25519 key encoding/decoding

User Story: As an asset owner or auditor, I want to verify that a CEL event log is valid and all proofs are intact, so that I can confirm the asset's integrity and history.
Expected Behaviour: verifyCommand (verify.ts) validates required --log flag, loads event log from JSON or CBOR file (verify.ts:48-63), calls verifyEventLog() algorithm, and outputs formatted report showing overall verification status (✅ PASS or ❌ FAIL), summary with per-event proof and chain validation counts, event-by-event breakdown with proof details and errors, witness attestations count, and truncated proof values (verify.ts:104-186). Exit code 0 on success, 1 on verification failure (index.ts:250-252).
Edge Cases: File not found returns error; Empty event log throws error (verifyEventLog must receive non-empty log); Invalid JSON/CBOR format returns parse error; Witness proofs detected via 'witnessedAt' field and displayed separately from controller proofs (verify.ts:40-42, 94-96); Proof value truncated to 60 chars max for display (verify.ts:88-90); Missing proof data on events handled gracefully
Notes: Deps: fs - file I/O, path - extension detection, verifyEventLog - algorithms/verifyEventLog, parseEventLogJson, parseEventLogCbor - serialization parsers

Feature Name: Inspect CEL Log in Human-Readable Format
User Story: As a developer or auditor, I want to inspect a CEL log in human-readable format showing current state, layer history, witness attestations, and event timeline, so that I can understand the asset's provenance at a glance.
Expected Behaviour: inspectCommand (inspect.ts) validates required --log flag, loads event log (inspect.ts:71-86), derives current state from events (inspect.ts:173-243), outputs formatted inspection to stdout showing: Current State section (status, name, layer, DID, creator, timestamps, resources with hashes and URLs, metadata), Layer History section (migration events showing layer badges and DIDs), Witness Attestations section (all witness proofs with timestamp and cryptosuite), and Event Timeline (all events with badges, timestamps, changes, proof counts, and hash chain links). State is derived by replaying events starting from create event (inspect.ts:199-240).
Edge Cases: Empty event log returns error; First event must be create type or error thrown; Migration events detected via presence of sourceDid/targetDid or layer field in update events (inspect.ts:58-65); Missing creator field in create event displays as undefined; Layer history section only shown if multiple layer entries detected; Witness attestations section only shown if witness proofs exist; Event data fields not in known set stored in metadata object (inspect.ts:223-228); Very long strings truncated (max 50 chars) for readability (inspect.ts:133-136); DIDs truncated showing first 25 + last 15 chars (inspect.ts:142-144); Relative time formatting handles dates before epoch and shows fallback to full timestamp (inspect.ts:111-128)
Notes: Deps: fs - file I/O, path - extension detection, parseEventLogJson, parseEventLogCbor - serialization parsers, AssetState, ExternalReference, WitnessProof types

User Story: As an asset owner, I want to migrate a CEL asset from one layer to another (peer→webvh or webvh→btco), so that I can move my asset up the trust hierarchy.
Expected Behaviour: migrateCommand (migrate.ts) validates required --log and --to flags, loads event log, detects current layer by scanning events for layer field (migrate.ts:82-101), validates migration path (migrate.ts:134-150), requires --domain for webvh target or --wallet for btco target, loads wallet key or generates temporary key (migrate.ts:342-371), creates signer, instantiates appropriate layer manager (WebVHCelManager or BtcoCelManager with mock Bitcoin manager for CLI), calls manager.migrate(), extracts target DID from migration event, serializes to JSON/CBOR, writes to file or stdout (base64 for CBOR), and outputs success message with layer transition to stderr (migrate.ts:464-470).
Edge Cases: Cannot migrate from btco layer (final layer); Cannot migrate directly peer→btco, must go peer→webvh→btco (migrate.ts:139-140); Cannot migrate to same layer already on (migrate.ts:143-149); Empty event log causes detection error; Missing domain for webvh target returns error; Missing wallet for btco target returns error; Temporary key generated for webvh if no wallet provided, written to file with mode 0600 (migrate.ts:364-370); Mock Bitcoin manager returns mock txid/inscriptionId with cli_mock_ prefix (migrate.ts:234-245); btco migration uses mock Bitcoin manager noting limitation to stderr (migrate.ts:238); Output format validated (json or cbor only)
Notes: Deps: fs - file I/O, path - extension detection, parseEventLogJson, parseEventLogCbor - load input, serializeEventLogJson, serializeEventLogCbor - serialize output, WebVHCelManager - webvh layer operations, BtcoCelManager - btco layer operations, @noble/ed25519 - temporary key generation, multikey - key encoding, @noble/hashes/sha2.js - hash for chain linking

User Story: As an asset owner, I want to transfer ownership of a CEL asset to a new owner, so that I can document asset provenance through ownership changes.
