Expected Behaviour: transferCommand (transfer.ts) validates required --log, --to, and --wallet flags, loads event log, extracts current DID from events, loads wallet private key (identical to migrate.ts key loading), creates signer, constructs transfer data object with type='transfer', previousOwner, newOwner, and transferredAt timestamp, signs transfer data, computes hash of last event for chain linking, appends update event to log with transfer proof and previousEvent hash, serializes output to JSON/CBOR, writes to file or stdout (base64 for CBOR), and logs transfer confirmation to stderr (transfer.ts:296-297).
Edge Cases: Missing log, to, or wallet flags returns error; File not found returns error; Empty event log fails DID extraction; Invalid wallet file format returns error; Non-Ed25519 wallet key returns type mismatch error; CBOR output to stdout is base64-encoded (transfer.ts:289-290); Output format validation (json or cbor only); previousEvent hash computed from last event using sha256 (transfer.ts:238-240)
Notes: Deps: fs - file I/O, path - extension detection, parseEventLogJson, parseEventLogCbor - load input, serializeEventLogJson, serializeEventLogCbor - serialize output, @noble/ed25519 - signing, @noble/hashes/sha2.js - hash chain linking, multikey - key encoding/decoding

Feature Name: Publish Asset to Web (Migrate to WebVH)
User Story: As an asset owner, I want to publish my CEL asset to the web by migrating it to the WebVH layer, so that I can make it discoverable and verifiable via web infrastructure.
Expected Behaviour: publishCommand (publish.ts) is a convenience wrapper that validates required --log and --domain flags, delegates to migrateCommand() with --to='webvh', --domain=<user domain>, and passes through --output and --format flags. Returns success message 'Published asset to web (did:webvh)' on success (publish.ts:75-76).
Edge Cases: Missing log flag returns error; Missing domain flag returns error; All errors from underlying migrateCommand are propagated; Output format not validated at publish level (delegated to migrate)
Notes: Deps: migrateCommand - core migration logic from migrate.ts

Feature Name: Inscribe Asset on Bitcoin (Migrate to BTCO)
User Story: As an asset owner, I want to inscribe my CEL asset on the Bitcoin blockchain by migrating it to the BTCO layer, so that I can anchor my asset to the immutable Bitcoin ledger.
Expected Behaviour: inscribeCommand (inscribe.ts) is a convenience wrapper that validates required --log and --wallet flags, delegates to migrateCommand() with --to='btco', --wallet=<user wallet key>, and passes through --output and --format flags. Returns success message 'Inscribed asset on Bitcoin (did:btco)' on success (inscribe.ts:76).
Edge Cases: Missing log flag returns error; Missing wallet flag returns error; All errors from underlying migrateCommand are propagated; Mock Bitcoin manager limitations inherited from migrateCommand
Notes: Deps: migrateCommand - core migration logic from migrate.ts

User Story: As a developer, I want to resolve a DID (peer, webvh, or btco) to its DID Document, so that I can retrieve the asset's public information and verification methods.
Expected Behaviour: resolveCommand (resolve.ts) validates DID format (must start with 'did:'), accepts optional --network flag (mainnet/regtest/signet, defaults to mainnet), detects network from DID if not provided (did:btco:reg:* → regtest, did:btco:sig:* → signet), creates OriginalsSDK instance, calls sdk.did.resolveDID(), outputs JSON to file (--output) or stdout, returns success with resolved didDocument (resolve.ts:79-84).
Edge Cases: Missing DID returns error; DID not starting with 'did:' returns format error; Invalid network flag (not mainnet/regtest/signet) returns error; Network auto-detection from DID prefix handled for regtest/signet (resolve.ts:73-76); DID resolution failure returns error message; null didDocument return treated as resolution failure; Output file write errors propagated
Notes: Deps: OriginalsSDK - core/OriginalsSDK for DID resolution, fs - file I/O for output, BitcoinNetworkName type from types/network

User Story: As a CLI user, I want to access help documentation and version information, so that I can understand available commands and verify the tool version.
Expected Behaviour: main() function (index.ts) accepts --version/-v flags and displays 'originals-cel v1.5.0' (index.ts:312-315). Accepts --help/-h without command to display main HELP_TEXT (index.ts:318-324). Each command accepts --help/-h to display command-specific help. Help text defined as constants HELP_TEXT (general), CREATE_HELP, VERIFY_HELP, INSPECT_HELP, MIGRATE_HELP (index.ts:17-133). showCommandHelp() dispatches to appropriate help text (index.ts:188-205).
Edge Cases: Version flag with other flags returns version only (index.ts:312-315); Help flag without command shows general help; Help flag with invalid command returns 'Unknown command' error; Invalid command without help flag returns error with usage hint
Notes: Deps: process.argv for CLI args access, console.log for output to stdout

User Story: As a CLI tool, I want to parse command-line arguments consistently, so that users can provide input via standardized flags.
Expected Behaviour: parseArgs() function (index.ts:138-168) accepts string array of CLI args, returns { command, flags } object. Parses double-dash flags (--key value) with optional values, single-dash short flags (-h), and positional commands. Flags with following arg that doesn't start with dash treated as value (index.ts:153-156). Flags with no following arg or next arg starts with dash treated as boolean true (index.ts:157-158). First non-flag arg becomes command (index.ts:162-164).
Edge Cases: Flag at end of args with no value → boolean true; Single dash followed by multiple chars treated as flag name (-abc → flags.abc); Double dash with no value → boolean true; Value arg starting with dash not consumed (next arg required); Multiple same flag → last value wins (object key overwrite); Empty args array returns { command: null, flags: {} }
Notes: Deps: process.argv for runtime arg access

User Story: As a developer, I want to choose between JSON and CBOR output formats for CEL event logs, so that I can use the format appropriate for my use case.
Expected Behaviour: create, migrate, transfer, and inscribe commands all accept optional --format flag (default 'json'). Format validated to be 'json' or 'cbor' (case-insensitive, create.ts:191-197). JSON output serialized via serializeEventLogJson() and written as UTF-8 text. CBOR output serialized via serializeEventLogCbor() and written as binary. When binary format output to stdout without file, base64-encoded for console safety (create.ts:300-301, migrate.ts:455-459, transfer.ts:289-290). File output writes binary directly without base64 encoding (create.ts:286, migrate.ts:443).
Edge Cases: Invalid format returns validation error; CBOR to stdout → base64 encoded; CBOR to file → binary (Uint8Array) written directly; JSON to stdout → text written as string; JSON to file → UTF-8 text file; Format case-insensitive (JSON, json, Json all valid)
Notes: Deps: serializeEventLogJson - serialization/json, serializeEventLogCbor - serialization/cbor, fs - file write operations

Feature Name: MIME Type Detection for Asset Content
User Story: As a developer creating CEL assets, I want the CLI to automatically detect the MIME type of my content file, so that I don't need to specify it manually.
Expected Behaviour: create command calls getMimeType() (create.ts:73-76) to detect MIME type from file extension. Lookup table MIME_TYPES (create.ts:48-68) maps extensions to types (.png→image/png, .jpg→image/jpeg, .pdf→application/pdf, etc.). Unknown extensions default to 'application/octet-stream' (create.ts:75). MIME type used to create external reference via createExternalReference() (create.ts:249).
Edge Cases: Unknown file extension → 'application/octet-stream'; File with no extension → 'application/octet-stream'; Uppercase extension → lowercased for lookup (create.ts:74); Extension includes dot prefix (.png not png); Common media types covered (image, audio, video, document, application)
Notes: Deps: path.extname - extract file extension, MIME_TYPES lookup table - hardcoded in create.ts

User Story: As a user creating CEL assets, I want the CLI to generate Ed25519 key pairs automatically and securely store private keys, so that I can start using the tool without pre-existing keys.
Expected Behaviour: create command calls generateKeyPair() (create.ts:81-90) when no --key provided. Uses @noble/ed25519 library to generate random private key bytes and derive public key. Encodes both using multikey.encodePrivateKey() and multikey.encodePublicKey() with 'Ed25519' type. Generated private key written to file with name derived from asset name or output file, mode 0600, containing JSON { privateKey, publicKey } (create.ts:325-330). Public key and path logged to stderr with security warning (create.ts:328-330).
Edge Cases: Key generation uses @noble/ed25519.utils.randomPrivateKey(); Public key derived from private via getPublicKeyAsync(); Key file naming sanitizes asset name (replaces non-alphanumeric with _ per create.ts:325); File mode 0600 set via fs.writeFileSync options (create.ts:327); Private key never written to stdout (stderr only); Key files overwrite silently if path exists; Generated key returned in result.privateKey and result.publicKey
Notes: Deps: @noble/ed25519 - cryptographic key generation, multikey - key encoding to multibase format, fs - key file persistence

User Story: As a CLI user, I want to control where the output of CLI commands is written, so that I can integrate them into workflows.
Expected Behaviour: All output commands (create, migrate, transfer, verify, inspect, resolve) accept optional --output flag. If provided, output written to file at that path (create.ts:282-295, migrate.ts:440-462, transfer.ts:274-294). If not provided, output written to stdout (create.ts:296-304, migrate.ts:453-461, transfer.ts:287-293). Binary CBOR output to stdout is base64-encoded for safety. Text JSON output to stdout written directly. Errors and status messages written to stderr (console.error) (create.ts:328-330, migrate.ts:468-470).
Edge Cases: Output file not writable returns error; Output path is directory (not file) returns write error; Output file overwritten if exists; File write errors propagated up; CBOR binary requires base64 encoding for stdout (create.ts:300); JSON text safe for stdout; No --output flag means stdout used
Notes: Deps: fs.writeFileSync - file writing, process.stdout.write - stdout output

User Story: As a CLI user, I want clear error messages when something goes wrong, so that I can debug and fix issues.
Expected Behaviour: All commands return result object { success, message, ... }. On error, success=false and message contains 'Error: ' prefix describing issue (index.ts:180-183). error() function prints to stderr and exits with code 1 or custom code (index.ts:180-183). Command runners catch exceptions and convert to result messages (index.ts:227-229, 254-256, 273-276, 300-302). Detailed errors include context (file paths, expected formats, etc.). Success messages go to stderr for logging commands (migrate.ts:468-470) or stdout for query commands (verify.ts output).
Edge Cases: File not found errors include path; Validation errors include expected format; Parsing errors include specific problem; Key loading errors distinguish between file access and format issues; Migration errors describe invalid path with current and target layer; Uncaught exceptions in command runner converted to error result; Exit code 1 on CLI error (index.ts:182); Exit code 0 on success (main returns void)
Notes: Deps: console.error - stderr output, process.exit - exit code control

User Story: As a user, I want to inscribe arbitrary data (with a MIME type) onto the Bitcoin blockchain via the Ordinals protocol, so that I can permanently store data on Bitcoin.
Expected Behaviour: BitcoinManager.inscribeData(data, contentType, feeRate?) creates an inscription by delegating to the configured ordinalsProvider. Validates data is non-null, contentType is a valid MIME type, and feeRate is positive (max 10,000 sat/vB for safety). Resolves fee rate via: (1) external FeeOracleAdapter if configured, (2) fallback to ordinalsProvider.estimateFee(), (3) user-provided feeRate. Returns OrdinalsInscription with satoshi ID, inscriptionId, content, contentType, txid, vout, blockHeight, and recorded feeRate. Emits telemetry for fee resolution and errors.
Edge Cases: Data is null or undefined - rejects with INVALID_INPUT; ContentType is empty or not a string - rejects with INVALID_INPUT; ContentType does not match MIME format regex - rejects with INVALID_INPUT; FeeRate is non-positive or non-finite - rejects with INVALID_INPUT; FeeRate exceeds 10,000 sat/vB - rejects as potential fund drainage; No ordinalsProvider configured - rejects with ORD_PROVIDER_REQUIRED; OrdinalsProvider lacks createInscription method - rejects with ORD_PROVIDER_UNSUPPORTED; Provider returns invalid inscriptionId or txid - rejects with ORD_PROVIDER_INVALID_RESPONSE; Retrieved satoshi fails validation - rejects with INVALID_SATOSHI; Both feeOracle and ordinalsProvider fail - falls back to user-provided feeRate; No fee rate available from any source - returns undefined, user must provide explicit feeRate
Notes: Deps: OrdinalsProvider adapter (ordinalsProvider config), FeeOracleAdapter (optional, feeOracle config), Telemetry hooks for metrics/logging, validateSatoshiNumber() utility

User Story: As a user, I want to look up an inscription by ID to see its current state (satoshi, content, blockHeight), so that I can verify it was created or monitor confirmation status.
Expected Behaviour: BitcoinManager.trackInscription(inscriptionId) queries ordinalsProvider.getInscriptionById(inscriptionId). Returns null if provider returns null or no provider is configured. Otherwise returns OrdinalsInscription with satoshi, inscriptionId, content, contentType, txid, vout, blockHeight.
Edge Cases: No ordinalsProvider configured - returns null; inscriptionId is empty string - provider.getInscriptionById() returns null, trackInscription returns null; Inscription not found on chain - provider returns null, trackInscription returns null; Provider throws error - exception propagates to caller
Notes: Deps: OrdinalsProvider adapter

User Story: As a user, I want to send an inscription from one address to another by transferring its satoshi, so that I can transfer ownership of the inscribed data.
Expected Behaviour: BitcoinManager.transferInscription(inscription, toAddress) validates inscription object has inscriptionId, validates toAddress via validateBitcoinAddress(toAddress, network), resolves feeRate via same priority as inscribeData. Calls ordinalsProvider.transferInscription(inscriptionId, toAddress, {feeRate}). Returns BitcoinTransaction with txid, vin (from inscription.txid:vout or provider response), vout (recipient at dust limit or provider response), fee, blockHeight, confirmations. Updates inscription.satoshi if provider returns new satoshi.
Edge Cases: inscription is null or not object - rejects with INVALID_INPUT; inscription.inscriptionId is missing or not string - rejects with INVALID_INPUT; toAddress is empty or not string - rejects with INVALID_INPUT; toAddress fails Bitcoin address validation - rejects with INVALID_ADDRESS; No ordinalsProvider configured - rejects with ORD_PROVIDER_REQUIRED; OrdinalsProvider lacks transferInscription method - rejects with ORD_PROVIDER_UNSUPPORTED; Provider returns missing txid - rejects with ORD_PROVIDER_INVALID_RESPONSE; No fee rate available - uses effectiveFeeRate as undefined if no oracle/provider
Notes: Deps: OrdinalsProvider adapter, FeeOracleAdapter (optional), validateBitcoinAddress() utility with network awareness

User Story: As a user, I want to check if a satoshi has multiple inscriptions (indicating potential front-running), so that I can avoid selling to a satoshi that has been tampered with.
Expected Behaviour: BitcoinManager.preventFrontRunning(satoshi) calls ordinalsProvider.getInscriptionsBySatoshi(satoshi), returns true if list length <= 1 (safe), false if > 1 (multiple inscriptions detected). Returns true if no provider configured.
Edge Cases: satoshi is empty string - throws SATOSHI_REQUIRED error; satoshi not found - provider returns empty array, returns true (safe); satoshi has exactly 1 inscription - returns true (safe); satoshi has 2+ inscriptions - returns false (front-running detected); No ordinalsProvider - returns true
Notes: Deps: OrdinalsProvider adapter (optional)

User Story: As a user, I want to extract the satoshi identifier from an inscription ID, so that I can track which specific satoshi an inscription is bound to.
Expected Behaviour: BitcoinManager.getSatoshiFromInscription(inscriptionId) calls ordinalsProvider.getInscriptionById(inscriptionId), extracts satoshi field, validates it via validateSatoshiNumber(). Returns satoshi string if valid, null if missing or validation fails. Does not return empty/invalid satoshi.
Edge Cases: inscriptionId is empty - provider returns null, returns null; Inscription found but satoshi is missing - returns null; Inscription found but satoshi fails validateSatoshiNumber() - returns null (does not return invalid value); No ordinalsProvider - returns null
Notes: Deps: OrdinalsProvider adapter, validateSatoshiNumber() utility

User Story: As a user, I want to verify that a did:btco DID actually exists on Bitcoin by checking its satoshi inscription, so that I can confirm the DID is real.
Expected Behaviour: BitcoinManager.validateBTCODID(didId) extracts satoshi from did:btco DID format (did:btco:satoshi for mainnet, did:btco:test:satoshi or did:btco:sig:satoshi for testnet/signet), validates satoshi via validateSatoshiNumber(), calls ordinalsProvider.getInscriptionsBySatoshi(satoshi), returns true if array length > 0, false otherwise. Returns false if extraction or validation fails.
Edge Cases: didId doesn't start with 'did:btco:' - returns false; Malformed DID (wrong number of colons) - returns false; Unknown network prefix (not 'test' or 'sig') - returns false; Extracted satoshi fails validateSatoshiNumber() - returns false; No ordinalsProvider - returns false; Satoshi not found on chain - provider returns empty array, returns false
Notes: Deps: OrdinalsProvider adapter, validateSatoshiNumber() utility

User Story: As a user with a hardware wallet or custom signing flow, I want to build a Partially Signed Bitcoin Transaction (PSBT) that I can sign offline, so that I can control the signing process.
Expected Behaviour: PSBTBuilder.build(params: BuildPsbtParams) takes utxos, outputs, changeAddress, feeRate, network, dustLimit. Sorts UTXOs ascending by value. Iteratively selects UTXOs using greedy accumulation until total >= targetAmount + estimatedFee. Estimates vbytes as 10 + inputs*68 + outputs*31. Re-estimates after determining if change output is needed (change >= dustLimit). Returns BuildPsbtResult with psbtBase64 (JSON payload base64-encoded), selectedUtxos, fee, changeOutput (or undefined if dust). PSBT encodes as JSON: {version: 0, inputs: [{txid, vout}], outputs: [{address, value}, ...], fee}.
Edge Cases: No UTXOs provided - throws 'No UTXOs'; No outputs specified - throws 'No outputs'; Total value < target + initial fee estimate - throws 'Insufficient funds'; Change amount 0 < amount < dustLimit - adds to fee instead of creating change output; Base64 encoding fails - falls back to 'psbt:' + JSON string format
Notes: Deps: Node.js Buffer API for base64 encoding (with fallback to btoa)

User Story: As a user, I want to build a Bitcoin transaction that sends satoshis from available UTXOs to a recipient address with proper change handling, so that I can construct custom transfers.
Expected Behaviour: buildTransferTransaction(availableUtxos, recipientAddress, amountSats, feeRateSatsPerVb, options) calls selectUtxos() with forbidInscriptionBearingInputs, allowLocked, changeAddress, feeEstimate options. Builds transaction with vin from selected UTXOs, output to recipient at amountSats, second output to changeAddress if change >= DUST_LIMIT_SATS (546). Returns {tx: BitcoinTransaction, selection: SelectionResult} containing vout array, fee, and change info.
Edge Cases: No UTXOs available - selectUtxos throws UtxoSelectionError; Insufficient funds - selectUtxos throws INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS; Change below dust - not included in outputs, absorbed into fee; Inscription-bearing inputs in availableUtxos with forbidInscriptionBearingInputs=true - filtered out by selectUtxos
Notes: Deps: selectUtxos() function from utxo.ts, DUST_LIMIT_SATS constant (546)

User Story: As a user, I want to automatically select UTXOs from my wallet to fund a payment, accounting for transaction fees and change, so that I don't have to manually calculate coin selection.
Expected Behaviour: selectUtxos(utxos, options: SelectionOptions) filters UTXOs based on locked/inscription policies, sorts by value descending. Greedily accumulates UTXOs until accumulated >= targetAmountSats + estimatedFee. Recalculates fee after determining change output (estimates 2 outputs initially, 1 if change would be dust). If change < DUST_LIMIT_SATS, adds to fee instead. Returns SelectionResult {selected, feeSats, changeSats}. Throws UtxoSelectionError with code INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS, TOO_LOW_FEE, DUST_OUTPUT, CONFLICTING_LOCKS, or SAT_SAFETY.
Edge Cases: feeRateSatsPerVb <= 0 - throws TOO_LOW_FEE; targetAmountSats < DUST_LIMIT_SATS (546) - throws DUST_OUTPUT; allowLocked=false but only locked UTXOs available - throws CONFLICTING_LOCKS; forbidInscriptionBearingInputs=true but all UTXOs have inscriptions - throws INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS; change > 0 but < DUST_LIMIT_SATS - absorbed into fee, change returned as 0
Notes: Deps: estimateFeeSats() for fee calculation, DUST_LIMIT_SATS constant

Feature Name: Select Resource UTXOs with Advanced Strategies
User Story: As a user managing inscriptions, I want to select UTXOs while avoiding those that contain resources, with options to prefer older UTXOs or closer-value matches, so that I don't accidentally spend inscribed satoshis.
Expected Behaviour: selectResourceUtxos(availableUtxos, options: ResourceUtxoSelectionOptions) filters out avoidUtxoIds and UTXOs with hasResource=true (unless allowResourceUtxos=true). Applies sorting: preferCloserAmount sorts by |value - requiredAmount|, preferOlder sorts by txid, default sorts descending by value. Attempts single-UTXO match first, then accumulates greedily. Recalculates fee with actual input count. If final change < MIN_DUST_LIMIT (546), adds to fee. Returns ResourceUtxoSelectionResult {selectedUtxos, totalSelectedValue, estimatedFee, changeAmount}. Throws error if all UTXOs filtered out or insufficient funds.
Edge Cases: All UTXOs have hasResource=true and allowResourceUtxos=false - throws specific error message; No eligible UTXOs after filtering - throws 'No eligible UTXOs'; Single UTXO covers amount (no accumulation needed) - selected immediately; Change < MIN_DUST_LIMIT - added to fee instead of output; requiredAmountBigInt + estimatedFee uses bigint arithmetic for precision
Notes: Deps: estimateTransactionSize() for size calculation, calculateFee() using bigint for precision

User Story: As a user making a payment, I want a simple function that automatically excludes resource UTXOs and selects enough UTXOs to cover the payment, so that I can make safe payments without worrying about inscriptions.
Expected Behaviour: selectUtxosForPayment(availableUtxos, requiredAmount, feeRate) calls selectResourceUtxos() with allowResourceUtxos=false (enforced). Returns ResourceUtxoSelectionResult with selectedUtxos, totalSelectedValue, estimatedFee, changeAmount. Fails if all UTXOs contain resources.
Edge Cases: All UTXOs have hasResource=true - throws 'All available UTXOs contain resources' error; No UTXOs provided - selectResourceUtxos throws 'No eligible UTXOs'
Notes: Deps: selectResourceUtxos() with enforced allowResourceUtxos=false

User Story: As a user, I want to mark UTXOs that contain inscriptions so that downstream coin selection can avoid them, so that I can prevent accidental spending of valuable inscriptions.
Expected Behaviour: tagResourceUtxos(utxos: ResourceUtxo[], resourceData?: {[utxoId: string]: boolean}) adds/updates hasResource flag on each UTXO. If resourceData provided, sets hasResource from map lookup (key='txid:vout'). Otherwise uses existing utxo.hasResource or defaults to false. Returns new array with tagged UTXOs.
Edge Cases: resourceData not provided - uses UTXO's existing hasResource or false; UTXO ID not in resourceData - defaults hasResource to false; resourceData maps to explicitly false - hasResource set false
Notes: Deps: ResourceUtxo interface

User Story: As a user, I want to calculate the transaction fee in satoshis based on estimated transaction size and fee rate, so that I can know how much the transaction will cost.
Expected Behaviour: estimateFeeSats(numInputs, numOutputs, feeRateSatsPerVb, feeEstimate?) calculates vbytes as baseTxBytes + numInputs*bytesPerInput + numOutputs*bytesPerOutput. Multiplies vbytes * feeRateSatsPerVb and rounds up. Uses DEFAULT_FEE_ESTIMATE if feeEstimate not provided (148 bytes/input, 34 bytes/output, 10 bytes base).
Edge Cases: numInputs=0 - calculates fee with no input bytes; numOutputs=0 - calculates fee with only overhead; feeRateSatsPerVb very small - still calculates accurately
Notes: Deps: DEFAULT_FEE_ESTIMATE constant

Feature Name: Calculate Fee with Minimum Relay Enforcement
User Story: As a user, I want to ensure my transaction fee meets Bitcoin's minimum relay rate, so that my transaction doesn't get rejected by the mempool.
Expected Behaviour: calculateFee(vbytes: number, feeRate: number): bigint validates vbytes > 0 and feeRate > 0 (logs warning and returns 0n if not). Calculates calculatedFee = ceil(vbytes * feeRate). Calculates minimumFee = ceil(vbytes * MIN_RELAY_FEE_RATE) where MIN_RELAY_FEE_RATE = 1.1 sat/vB. Returns max(1n, calculatedFee, minimumFee) as bigint. Ensures result is always >= 1 satoshi.
Edge Cases: vbytes=0 - logs warning, returns 0n; feeRate=0 - logs warning, returns 0n; vbytes or feeRate NaN - logs warning, returns 0n; calculatedFee < minimumFee - returns minimumFee instead; Both < 1 - returns 1n (minimum)
Notes: Deps: MIN_RELAY_FEE_RATE constant (1.1 sat/vB)

User Story: As a user, I want to estimate how many virtual bytes a transaction will be before building it, so that I can calculate fees accurately.
Expected Behaviour: estimateTransactionSize(inputCount, outputCount) returns 10 + inputCount*68 + outputCount*31. Formula assumes segwit (10 vB overhead, 68 vB per P2WPKH input, 31 vB per output).
Edge Cases: inputCount=0 - returns overhead only (10 vB); outputCount=0 - only outputs for change/recipient missing (rare edge case)
Notes: Deps: None

User Story: As a user, I want to query an ordinals provider HTTP API to fetch inscription details by ID, so that I can retrieve inscription metadata and content.
Expected Behaviour: OrdinalsClient.resolveInscription(identifier) fetches /inscription/{identifier} and /content/{identifier} from rpcUrl. Parses inscription JSON, extracts owner_output as 'txid:vout' pair, converts sat to string, returns OrdinalsInscription. On content fetch failure, throws error. Supports both {inscription_ids: []} and {data: {inscription_ids: []}} response shapes.
Edge Cases: identifier is empty - returns null; Inscription endpoint returns 404 - returns null; Content endpoint returns 404 - throws error; owner_output missing or not 'txid:vout' format - defaults txid='unknown', vout=0; sat not present - satoshi=''
Notes: Deps: fetch API, JSON parsing

Feature Name: Fetch Sat Info from Ordinals API
User Story: As a user, I want to query which inscriptions are on a particular satoshi, so that I can check if a satoshi has been inscribed.
Expected Behaviour: OrdinalsClient.getSatInfo(satoshi) fetches /sat/{satoshi} from rpcUrl, parses JSON, extracts inscription_ids array (handles both {inscription_ids: []} and {data: {inscription_ids: []}} shapes). Returns {inscription_ids: []} (or empty array if data missing).
Edge Cases: satoshi endpoint returns 404 - returns {inscription_ids: []}; inscription_ids field missing or not array - returns empty array; Response wrapped in {data: ...} - unwraps it
Notes: Deps: fetch API

Feature Name: Fetch Inscription Metadata from Ordinals API
User Story: As a user, I want to retrieve CBOR-encoded metadata for an inscription, so that I can access structured data embedded in the inscription.
Expected Behaviour: OrdinalsClient.getMetadata(inscriptionId) fetches /r/metadata/{inscriptionId} from rpcUrl as JSON. If text is quoted JSON string, parses it. Attempts to decode as hex bytes to CBOR. Returns decoded object or null if parsing/decoding fails.
Edge Cases: inscriptionId is empty - returns null; Metadata endpoint returns 404 - returns null; Response is quoted JSON - attempts JSON.parse first; Hex decode fails - catches error, returns null; CBOR decode fails - catches error, returns null
Notes: Deps: fetch API, decodeCbor() utility, hexToBytes() utility

User Story: As a user, I want to query all inscriptions on a specific satoshi, so that I can check for multiple inscriptions (front-running).
Expected Behaviour: OrdinalsClient.getInscriptionsBySatoshi(satoshi) calls getSatInfo(satoshi), retrieves inscription_ids array, calls resolveInscription() for each ID in parallel, filters out null results. Returns array of OrdinalsInscription objects.
Edge Cases: satoshi has no inscriptions - returns empty array; Some resolveInscription() calls fail - failed items filtered out, succeeds return included; All resolveInscription() calls fail - returns empty array
Notes: Deps: getSatInfo(), resolveInscription()

User Story: As a user, I want to broadcast a signed transaction to the Bitcoin network with idempotency guarantees, so that retries don't result in double-spends.
Expected Behaviour: BroadcastClient.broadcastIdempotent(txidKey, create: () => Promise<string>) maintains inflight Map. If txidKey already in flight, returns existing Promise. Otherwise calls create(), stores Promise, returns it, and deletes from Map on completion (finally). Ensures only one broadcast per key.
Edge Cases: create() called multiple times with same txidKey - second call waits for first Promise; create() throws error - error propagates, key removed from inflight; Multiple concurrent calls with same txidKey - only first executes
Notes: Deps: Promise handling

User Story: As a user, I want to broadcast a transaction and wait for confirmation with polling, so that I know when my transaction is confirmed on chain.
Expected Behaviour: BroadcastClient.broadcastAndConfirm(txHex, options) calls broadcastIdempotent with broadcaster(txHex), then polls statusProvider(txid) at interval (default 500ms, min 100ms) for up to maxAttempts attempts (default 20). Stops early if status.confirmed=true. Returns {txid, confirmations}. If max attempts reached, returns last known confirmations (may be 0).
Edge Cases: Transaction confirmed before polling starts - returns immediately with confirmations; Transaction never confirms - returns after 20 attempts with confirmations=0 (or last known); pollIntervalMs < 100 - uses 100ms minimum; maxAttempts < 1 - uses 1
Notes: Deps: broadcastIdempotent(), setTimeout() for polling

User Story: As a user, I want to generate the commit phase of an ordinals inscription, including a P2TR address to send funds to and a PSBT, so that I can prepare an inscription for reveal.
Expected Behaviour: createCommitTransaction(params: CommitTransactionParams) validates inputs (content, contentType, utxos, changeAddress, feeRate > 0). Pre-filters UTXOs to ensure all have valid scriptPubKey. Creates ordinals.Inscription with tags (contentType, optional metadata/pointer) and body. Generates random reveal keypair via schnorr. Creates P2TR address via btc.p2tr using reveal public key and inscription script tree. Iteratively selects UTXOs: estimates fee with 1 input, 2 outputs initially; if accumulated >= targetAmount + fee, recalculates with actual input count and final output count (1 if change < dust); repeats up to 5 iterations if fee increases. Returns CommitTransactionResult with commitAddress, commitPsbtBase64, commitAmount, selectedUtxos, fees, revealPrivateKey, revealPublicKey, inscriptionScript {script, controlBlock, leafVersion}.
Edge Cases: No UTXOs provided - throws 'No UTXOs provided'; No content or empty content - throws 'missing content'; No contentType - throws 'missing content type'; No changeAddress - throws 'Change address is required'; feeRate <= 0 - throws 'Invalid fee rate'; No valid spendable UTXOs (all lack scriptPubKey) - throws detailed error with reasons; Insufficient funds after selection - throws error with required vs available breakdown; Selection fails after 5 iterations - throws error with iteration count and values; Change < MIN_DUST_LIMIT (546) - added to final fee, no change output
Notes: Deps: @scure/btc-signer for transaction building, micro-ordinals for inscription script creation, @noble/curves for schnorr keypair generation, calculateFee() for fee estimation, selectUtxos() for UTXO selection, estimateCommitTxSize() for size estimation

User Story: As a user on signet testnet, I want to connect to an ord node to fetch inscription data and optionally broadcast transactions, so that I can test ordinals functionality.
Expected Behaviour: SignetProvider(options: SignetProviderOptions) initializes with ordUrl (required), bitcoinRpcUrl (optional), timeout (default 10s). Read operations (getInscriptionById, getInscriptionsBySatoshi, getTransactionStatus, estimateFee) are fully supported via ord HTTP API. Write operations (createInscription, transferInscription) throw if bitcoinRpcUrl not configured, otherwise throw 'not yet supported' (programmatic inscription creation not available on signet).
Edge Cases: ordUrl has trailing slash - strips it; bitcoinRpcUrl not configured for write operations - throws helpful error message; Fetch timeout - AbortSignal.timeout() aborts request; estimateFee uses Bitcoin RPC if available - falls back to default (blocks * 2 for signet)
Notes: Deps: fetch API with AbortSignal.timeout(), OrdinalsClient for HTTP requests, Bitcoin Core JSON-RPC (if bitcoinRpcUrl configured)

User Story: As a user, I want to wrap an OrdinalsClient with retry logic and expose a standard OrdinalsProvider interface, so that I can use it in SDK configuration.
Expected Behaviour: OrdinalsClientProvider(client: OrdinalsClient, options?: {retries?, baseUrl?}) wraps client with withRetry(). Exposes getSatInfo(), resolveInscription(), getMetadata(), estimateFee() methods, all with retry logic (default 2 retries). resolveInscription() validates response has inscriptionId, satoshi (as number), contentType, constructs content_url from baseUrl.
Edge Cases: baseUrl not provided to resolveInscription - throws 'baseUrl is required'; Satoshi value is NaN - throws 'Invalid satoshi value'; retries not specified - defaults to 2; All retries fail - error propagates
Notes: Deps: OrdinalsClient, withRetry() utility

User Story: As a provider developer, I want to implement the ResourceProvider interface to support resource queries (inscriptions, collections, crawling), so that I can integrate custom data sources.
Expected Behaviour: ResourceProvider interface defines methods: resolve(), resolveInscription(), resolveInfo(), resolveCollection(), getSatInfo(), getMetadata(), getAllResources(), getAllResourcesChronological(), getInscriptionLocationsByAddress(), getInscriptionByNumber(), getAddressOutputs(), getOutputDetails(). Each method returns Promise or AsyncGenerator of LinkedResource, Inscription, ResourceInfo, or metadata. OrdNodeProvider provides stub implementation with placeholder data.
Edge Cases: Implementer leaves methods unimplemented - returns empty arrays or placeholder data; AsyncGenerator methods used with await async for - iteration works correctly
Notes: Deps: LinkedResource, Inscription, ResourceInfo, ResourceCrawlOptions types

User Story: As a developer testing ordinals functionality, I want a mock OrdinalsClient that returns placeholder data without hitting a real ord node, so that I can develop offline.
Expected Behaviour: OrdinalsClient provides basic stub methods: getInscriptionById() returns null if no id, resolveInscription() returns null if not found, broadcastTransaction() returns txid or 'txid', getTransactionStatus() returns {confirmed: false}, estimateFee() returns Math.max(1, blocks) * 10. Used for basic unit testing.
Edge Cases: Empty id passed - returns null; No network connectivity - methods do not require network
Notes: Deps: None (stub implementation)

User Story: As a developer, I want to create a new asset from a collection of resources, so that I can build Originals with version-tracked content
Expected Behaviour: LifecycleManager.createAsset(resources) validates all resources, creates a DID:peer document, optionally registers private key in keyStore, and returns an OriginalsAsset with currentLayer='did:peer'. Emits 'asset:created' event with asset id, layer, resource count, and creation timestamp. Resources require id, type, contentType, and hex-encoded hash. At least one resource is required. file:LifecycleManager.ts:createAsset (lines 198-317)
Edge Cases: No resources provided - throws INVALID_INPUT error; Invalid resource object shape (missing id, type, contentType, or hash) - throws INVALID_RESOURCE error; Hash not valid hex string - throws INVALID_RESOURCE error; ContentType not valid MIME format - throws INVALID_RESOURCE error; KeyStore configured - automatically registers first verification method's private key; Event emission deferred via queueMicrotask to allow caller to subscribe first
Notes: Deps: DIDManager (createDIDPeer), KeyStore (setPrivateKey, optional), EventEmitter, Logger, MetricsCollector

Feature Name: Create Typed Original with Kind-Specific Validation
User Story: As a developer, I want to create an Original with a specific kind (App, Agent, Module, Dataset, Media, Document), so that my assets benefit from kind-specific validation and metadata schemas
Expected Behaviour: LifecycleManager.createTypedOriginal(kind, manifest, options) validates manifest kind matches requested kind, runs KindRegistry validation, creates asset from manifest resources, and attaches manifest as non-enumerable property. Emits 'asset:created' event including kind, name, and version. Warnings logged if skipValidation is false. file:LifecycleManager.ts:createTypedOriginal (lines 350-430)
Edge Cases: Manifest kind does not match requested kind - throws INVALID_INPUT error; Validation fails and skipValidation=false - throws validation error from KindRegistry; Validation produces warnings - logged but operation continues unless strictMode=true; Manifest stored as non-enumerable property to avoid serialization issues
Notes: Deps: LifecycleManager.createAsset, KindRegistry.getInstance(), OriginalKind enum, OriginalManifest type

User Story: As a developer, I want to estimate the cost of creating a typed Original before committing, so that I can show users fee information and plan budget
Expected Behaviour: LifecycleManager.estimateTypedOriginalCost(manifest, targetLayer, feeRate) calculates inscription costs by determining manifest size (including metadata overhead), consulting fee oracle or ordinalsProvider for fee rate (defaults to 10 sat/vB), and returning CostEstimate with totalSats breakdown, dataSize, feeRate, and confidence level. Returns 0 cost for did:webvh target. file:LifecycleManager.ts:estimateTypedOriginalCost (lines 452-550)
Edge Cases: targetLayer='did:webvh' - returns 0 cost estimate with high confidence; feeRate not provided - queries feeOracle first, then ordinalsProvider, falls back to 10 sat/vB; No feeOracle or ordinalsProvider available - defaults to 10 sat/vB with low confidence; Resource size unknown - estimates 1000 bytes per resource for fee calculation; Manifest serialization produces large JSON - included in cost calculation
Notes: Deps: OriginalsConfig.feeOracle (optional), OriginalsConfig.ordinalsProvider (optional), CostEstimate type

User Story: As a developer, I want to publish a draft asset to web storage under a publisher DID, so that it becomes discoverable and accessible via HTTPS
Expected Behaviour: LifecycleManager.publishToWeb(asset, publisherDidOrSigner) validates asset is in did:peer layer, publishes all resources to storage with multibase-encoded hashes, migrates asset to did:webvh layer, records bindings for did:peer and did:webvh, and issues best-effort publication credential. Emits 'resource:published' events per resource and 'credential:issued' event. file:LifecycleManager.ts:publishToWeb (lines 552-599)
Edge Cases: Asset not in did:peer layer - throws INVALID_STATE error; publisherDidOrSigner is domain string - converts to did:webvh with encoded domain and ':user' path; publisherDidOrSigner is external signer - extracts verification method ID and validates it's did:webvh; Storage adapter not configured - uses MemoryStorageAdapter; Credential issuance fails - logs error but does not block publication; Resource has no content or url - stores hash as data
Notes: Deps: Asset.migrate(targetLayer, details), StorageAdapter (put or putObject), CredentialManager.createResourceCredential and sign methods, DIDManager.resolveDID, KeyStore (for signing)

User Story: As a developer, I want to inscribe an asset on Bitcoin, so that it's permanently anchored to the blockchain via Ordinals
Expected Behaviour: LifecycleManager.inscribeOnBitcoin(asset, feeRate) creates a JSON manifest of asset metadata, inscribes it via BitcoinManager.inscribeData(), migrates asset to did:btco with transaction details, records binding to satoshi/inscriptionId, and emits migration metrics. Returns asset in did:btco layer. file:LifecycleManager.ts:inscribeOnBitcoin (lines 829-910)
Edge Cases: Asset already in did:btco - no error but asset.currentLayer already set; feeRate out of range (<=0 or >1000000 sat/vB) - throws INVALID_INPUT error; Asset currentLayer not did:peer or did:webvh - throws NOT_IMPLEMENTED error; BitcoinManager not configured - creates new instance with OriginalsConfig; Inscription returns revealTxId or txid - uses revealTxId preferentially; Satoshi not returned - binds to inscriptionId instead
Notes: Deps: BitcoinManager.inscribeData(), Asset.migrate(), MetricsCollector

User Story: As a developer, I want to transfer ownership of a Bitcoin-inscribed asset to a new owner, so that users can trade inscribed Originals
Expected Behaviour: LifecycleManager.transferOwnership(asset, newOwner) validates asset is in did:btco layer and newOwner is valid Bitcoin address, extracts satoshi from asset provenance, calls BitcoinManager.transferInscription(), records transfer in asset provenance, and returns BitcoinTransaction. file:LifecycleManager.ts:transferOwnership (lines 912-975)
Edge Cases: Asset not in did:btco layer - throws INVALID_STATE error; newOwner not valid Bitcoin address - throws INVALID_ADDRESS with validation error message; Satoshi not in provenance - extracts from asset.id if format is 'did:btco:satoshi:...'; Satoshi extraction fails - falls back to 'unknown' placeholder
Notes: Deps: BitcoinManager.transferInscription(), Asset.recordTransfer(), validateBitcoinAddress() utility, OriginalsConfig.network

User Story: As a developer, I want to register a private key in the KeyStore, so that I can sign credentials and publish assets
Expected Behaviour: LifecycleManager.registerKey(verificationMethodId, privateKey) validates verificationMethodId and privateKey formats, decodes multibase-encoded private key using multikey utility, and stores in KeyStore via setPrivateKey(). file:LifecycleManager.ts:registerKey (lines 173-196)
Edge Cases: KeyStore not configured - throws KEYSTORE_REQUIRED error; verificationMethodId is empty or null - throws INVALID_INPUT error; privateKey is empty or null - throws INVALID_INPUT error; privateKey not valid multibase encoding - throws INVALID_KEY error; multikey.decodePrivateKey() throws - caught and re-thrown as INVALID_KEY error
Notes: Deps: KeyStore.setPrivateKey(), multikey.decodePrivateKey(), StructuredError

User Story: As a developer, I want to create multiple assets in parallel with configurable concurrency and retry logic, so that I can efficiently bulk-import Originals
Expected Behaviour: LifecycleManager.batchCreateAssets(resourcesList, options) delegates to BatchLifecycleOperations, validates all resources upfront, processes in parallel with concurrency control, supports fail-fast or continue-on-error modes, and returns BatchResult with successful/failed counts and metrics. file:LifecycleManager.ts:batchCreateAssets (lines 981-986), BatchLifecycleOperations.ts:batchCreateAssets (lines 54-117)
Edge Cases: Empty resourcesList - still creates batch, returns empty successful array; validateFirst=false - skips validation, may fail per-item during processing; continueOnError=true - collects all errors and returns partial results; continueOnError=false (default) - throws first error, stops processing; retryCount>0 - retries failed items with exponential backoff before marking failed
Notes: Deps: BatchLifecycleOperations, BatchOperationExecutor.execute(), BatchValidator.validateBatchCreate(), LifecycleManager.createAsset()

User Story: As a developer, I want to publish multiple assets to a domain in parallel, so that I can efficiently distribute Originals to web storage
Expected Behaviour: LifecycleManager.batchPublishToWeb(assets, domain, options) validates domain format (hostname, localhost, or IP with optional port), validates all assets in did:peer layer, publishes each asset with same domain and concurrency control, and returns BatchResult. file:LifecycleManager.ts:batchPublishToWeb (lines 991-997), BatchLifecycleOperations.ts:batchPublishToWeb (lines 122-202)
Edge Cases: domain contains port (e.g., localhost:5000) - split and validate port range 1-65535; domain is localhost - allowed for development even without TLD; domain is IPv4 address - recognized and allowed without TLD validation; domain has invalid port - throws INVALID_DOMAIN error; domain fails hostname regex - throws INVALID_DOMAIN error with suggestion
Notes: Deps: BatchLifecycleOperations.batchPublishToWeb(), LifecycleManager.publishToWeb(), Domain regex validation

Feature Name: Batch Inscribe on Bitcoin with Cost Optimization
User Story: As a developer, I want to inscribe multiple assets on Bitcoin with an option to batch them into one transaction for 30%+ cost savings, so that I can minimize fees for bulk operations
Expected Behaviour: LifecycleManager.batchInscribeOnBitcoin(assets, options) delegates to BatchLifecycleOperations. If singleTransaction=true, combines all assets into one Bitcoin inscription with shared overhead, splits fees proportionally by asset size, and returns BatchResult showing cost savings. Otherwise, inscribes each separately. file:LifecycleManager.ts:batchInscribeOnBitcoin (lines 1003-1008), BatchLifecycleOperations.ts:batchInscribeOnBitcoin (lines 208-227)
Edge Cases: singleTransaction=true - batches into one manifest JSON, all assets share one txid but track index in provenance; singleTransaction=false - each asset inscribed separately in own transaction (default fallback); Asset size calculation includes metadata + resource content; Fee split uses proportional distribution by asset data size; All assets in batch reuse same inscriptionId (marked with batchIndex and batchId in migration)
Notes: Deps: BatchLifecycleOperations.batchInscribeOnBitcoin(), BitcoinManager.inscribeData(), Asset.migrate(), estimateBatchSavings() utility

User Story: As a developer, I want to transfer ownership of multiple inscribed assets in batch, so that I can efficiently handle bulk trades or distributions
Expected Behaviour: LifecycleManager.batchTransferOwnership(transfers, options) validates all transfers (asset must be did:btco, to must be valid Bitcoin address), processes in parallel with concurrency control, and returns BatchResult with successful/failed transaction results. file:LifecycleManager.ts:batchTransferOwnership (lines 1013-1018), BatchLifecycleOperations.ts:batchTransferOwnership (lines 490-561)
Edge Cases: Transfer object missing asset or to field - validation error; Asset not in did:btco layer - validation error per item; Bitcoin address invalid for network - validation error with network-specific message; continueOnError=true - validates all before processing, records errors per item; continueOnError=false - stops on first transfer failure
Notes: Deps: BatchLifecycleOperations.batchTransferOwnership(), LifecycleManager.transferOwnership(), validateBitcoinAddress(), OriginalsConfig.network

User Story: As a developer, I want to configure batch operations with concurrency, retries, and timeouts, so that I can tune bulk operations for my use case
Expected Behaviour: BatchOperationExecutor.execute(items, operation, options) processes items with configurable maxConcurrent (1 for sequential, >1 for concurrent chunks), retryCount with exponential backoff delay, timeoutMs per operation, and continueOnError flag. Returns BatchResult with duration metrics and retry attempt counts. file:BatchOperations.ts:BatchOperationExecutor.execute (lines 107-214)
Edge Cases: maxConcurrent=1 - sequential processing (default); maxConcurrent>items.length - processes all in parallel; retryCount=0 - no retries (default); retryDelay applied with exponential backoff: baseDelay * 2^attempt; timeoutMs reached - rejects with timeout error message; continueOnError=true - logs per-item errors, continues, returns partial results; continueOnError=false - throws first error, halts processing
Notes: Deps: Promise API (race, all), Date.now() for timing

User Story: As a developer, I want to validate whether an asset can migrate to a target layer before attempting the operation, so that I can provide clear error feedback
Expected Behaviour: LifecycleManager.validateMigration(asset, targetLayer) performs comprehensive pre-flight checks: valid layer transition, resources have all required fields with valid hashes, DID document valid, credentials structurally valid, Bitcoin readiness (for did:btco). Returns MigrationValidation with bool valid, errors[], warnings[], and checks details. file:LifecycleManager.ts:validateMigration (lines 1502-1601)
Edge Cases: Asset currentLayer not in validTransitions - adds error, checks.layerTransition=false; Asset has zero resources - adds error; Resource missing id, type, contentType, or hash - adds per-resource error; Resource hash not hex string - adds error; No DID document - adds error, checks.didDocumentValid=false; did:btco target without ordinalsProvider - adds error, checks.bitcoinReadiness=false; Manifest size >100KB for did:btco - adds warning about high costs; targetLayer=did:btco checks ordinalsProvider configuration
Notes: Deps: LayerType definition, OriginalsConfig.ordinalsProvider

User Story: As a developer, I want to estimate the cost of migrating an existing asset to a target layer, so that I can show users fees before confirming migration
Expected Behaviour: LifecycleManager.estimateCost(asset, targetLayer, feeRate) calculates inscription costs for did:btco target (based on asset manifest JSON size, transaction overhead, and fee rate), returns 0 for did:webvh, 0 for did:peer. Consults fee oracle or provider if feeRate not given. Returns CostEstimate with totalSats, breakdown, dataSize, feeRate, confidence. file:LifecycleManager.ts:estimateCost (lines 1369-1476)
Edge Cases: targetLayer='did:webvh' - returns 0 cost; targetLayer='did:peer' - returns 0 cost (no migration needed); targetLayer='did:btco' with no feeRate - queries feeOracle, then ordinalsProvider, defaults to 10; Oracle/provider throws - falls back to default gracefully; Asset has no resources - uses empty array in manifest size calculation; Manifest JSON very large - included in dataSize and fee calculation
Notes: Deps: OriginalsConfig.feeOracle, OriginalsConfig.ordinalsProvider, CostEstimate type

Feature Name: Asset Provenance Querying with Fluent API
User Story: As a developer, I want to query an asset's provenance history with a fluent API to filter migrations and transfers by date range or criteria, so that I can audit asset origins and ownership changes
Expected Behaviour: OriginalsAsset.queryProvenance() returns ProvenanceQuery with fluent chainable methods: after(date), before(date), between(start, end), migrations(), transfers(), count(), first(), last(), all(). MigrationQuery filters by fromLayer, toLayer, transactionId, inscriptionId. TransferQuery filters by from address, to address, transactionId. Results sorted by timestamp. file:OriginalsAsset.ts:queryProvenance (line 191), ProvenanceQuery.ts (lines 10-281)
Edge Cases: No migrations or transfers - all() returns empty array, first/last return null, count returns 0; after/before/between filters out items outside date range; fromLayer/toLayer filters return only matching migrations; chaining multiple filters applies all (AND logic); switching between migrations() and transfers() returns appropriate typed query
Notes: Deps: ProvenanceChain structure, MigrationQuery class, TransferQuery class

User Story: As a developer, I want to retrieve a quick summary of an asset's provenance without querying details, so that I can display asset lineage in a UI
Expected Behaviour: OriginalsAsset.getProvenanceSummary() returns {created, creator, currentLayer, migrationCount, transferCount, lastActivity}. Creator is asset.id (DID), created is ISO timestamp from provenance, lastActivity is most recent migration/transfer timestamp or created time. file:OriginalsAsset.ts:getProvenanceSummary (lines 219-238)
Edge Cases: No migrations or transfers - lastActivity equals createdAt; Both migrations and transfers exist - lastActivity is maximum timestamp; Creator field uses asset.id not provenance.creator field
Notes: Deps: ProvenanceChain structure

User Story: As a developer, I want to verify the integrity of an asset's resources, DID document, and credentials, so that I can ensure authenticity
Expected Behaviour: OriginalsAsset.verify(deps) returns boolean. Validates: DID document structure via validateDIDDocument, resources have valid hashes (computed vs stored), credentials structure via validateCredential, optionally verifies credential signatures cryptographically if credentialManager provided. Returns false on any failure, true if all checks pass. file:OriginalsAsset.ts:verify (lines 258-325)
Edge Cases: Resource with inline content - hashes computed and compared; Resource with url and fetch provided - fetches and hashes, silently treats fetch errors as unverifiable (continues); Resource with only hash - structural validation only (cannot verify without content); credentialManager provided without didManager - skips crypto verification; Credential validation via credentialManager - verifies signature; Any validation failure - returns false immediately
Notes: Deps: validateDIDDocument() utility, validateCredential() utility, hashResource() utility, CredentialManager (optional for crypto verification), DIDManager (optional, paired with credentialManager)

User Story: As a developer, I want to subscribe to lifecycle events on an asset, so that I can react to migrations, transfers, and resource updates
Expected Behaviour: OriginalsAsset.on(eventType, handler) subscribes to event (asset:migrated, asset:transferred, resource:version:created, etc.), returns unsubscribe function. OriginalsAsset.once(eventType, handler) subscribes once then auto-unsubscribes. OriginalsAsset.off(eventType, handler) removes specific handler. Events emitted via internal EventEmitter when migrations, transfers, or versions occur. file:OriginalsAsset.ts:on/once/off (lines 334-366)
Edge Cases: Handler throws error - EventEmitter catches and logs (does not propagate); Unsubscribe function called multiple times - safe (removes handler once); once() handler auto-removed after first emission; Handler subscribed after asset already migrated - misses event (no replay); multiple handlers on same event type - all called in order
Notes: Deps: EventEmitter, EventTypeMap

User Story: As a developer, I want to version resources by creating new immutable versions linked to prior versions, so that I can track content changes while preserving history
Expected Behaviour: OriginalsAsset.addResourceVersion(resourceId, newContent, contentType, changes) creates new AssetResource with incremented version number, unique hash, links to previous version via previousVersionHash, adds to asset.resources array, emits 'resource:version:created' event, and records update in provenance.resourceUpdates. Returns new AssetResource. file:OriginalsAsset.ts:addResourceVersion (lines 390-481)
Edge Cases: Resource not found (no prior versions) - throws error; Content unchanged from current version - throws error; newContent as Buffer or string - handles both, normalizes to Buffer for hashing; Current resource at any version - uses latest version (highest version number); Version number incremented from latest or defaults to 2 if version missing; createdAt timestamp added to new resource version
Notes: Deps: hashResource() utility, EventEmitter

User Story: As a developer, I want to retrieve specific versions of a resource and view the complete version history, so that I can audit resource changes and understand evolution
Expected Behaviour: OriginalsAsset.getResourceVersion(resourceId, version) returns AssetResource for exact version or null if not found. OriginalsAsset.getAllVersions(resourceId) returns all versions sorted by version number ascending. OriginalsAsset.getResourceHistory(resourceId) returns ResourceVersionHistory with versions[], currentVersion, versionCount, or null if resource not found. file:OriginalsAsset.ts:getResourceVersion/getAllVersions/getResourceHistory (lines 489-514)
Edge Cases: resourceId never created - all methods return null or empty array; version out of range (too high or <=0) - getResourceVersion returns null; version matches exactly - getResourceVersion returns that AssetResource; getAllVersions with one version - returns array with single element; ResourceVersionManager tracks versions separately from resources array
Notes: Deps: ResourceVersionManager.getHistory(), ResourceVersionManager.getVersion(), ResourceVersionHistory type

User Story: As a developer, I want to verify the integrity of a resource's version chain, so that I can ensure all versions link correctly via previousVersionHash
Expected Behaviour: ResourceVersionManager.verifyChain(resourceId) returns boolean. Checks: version numbers are sequential starting at 1, first version has no previousVersionHash, each subsequent version's previousVersionHash matches prior version's hash. Returns false if chain broken, true if all valid. file:ResourceVersioning.ts:verifyChain (lines 115-144)
Edge Cases: No versions for resourceId - returns false; Version numbers not sequential (e.g., 1, 3, 2) - returns false; First version has previousVersionHash - returns false; Later version previousVersionHash doesn't match prior version hash - returns false; All versions valid and sequential - returns true
Notes: Deps: ResourceVersionManager state

User Story: As a developer, I want to create Originals with kind-specific metadata (App, Agent, Module, Dataset, Media, Document) with type-safe schema validation, so that my assets are properly categorized and validated
Expected Behaviour: OriginalKind enum defines 6 kinds. OriginalManifest<K> is generic with kind-specific metadata field typed via KindMetadata<K>. KindRegistry.getInstance() provides singleton with validators for each kind. Each validator enforces kind-specific rules and metadata schema. file:kinds/types.ts (lines 14-32, 383-389), kinds/KindRegistry.ts (lines 29-58)
Edge Cases: OriginalKind.App requires runtime, entrypoint metadata; OriginalKind.Agent requires capabilities array, optional model info; OriginalKind.Module requires format, main file references; OriginalKind.Dataset requires schema, format, recordCount optional; OriginalKind.Media requires mediaType, mimeType, optional dimensions/duration; OriginalKind.Document requires format, content resource ID
Notes: Deps: KindRegistry singleton, KindValidator implementations (AppValidator, etc.), KindMetadataMap type mapping

User Story: As a developer, I want to subscribe to lifecycle events on LifecycleManager or OriginalsAsset, so that I can monitor and react to migrations, publications, inscriptions, and transfers
Expected Behaviour: LifecycleManager.on(eventType, handler) and OriginalsAsset.on(eventType, handler) subscribe to events. Batch operations emit 'batch:started', 'batch:completed', 'batch:failed' events. Single-asset operations emit 'asset:created', 'asset:migrated', 'asset:transferred', 'resource:published', 'credential:issued', 'resource:version:created'. file:LifecycleManager.ts (lines 150-171), events/types.ts (all event interfaces)
Edge Cases: Multiple handlers on same event - all called in sequence; Handler throws - EventEmitter catches, logs, continues with next handler; Batch operations emit per-item events from underlying single-asset operations; asset:created events deferred via queueMicrotask to allow subscriber attachment; asset:migrated emitted from Asset.migrate(), asset:transferred from Asset.recordTransfer()
Notes: Deps: EventEmitter, EventTypeMap, EventHandler type

Feature Name: Clean Lifecycle API with Progress Tracking
User Story: As a developer, I want a simple, high-level API for common workflows (createDraft, publish, inscribe, transfer) with progress callbacks, so that I can build intuitive UIs with real-time feedback
Expected Behaviour: LifecycleManager.createDraft(resources, options) wraps createAsset with progress phases. LifecycleManager.publish/inscribe/transfer wrap their operations with progress callbacks. onProgress callback receives LifecycleProgress with phase, percentage, message, and optional details. file:LifecycleManager.ts (lines 1043-1348)
Edge Cases: onProgress callback not provided - operations continue silently; Progress callback throws - operation not affected (fire-and-forget); Phase transitions: preparing -> validating -> processing -> committing -> confirming -> complete or failed; percentage ranges 0-100, indicates operation progress; details includes currentStep, totalSteps, transactionId, confirmations as applicable
Notes: Deps: LifecycleOperationOptions type, LifecycleProgress type, ProgressCallback type

Feature Name: Migration Events with Layer Transition Validation
User Story: As a developer, I want to monitor asset migrations between layers with validation events, so that I can ensure migrations succeed before subsequent operations
Expected Behaviour: Asset.migrate(toLayer, details) validates transition is allowed, records migration in provenance, updates currentLayer, emits 'asset:migrated' event with fromLayer, toLayer, and details (Bitcoin transaction info for did:btco). Transitions: did:peer->{did:webvh,did:btco}, did:webvh->{did:btco}, did:btco->{(none)}. file:OriginalsAsset.ts:migrate (lines 108-159)
Edge Cases: Invalid transition (e.g., did:btco -> did:peer) - throws error; Migration details may be undefined (for did:webvh) or include Bitcoin tx info (for did:btco); transactionId recorded in provenance.txid if provided; feeRate, commitTxId, revealTxId stored in migration record for auditing; Event emitted after layer updated
Notes: Deps: EventEmitter, ProvenanceChain structure

User Story: As a developer, I want to sign data using ECDSA with Secp256k1 curves, so that I can create cryptographically verifiable signatures compatible with Ethereum and Bitcoin ecosystems
Expected Behaviour: ES256KSigner.sign() accepts Buffer data and multibase-encoded Secp256k1 private key (with 'z' prefix and proper multicodec header 0x13 0x01), computes SHA-256 hash of data, signs with secp256k1, converts signature to Buffer, and returns it. ES256KSigner.verify() accepts Buffer data, signature Buffer, and multibase-encoded Secp256k1 public key, verifies signature against SHA-256 hash, returns true/false. Both methods throw Error if key format is invalid or key type mismatches. Verify catches all exceptions and returns false (Signer.ts:16-81)
Edge Cases: Verify silently catches secp256k1.verify() exceptions and returns false instead of throwing (lines 76-79); Sign handles multiple signature object formats: direct Uint8Array, toCompactRawBytes(), toRawBytes(), or fallback array conversion (lines 42-49); Keys must start with 'z' prefix; throws immediately if not (line 18); Invalid multibase encoding throws descriptive error wrapping decode failure (lines 26-30)
Notes: Deps: @noble/secp256k1 (signAsync, verify), @noble/hashes/sha2.js (sha256), multikey.decodePrivateKey / multikey.decodePublicKey

User Story: As a developer, I want to sign and verify data using Ed25519 EdDSA scheme, so that I can use fast, modern asymmetric cryptography for secure authentication
Expected Behaviour: Ed25519Signer.sign() accepts Buffer data and multibase-encoded Ed25519 private key (with 'z' prefix and multicodec header 0x80 0x26), signs using ed25519.signAsync(), returns Buffer. Ed25519Signer.verify() accepts Buffer data, signature, and multibase-encoded Ed25519 public key (header 0xed 0x01), calls ed25519.verifyAsync(), returns true/false. Both throw Error on invalid key format or type mismatch. Verify catches exceptions and returns false (Signer.ts:83-138)
Edge Cases: Verify silently catches ed25519.verifyAsync() exceptions and returns false (lines 131-136); Key format validation occurs in both sign and verify paths before cryptographic operations (lines 84-102, 110-128); Invalid multibase encoding is caught, wrapped with descriptive message (lines 89-98, 115-124)
Notes: Deps: @noble/ed25519 (signAsync, verifyAsync), multikey.decodePrivateKey / multikey.decodePublicKey, noble-init.ts initialization of ed25519.etc.sha512Sync

User Story: As a developer, I want to sign data using ECDSA with NIST P-256 curves, so that I can integrate with systems requiring NIST elliptic curve cryptography
Expected Behaviour: ES256Signer.sign() accepts Buffer data and multibase-encoded P-256 private key (header 0x81 0x26), hashes data with SHA-256, signs using p256.sign(), converts signature to Buffer (handling multiple object formats), returns Buffer. ES256Signer.verify() accepts Buffer data, signature, and multibase-encoded P-256 public key (header 0x80 0x24), verifies signature against SHA-256 hash using p256.verify(), returns true/false. Both throw Error on invalid key format or type mismatch. Verify catches exceptions and returns false (Signer.ts:140-205)
Edge Cases: Verify silently catches p256.verify() exceptions and returns false (lines 199-203); Sign handles multiple signature return types from p256.sign(): direct Uint8Array, toCompactRawBytes(), toRawBytes(), or fallback array conversion (lines 164-172); Key format validated before cryptographic operations to fail fast (lines 141-159)
Notes: Deps: @noble/curves/p256 (sign, verify, getPublicKey), @noble/hashes/sha2.js (sha256), multikey.decodePrivateKey / multikey.decodePublicKey

User Story: As a developer, I want to use BLS signatures for advanced cryptographic applications, so that I can leverage pairing-friendly curves for threshold signatures and aggregation
Expected Behaviour: Bls12381G2Signer.sign() accepts Buffer data and multibase-encoded BLS12-381 private key (header 0x82 0x26), signs using bls.sign(), converts to Buffer, returns it. Bls12381G2Signer.verify() accepts Buffer data, signature, and multibase-encoded BLS12-381 public key (header 0xeb 0x01), verifies using bls.verify(), returns true/false. Both throw Error on invalid key format or type mismatch. Verify catches exceptions and returns false (Signer.ts:207-260)
Edge Cases: Verify silently catches bls.verify() exceptions and returns false (lines 254-258); BLS public key is much larger (96 bytes) compared to other schemes (32-33 bytes) due to G2 point representation (Multikey.ts line 91)
Notes: Deps: @noble/curves/bls12-381 (sign, verify, getPublicKey), multikey.decodePrivateKey / multikey.decodePublicKey

User Story: As a developer, I want to encode and decode cryptographic keys in multikey format with multicodec headers, so that I can exchange keys in a standardized, self-describing format
Expected Behaviour: multikey.encodePublicKey() accepts Uint8Array key bytes and MultikeyType ('Ed25519'|'Secp256k1'|'Bls12381G2'|'P256'), prepends appropriate multicodec header (2 bytes) based on type, encodes concatenated bytes as z-base58btc (prefix 'z'), returns string. multikey.encodePrivateKey() does same with private key headers. multikey.decodePublicKey() accepts 'z'-prefixed string, base58 decodes, reads 2-byte header to determine type, extracts key bytes (remaining), returns {key: Uint8Array, type: MultikeyType}. multikey.decodePrivateKey() does same. multikey.encodeMultibase() encodes arbitrary Uint8Array|Buffer as z-base58btc (Multikey.ts:119-194)
Edge Cases: Decode functions throw 'Invalid Multibase encoding' if string doesn't start with 'z' before attempting base58 decode (lines 151, 173); Decode functions throw 'Unsupported key type' if 2-byte header doesn't match any known multicodec (lines 169, 191); Encode functions check type with nested ternaries (not exhaustive); P256 is default if other types don't match (lines 122-128, 135-141); Header comparison is byte-by-byte (header[0] && header[1] must both match) allowing no partial matches (lines 157-168, 179-190)
Notes: Deps: @scure/base (base58.encode, base58.decode)

User Story: As a developer, I want to validate that a key string conforms to multikey format before using it, so that I can catch format errors early with descriptive messages
Expected Behaviour: validateMultikeyFormat() accepts key string, expectedType MultikeyType, and isPrivate boolean flag. Validates key is non-empty string (throws if null/undefined/empty). Validates 'z' prefix (throws if not). Base58 decodes remainder and checks decoded length >= 2 bytes. Extracts first 2 bytes as multicodec header, validates header matches expected type and key class (private vs public). Extracts key material (bytes after header) and validates byte length matches expected: Ed25519 priv/pub 32, Secp256k1 priv 32/pub 33, P256 priv 32/pub 33, BLS12381G2 priv 32/pub 96 bytes. Throws descriptive Error on any validation failure; succeeds silently if valid. Re-throws errors starting with 'Invalid multibase key format' as-is; wraps other errors (Multikey.ts:29-117)
Edge Cases: Null/undefined keys fail at first check (line 35-37), giving generic error message; Base58 decode errors are caught and wrapped with context (lines 105-116); Header byte-by-byte comparison allows false positives only if both bytes happen to match wrong type—very low probability (line 73); Empty string passes initial null check but fails on 'z' prefix check (line 39-43); Validates key length after header; a key with correct header but wrong byte length is rejected (lines 86-104)
Notes: Deps: @scure/base (base58.decode), Error class (built-in)

User Story: As a library developer, I want to automatically initialize @noble/secp256k1 and @noble/ed25519 with required hash functions, so that users don't need to manually configure these libraries before crypto operations
Expected Behaviour: noble-init.ts imports secp256k1, ed25519, SHA algorithms, and HMAC. Exports initNobleCrypto() function that sets up hash utilities. Safely sets secp256k1.utils.hmacSha256Sync to HMAC-SHA256 implementation. Safely sets ed25519.etc.sha512Sync to SHA512 implementation. Uses safeSetProperty() helper to handle readonly properties gracefully (attempts direct assignment first, then Object.defineProperty, ignores errors if property already set correctly). Module auto-invokes initNobleCrypto() on import (line 137). This initialization is idempotent—safe to call/import multiple times (noble-init.ts:1-138)
Edge Cases: safeSetProperty() attempts direct assignment first, then defineProperty as fallback, returns false if both fail (lines 39-56); Bun runtime environment may have readonly properties that fail assignment—caught and handled by try-catch in defineProperty (lines 44-55); Libraries may already be initialized (utils/etc namespace exists); safeSetProperty() checks and skips if function already defined (lines 67, 84, 100, 121); Auto-initialization on module load (line 137) means crypto operations can start immediately after importing index.ts (line 3)
Notes: Deps: @noble/secp256k1 (utils namespace), @noble/ed25519 (etc and utils namespaces), @noble/hashes/sha2.js (sha256, sha512), @noble/hashes/hmac.js (hmac), @noble/hashes/utils.js (concatBytes)

User Story: As a developer, I want to store and retrieve arbitrary binary data in memory with domain/path isolation, so that I can test storage operations without file system dependencies
Expected Behaviour: MemoryStorageAdapter.putObject() accepts domain string, objectPath string, and content (Uint8Array|string). Normalizes path by stripping leading slashes. Converts string content to Uint8Array if needed. Stores in global Map keyed by 'domain::cleanPath'. Returns synchronously-resolved Promise<string> with 'mem://{domain}/{cleanPath}' URL. getObject() looks up domain::cleanPath in Map, returns Promise<{content: Uint8Array}|null> (null if not found, content omitted if missing). exists() returns Promise<boolean> checking Map.has(). All operations are synchronous wrapped in Promise for interface compatibility (MemoryStorageAdapter.ts:12-28)
Edge Cases: Path normalization strips ALL leading slashes, so '///path' becomes 'path' (line 9, 16); String content is encoded to Uint8Array via TextEncoder (line 14), preserving UTF-8; Global store is shared across all MemoryStorageAdapter instances—NOT isolated per instance (line 5); Storing content at same domain::path overwrites previous value without warning (line 15); getObject returns null for missing keys, NOT throwing error (line 21); GetObjectResult omits contentType field (never populated, always undefined) (line 22)
Notes: Deps: StorageAdapter interface (provides contract), TextEncoder (built-in, for string encoding)

User Story: As a developer, I want to store cryptographic keys and data on the local file system with domain-based directory isolation, so that I can persist data across process restarts
Expected Behaviour: LocalStorageAdapter constructor accepts LocalStorageAdapterOptions {baseDir, baseUrl?}. putObject() normalizes domain name (alphanumeric/dash/dot only, non-matching chars become '_'), normalizes objectPath (strip leading slashes), joins with baseDir using path.join() to create full file system path. Creates parent directories recursively via fs.mkdir(). Converts string content to Buffer. Writes file via fs.writeFile(). Returns URL via toUrl() helper (baseUrl if set, else file:// URI). getObject() reads file via fs.readFile(), catches ENOENT errors and returns null, throws on other errors, returns {content: Uint8Array}. exists() uses fs.access() and returns boolean (catches exceptions as false) (LocalStorageAdapter.ts:8-63)
Edge Cases: Domain sanitization replaces non-alphanumeric chars (except dash/dot) with '_', creating filesystem-safe names (line 18); Path traversal attack prevention: domain sanitization prevents '..' or '/' from domain names (line 18); Leading slashes are stripped from objectPath (line 19), but relative paths like '../' are not explicitly filtered; fs.readFile() catches only ENOENT (file not found); other errors (permission, I/O) bubble up and throw (line 49); baseUrl is optional; if not provided, falls back to file:// scheme with full resolved path (line 25-29); baseUrl is trimmed of trailing slashes before use (line 26); String content is converted to Buffer, then Buffer is written (line 35); getObject() returns Uint8Array (new Uint8Array(Buffer)), preserving byte identity (line 44)
Notes: Deps: fs/promises (mkdir, writeFile, readFile, access), path (join, dirname), StorageAdapter interface, Buffer, Uint8Array (Node.js built-ins)

User Story: As a framework developer, I want to define a pluggable interface for storage implementations, so that users can swap storage backends without changing application code
Expected Behaviour: StorageAdapter interface defines three methods: putObject(domain, path, content, options?) -> Promise<string> accepts domain, path, Uint8Array|string content, optional PutOptions {contentType?}, returns public URL. getObject(domain, path) -> Promise<GetObjectResult|null> returns {content: Uint8Array, contentType?: string} or null if not found. exists(domain, path) -> Promise<boolean> checks path existence. LocalStorageAdapterOptions {baseDir: string, baseUrl?: string} configure file adapter (StorageAdapter.ts:1-25)
Edge Cases: contentType is optional in both PutOptions and GetObjectResult; implementations may not populate it; getObject returns null for missing paths (not error), allowing graceful handling; putObject returns public URL (implementation-specific format); URLs are opaque to caller; Domain parameter semantics are implementation-dependent (directory, namespace, account, etc.)
Notes: Deps: TypeScript interface definition only (no runtime dependencies)

User Story: As a developer, I want to create and configure the Originals SDK with network, key type, and logging options so that I can use it for managing DIDs and assets.
Expected Behaviour: OriginalsSDK.constructor validates config object, network (mainnet/regtest/signet), and defaultKeyType (ES256K/Ed25519/ES256). Initializes Logger, MetricsCollector, EventLogger, and four managers: DIDManager, CredentialManager, LifecycleManager, BitcoinManager. OriginalsSDK.create() provides factory method with defaults. (packages/sdk/src/core/OriginalsSDK.ts:112-149, 183-192)
Edge Cases: Config object missing or not an object throws error; Invalid network value (not in allowed list) throws error; Invalid defaultKeyType throws error; BitcoinManager initialized conditionally if bitcoin features needed; Event logging setup depends on config.logging?.eventLogging presence
Notes: Deps: DIDManager, CredentialManager, LifecycleManager, BitcoinManager, Logger, MetricsCollector, EventLogger, didwebvh-ts (dynamic import for DID creation)

User Story: As a developer integrating Bitcoin operations, I want the SDK to validate that ordinalsProvider is configured so that I get early failure rather than runtime errors during inscriptions.
Expected Behaviour: OriginalsSDK.validateBitcoinConfig() checks if config.ordinalsProvider is set. Throws StructuredError with code 'ORD_PROVIDER_REQUIRED' and helpful message if not configured. (packages/sdk/src/core/OriginalsSDK.ts:172-181)
Edge Cases: ordinalsProvider is undefined or null; Config exists but ordinalsProvider field is missing entirely
Notes: Deps: StructuredError utility

User Story: As a developer, I want to create DID-based Originals using the SDK's static method so that I don't need to manage didwebvh-ts imports and can get a unified result object.
Expected Behaviour: OriginalsSDK.createDIDOriginal(options) dynamically imports didwebvh-ts, calls createDID with mapped options (domain, signer, verifier, updateKeys, verificationMethods, context defaults to W3C/multikey), returns OriginalResult with did, doc, log, meta.
