OriginalResult.context defaults to ['https://www.w3.org/ns/did/v1', 'https://w3id.org/security/multikey/v1'] if not provided. (packages/sdk/src/core/OriginalsSDK.ts:287-326)
Edge Cases: didwebvh-ts module fails to import dynamically; createDID function not present on imported module; signer parameter missing (required by didwebvh-ts); updateKeys and verificationMethods formats incompatible with didwebvh-ts; Custom context provided but conflicts with defaults
Notes: Deps: didwebvh-ts library (dynamic import), ExternalSigner interface, ExternalVerifier interface (optional), VerificationMethod type, ServiceEndpoint type

User Story: As a developer, I want to update existing DID documents with optional field updates so that I can modify only the fields I need without rewriting the entire DID.
Expected Behaviour: OriginalsSDK.updateDIDOriginal(options) validates required fields (log, signer), constructs updateOptions object conditionally adding only defined optional fields (updateKeys, verificationMethods, services, controller, context, alsoKnownAs, portable, nextKeyHashes, authentication, assertionMethod, keyAgreement, domain). Uses verifier from options or defaults to signer. Calls updateDID on imported module. Extracts targetDid from result or reconstructs from log. Returns OriginalResult. (packages/sdk/src/core/OriginalsSDK.ts:357-417)
Edge Cases: log parameter is empty array; signer provided but verifier not provided (falls back to signer); updateDID returns result without did field; must extract from log; log array has entries but latest entry lacks id field; Partial options cause incomplete DID document
Notes: Deps: didwebvh-ts (dynamic import for updateDID), UpdateDIDOriginalOptions interface, DIDDocument type

User Story: As a developer, I want a static helper to prepare DID data for signing using didwebvh-ts's canonical format so that I can sign DIDs consistently across the SDK.
Expected Behaviour: OriginalsSDK.prepareDIDDataForSigning(document, proof) dynamically imports didwebvh-ts, calls prepareDataForSigning(document, proof), returns Uint8Array of canonical bytes. Runtime check ensures prepareDataForSigning is a function before calling. (packages/sdk/src/core/OriginalsSDK.ts:199-219)
Edge Cases: didwebvh-ts module fails to import; prepareDataForSigning not exported or not a function; document or proof parameters have circular references; document/proof are null or undefined
Notes: Deps: didwebvh-ts (dynamic import)

User Story: As a developer, I want to verify Ed25519 signatures in a browser-compatible way so that I can validate DID signatures without requiring Node.js Buffer.
Expected Behaviour: OriginalsSDK.verifyDIDSignature(signature, message, publicKey) accepts Uint8Array inputs, handles Ed25519 key length validation (32 or 33 bytes, strips prefix if 33), dynamically imports @noble/ed25519, calls verifyAsync with Uint8Array directly. Returns boolean; returns false on any verification error. (packages/sdk/src/core/OriginalsSDK.ts:231-256)
Edge Cases: publicKey length is 33 bytes (has version prefix) - automatically strips to 32 bytes; publicKey length is not 32 or 33 bytes - throws error; Verification fails - returns false instead of throwing; @noble/ed25519 module fails to import; verifyAsync throws exception during verification
Notes: Deps: @noble/ed25519 library (dynamic import), Browser-compatible Uint8Array API

User Story: As a developer, I want to call createOriginal() with a type-discriminated options object so that I can create any Original type with a unified API.
Expected Behaviour: OriginalsSDK.createOriginal(options) uses options.type discriminator to route to appropriate handler. Currently only 'did' type is supported, routed to createDIDOriginal(). Throws error if type is unsupported. (packages/sdk/src/core/OriginalsSDK.ts:266-275)
Edge Cases: options.type is missing or falsy; options.type is not a recognized string; Future: additional Original types added without updating switch statement
Notes: Deps: CreateOriginalOptions union type, OriginalResult interface

User Story: As a developer, I want to call updateOriginal() with a type-discriminated options object so that I can update any Original type with a unified API.
Expected Behaviour: OriginalsSDK.updateOriginal(options) uses options.type discriminator to route to appropriate handler. Currently only 'did' type is supported, routed to updateDIDOriginal(). TypeScript exhaustiveness check via 'never' type ensures all cases handled. (packages/sdk/src/core/OriginalsSDK.ts:336-348)
Edge Cases: options.type is missing or falsy; options.type is not a recognized string; New type added but switch statement not updated (caught by TypeScript)
Notes: Deps: UpdateOriginalOptions union type, OriginalResult interface

User Story: As a developer, I want to emit and subscribe to type-safe events so that I can track asset lifecycle operations with proper TypeScript support.
Expected Behaviour: EventEmitter provides on<K>(eventType, handler) and once<K>(eventType, handler) methods accepting only valid event types from EventTypeMap. emit(event) broadcasts to all subscribed handlers (sync and async), isolating errors in individual handlers. Returns unsubscribe function from on/once. (packages/sdk/src/events/EventEmitter.ts:38-83)
Edge Cases: Multiple handlers subscribed to same event - all called in order; Handler throws error - error logged but doesn't affect other handlers; off() called with handler not currently subscribed; once() handler throws - handler still removed from list; Handlers collection modified during emit - uses array snapshot to avoid issues
Notes: Deps: EventTypeMap interface, EventHandler<T> type, OriginalsEvent union type

User Story: As a developer, I want to manage event listeners with subscribe/unsubscribe functions and bulk removal so that I can control memory and event handling cleanly.
Expected Behaviour: EventEmitter.on() and once() return unsubscribe function. off(eventType, handler) removes specific handler. removeAllListeners() clears all handlers for optional eventType or all events. listenerCount(eventType) returns subscription count. hasListeners(eventType) returns boolean. (packages/sdk/src/events/EventEmitter.ts:49, 77-82, 98-111, 191-221)
Edge Cases: Unsubscribe function called multiple times - idempotent, safe; removeAllListeners() called with no arguments - clears everything; listenerCount() called for event with no subscribers - returns 0; off() called for event type with no handlers - no-op
Notes: Deps: EventTypeMap, Set<EventHandler> internal storage

User Story: As a developer, I want to receive events for asset creation, migration, transfer, and verification so that I can track asset lifecycle in real-time.
Expected Behaviour: EventEmitter emits typed events: AssetCreatedEvent (type, timestamp, asset.id/layer/resourceCount/createdAt), AssetMigratedEvent (type, timestamp, asset, fromLayer, toLayer, details with transaction data for btco), AssetTransferredEvent (from/to/transactionId), VerificationCompletedEvent (result, checks). All events include type and ISO 8601 timestamp. (packages/sdk/src/events/types.ts:23-136)
Edge Cases: AssetMigratedEvent.details is undefined for did:webvh migrations (web has no tx); AssetMigratedEvent.details includes transactionId, inscriptionId, satoshi, commitTxId, revealTxId, feeRate for did:btco migrations; resourceCount can be 0 for empty assets; VerificationCompletedEvent.checks field is optional
Notes: Deps: LayerType enum, BaseEvent interface

User Story: As a developer, I want to receive events for credential issuance, resource versioning, and batch operations so that I can track multi-step operations.
Expected Behaviour: EventEmitter emits CredentialIssuedEvent (credential.type[], issuer), ResourceVersionCreatedEvent (fromVersion/toVersion, hashes, optional changes), ResourcePublishedEvent (url, contentType, hash, publisherDid, domain). Batch events: BatchStartedEvent, BatchCompletedEvent (results with successful/failed counts), BatchFailedEvent, BatchProgressEvent. (packages/sdk/src/events/types.ts:93-276)
Edge Cases: credential.type is array of strings (e.g., ['VerifiableCredential', 'AssetCredential']); changes field optional in ResourceVersionCreatedEvent; costSavings optional in BatchCompletedEvent.results; partialResults optional in BatchFailedEvent
Notes: Deps: BaseEvent interface

User Story: As a developer, I want to receive events for migration start, validation, checkpoint, anchoring, completion, and failure so that I can monitor migration progress and react to state changes.
Expected Behaviour: EventEmitter emits MigrationStartedEvent (migrationId, sourceDid, targetLayer), MigrationValidatedEvent (valid boolean), MigrationCheckpointedEvent (checkpointId), MigrationInProgressEvent (currentOperation, progress 0-100), MigrationAnchoringEvent (transactionId for btco), MigrationCompletedEvent (sourceDid, targetDid), MigrationFailedEvent (error object), MigrationRolledbackEvent (checkpointId), MigrationQuarantineEvent (reason). All timestamped. (packages/sdk/src/events/types.ts:183-301)
Edge Cases: MigrationAnchoringEvent.transactionId optional (not all migrations anchor to Bitcoin); MigrationFailedEvent.error can be Error object or {message, code?} object; MigrationInProgressEvent.progress ranges 0-100 at different stages; MigrationQuarantineEvent indicates rollback failed, manual intervention needed
Notes: Deps: BaseEvent interface, MigrationStateEnum

User Story: As a developer, I want to access the MigrationManager singleton so that I can perform DID layer migrations with orchestrated validation, checkpointing, and rollback.
Expected Behaviour: MigrationManager.getInstance(config, didManager, credentialManager, bitcoinManager?) returns singleton instance. First call requires config and managers; subsequent calls return same instance. resetInstance() clears singleton (testing). Constructor initializes ValidationPipeline, CheckpointManager, RollbackManager, StateTracker, AuditLogger, three migration operation classes. (packages/sdk/src/migration/MigrationManager.ts:31-98, 30-80)
Edge Cases: getInstance() called without required managers on first call - throws error; bitcoinManager undefined - btco migration operations set to null stubs; bitcoinManager provided on subsequent getInstance() calls - ignored (singleton pattern); resetInstance() called to clear singleton for testing
Notes: Deps: OriginalsConfig, DIDManager, CredentialManager, BitcoinManager (optional), ValidationPipeline, CheckpointManager, RollbackManager, StateTracker, AuditLogger, EventEmitter

Feature Name: Migration Execution with Validation and Checkpoints
User Story: As a developer, I want to migrate a DID from one layer to another with automatic validation, checkpoint creation, and rollback on failure so that migrations are safe and recoverable.
Expected Behaviour: MigrationManager.migrate(options) executes 6-step process: (1) Create migration state via StateTracker (emits migration:started), (2) Validate via ValidationPipeline (validates DID, credentials, storage, lifecycle, bitcoin), transitions state to VALIDATING (emits migration:validated), (3) Create checkpoint via CheckpointManager (emits migration:checkpointed), (4) Execute migration operation (peer->webvh/webvh->btco/peer->btco), (5) Transition to COMPLETED, emit migration:completed, (6) Create signed audit record via AuditLogger. Returns MigrationResult with migrationId, success, sourceDid, targetDid, state, duration, cost, auditRecord. On failure, attempts rollback and returns failed result. (packages/sdk/src/migration/MigrationManager.ts:110-245)
Edge Cases: Validation fails - throws MigrationError, transitions to FAILED without executing; Checkpoint creation fails - throws error; Migration execution fails - transitions to FAILED, attempts rollback, emits migration:failed; Rollback succeeds - final state is ROLLED_BACK; Rollback fails - final state is QUARANTINED (manual intervention needed), emits migration:quarantine; Checkpoint auto-deleted after 24 hours on success
Notes: Deps: MigrationOptions interface, StateTracker (create and update migration state), ValidationPipeline (run all validators), CheckpointManager (save state before execution), Migration operation classes (PeerToWebvhMigration, etc.), RollbackManager (rollback on failure), AuditLogger (log signed audit record), EventEmitter

User Story: As a developer, I want to estimate migration costs before executing so that I can inform users of fees without committing to the operation.
Expected Behaviour: MigrationManager.estimateMigrationCost(sourceDid, targetLayer, feeRate?) creates MigrationOptions with estimateCostOnly=true, runs ValidationPipeline.validate(), returns estimated CostEstimate (storageCost, networkFees, totalCost, estimatedDuration). No state created, no migration executed. (packages/sdk/src/migration/MigrationManager.ts:251-262)
Edge Cases: BitcoinValidator requires ordinalsProvider to estimate fees for btco; feeRate parameter overrides default for Bitcoin network fee calculation; Validation fails - errors returned in validation result, not thrown
Notes: Deps: ValidationPipeline, CostEstimate interface, MigrationOptions interface

User Story: As a developer, I want to query the current state of an in-progress migration so that I can display progress to users or wait for completion.
Expected Behaviour: MigrationManager.getMigrationStatus(migrationId) returns MigrationState object (state, sourceDid, targetDid, progress 0-100, currentOperation, startTime, endTime, error). Returns null if migration not found. Does not modify state. (packages/sdk/src/migration/MigrationManager.ts:268-270)
Edge Cases: migrationId doesn't exist - returns null; Migration in FAILED state - state includes error object; Migration in QUARANTINED state - state indicates manual intervention needed
Notes: Deps: StateTracker, MigrationState interface

User Story: As a developer, I want to rollback a failed migration to restore the source DID state so that users can retry or take other actions.
Expected Behaviour: MigrationManager.rollback(migrationId) retrieves migration state, verifies checkpointId exists, calls RollbackManager.rollback(). On success, emits migration:rolledback event and returns RollbackResult (success=true, restoredState=ROLLED_BACK). On failure, emits migration:quarantine event and returns success=false, restoredState=QUARANTINED. (packages/sdk/src/migration/MigrationManager.ts:276-287)
Edge Cases: Migration not found - throws error; Migration has no checkpoint - throws error; Checkpoint not found during rollback - returns success=false, state=QUARANTINED; Checkpoint belongs to different migration - returns success=false, state=QUARANTINED; Bitcoin transaction cannot be reversed (but source DID restored)
Notes: Deps: RollbackManager, CheckpointManager, StateTracker, EventEmitter

User Story: As a developer, I want to retrieve signed audit records of migrations for a DID so that I can track lineage and verify integrity.
Expected Behaviour: MigrationManager.getMigrationHistory(did) calls AuditLogger.getMigrationHistory(did), returns array of MigrationAuditRecord sorted by timestamp. Records include signature (Ed25519 multibase if signer configured, else SHA-256 hash) and are queryable via getSystemMigrationLogs(filters). (packages/sdk/src/migration/MigrationManager.ts:294-296)
Edge Cases: DID has no migration history - returns empty array; Records signed with Ed25519 (base58btc multibase, 'z' prefix); Records signed keyless SHA-256 if no signer configured; Multiple records for same DID if migrated multiple times
Notes: Deps: AuditLogger, MigrationAuditRecord interface

User Story: As a developer, I want to migrate multiple DIDs in a batch operation so that I can manage large-scale migrations with progress tracking and error handling.
Expected Behaviour: MigrationManager.migrateBatch(dids, targetLayer, options) iterates over DIDs, calls migrate() for each, collects results in Map<sourceDid, MigrationResult>. Returns BatchMigrationResult with batchId, total/completed/failed/inProgress counts, overallProgress (0-100), startTime, errors array. Continues on error if options.continueOnError=true, else breaks on first failure. maxConcurrent option available for parallel execution. (packages/sdk/src/migration/MigrationManager.ts:301-354)
Edge Cases: Empty dids array - returns result with total=0, completed=0, failed=0; continueOnError=false - batch stops on first error; continueOnError=true - batch continues, collects errors in result.errors; maxConcurrent not implemented yet (placeholder for future); Batch processing sequential (not parallel currently)
Notes: Deps: MigrationOptions interface, BatchMigrationResult interface, MigrationResult interface

User Story: As a developer, I want to ensure migrations only transition through valid states so that the system remains consistent and prevents invalid state sequences.
Expected Behaviour: StateMachine defines valid state transitions: PENDING->(VALIDATING|FAILED), VALIDATING->(CHECKPOINTED|FAILED), CHECKPOINTED->(IN_PROGRESS|FAILED), IN_PROGRESS->(ANCHORING|COMPLETED|FAILED), ANCHORING->(COMPLETED|FAILED), FAILED->(ROLLED_BACK|QUARANTINED), COMPLETED/ROLLED_BACK/QUARANTINED are terminal. canTransition(from, to) checks validity. getValidTransitions(from) returns copy of allowed states. isTerminalState(state) returns true if no further transitions. (packages/sdk/src/migration/state/StateMachine.ts:7-92)
Edge Cases: Unknown state passed - returns empty array for valid transitions; Terminal state queried - returns empty array; Copy returned from getValidTransitions - external mutations don't affect internal map
Notes: Deps: MigrationStateEnum

User Story: As a developer, I want the system to automatically create checkpoints before migrations so that failed migrations can be rolled back to a known good state.
Expected Behaviour: CheckpointManager.createCheckpoint(migrationId, options) generates checkpointId (chk_{uuid}), resolves source DID via DIDManager, extracts layer, creates MigrationCheckpoint object with DID document, empty credentials/storageReferences/lifecycleState/ownershipProofs arrays (placeholders), stores via CheckpointStorage. Returns checkpoint or throws error. (packages/sdk/src/migration/checkpoint/CheckpointManager.ts:30-68)
Edge Cases: Source DID cannot be resolved - throws error; Invalid source DID format - throws error; Checkpoint storage fails - throws error with wrapped message; Optional metadata included in checkpoint
Notes: Deps: CheckpointStorage, DIDManager, MigrationCheckpoint interface

User Story: As a developer, I want to retrieve checkpoints during rollback or delete them after successful migrations so that storage is managed efficiently.
Expected Behaviour: CheckpointManager.getCheckpoint(checkpointId) returns MigrationCheckpoint from storage or null if not found. deleteCheckpoint(checkpointId) removes from storage silently (errors logged, not thrown). cleanupOldCheckpoints() deletes checkpoints older than 24 hours. (packages/sdk/src/migration/checkpoint/CheckpointManager.ts:73-104)
Edge Cases: getCheckpoint for non-existent ID - returns null, logs error; deleteCheckpoint fails - errors logged but function succeeds (idempotent); cleanupOldCheckpoints called with no old checkpoints - no-op; Checkpoint timestamp vs current time for age calculation
Notes: Deps: CheckpointStorage, MigrationCheckpoint interface

User Story: As a developer, I want the system to safely rollback migrations based on the source layer so that the source DID and any artifacts are properly cleaned up.
Expected Behaviour: RollbackManager.rollback(migrationId, checkpointId) retrieves checkpoint, verifies it matches migrationId, calls performLayerSpecificRollback (verifies source DID still resolves, layer-specific cleanup for peer->webvh removes published resources), cleanupMigrationArtifacts. Returns RollbackResult (success, migrationId, checkpointId, restoredState, duration, errors). (packages/sdk/src/migration/rollback/RollbackManager.ts:26-122)
Edge Cases: Checkpoint not found - returns success=false, restoredState=QUARANTINED; Checkpoint ID mismatch - returns success=false, restoredState=QUARANTINED; Source DID cannot be resolved - throws error, results in QUARANTINED state; Bitcoin transactions cannot be reversed (rollback notes this); Layer-specific cleanup for peer->webvh (remove published resources)
Notes: Deps: CheckpointManager, DIDManager, RollbackResult interface, MigrationError interface

User Story: As a developer, I want to check if a migration can be rolled back so that I can inform users of recovery options.
Expected Behaviour: RollbackManager.canRollback(migrationId, checkpointId) checks if checkpoint exists and belongs to migration. Returns true if checkpoint valid (even for Bitcoin - source DID can be restored), false if checkpoint not found or mismatch. (packages/sdk/src/migration/rollback/RollbackManager.ts:156-170)
Edge Cases: Checkpoint not found - returns false; Checkpoint ID mismatch - returns false; Any error during check - returns false; Bitcoin migrations always technically rollback-able (source restored)
Notes: Deps: CheckpointManager

User Story: As a developer, I want migration audit records to be cryptographically signed so that I can verify integrity and authenticity of migration history.
Expected Behaviour: AuditLogger.logMigration(record) calls signAuditRecord(). If AuditSignerConfig provided: generates canonical JSON (record without signature), signs with Ed25519, encodes as multibase (base58btc, 'z' prefix). If no signer: computes SHA-256 hash of canonical JSON, encodes as base64url multibase. Stores signed record in memory (and optional storage adapter). (packages/sdk/src/migration/audit/AuditLogger.ts:35-54, 110-124)
Edge Cases: No AuditSignerConfig provided - uses keyless SHA-256 fallback; Storage adapter not configured - records in memory only; Storage adapter.put fails - error logged, in-memory record persists; Circular references in record - JSON.stringify may fail
Notes: Deps: AuditSignerConfig interface (optional), @noble/ed25519 (if signer configured), @noble/hashes/sha2, encoding utilities (encodeBase64UrlMultibase, base58)

User Story: As a developer, I want to retrieve audit records for a DID to trace its migration history so that I can verify the chain of migrations.
Expected Behaviour: AuditLogger.getMigrationHistory(did) returns array of MigrationAuditRecord for sourceDid or targetDid. getSystemMigrationLogs(filters) returns deduplicated array of all records matching filter criteria (by signature to preserve timeline). Deduplication uses signature as key. (packages/sdk/src/migration/audit/AuditLogger.ts:59-91)
Edge Cases: DID has no history - returns empty array; Records stored by both sourceDid and targetDid keys; Deduplication across source/target - uses signature as dedupe key; Records with same migrationId/timestamp but different state - kept separate
Notes: Deps: MigrationAuditRecord interface

User Story: As a developer, I want to verify audit record signatures so that I can ensure records haven't been tampered with.
Expected Behaviour: AuditLogger.verifyAuditRecord(record) extracts canonical JSON (record without signature), verifies signature field matches. If Ed25519 signer configured: decodes base58btc multibase, calls ed25519.verifyAsync(). If keyless: recomputes SHA-256, compares to signature. Returns boolean. (packages/sdk/src/migration/audit/AuditLogger.ts:129-152)
Edge Cases: record.signature missing - returns false; Base58 decode fails - returns false; ed25519.verifyAsync throws - returns false; Canonical JSON formatting matters (must match signing process)
Notes: Deps: @noble/ed25519 (if signer configured), @noble/hashes/sha2, base58 codec

User Story: As a developer, I want audit records persisted to storage so that migration history survives application restarts.
Expected Behaviour: AuditLogger.persistAuditRecord(record) checks if storageAdapter configured. If available: generates unique key audit/migrations/{migrationId}/{timestamp}-{finalState}.json, serializes record as JSON, calls storageAdapter.put(). Errors logged but don't throw (in-memory record still available). AuditLogger.loadAuditRecords(did) lists audit/migrations/, loads matching records into in-memory store. (packages/sdk/src/migration/audit/AuditLogger.ts:158-213)
Edge Cases: storageAdapter not configured - records in-memory only; storageAdapter.put fails - error logged, in-memory persists; Key collision prevented by unique naming (migrationId/timestamp/state); Invalid JSON in storage skipped on load; Loading deduplicates by signature to preserve timeline
Notes: Deps: storageAdapter (optional, with put/list/get methods)

User Story: As a developer, I want the system to run all required validators before migrations so that invalid states are detected early and users get clear error messages.
Expected Behaviour: ValidationPipeline.validate(options) runs sequential validators: DIDCompatibilityValidator, CredentialValidator (if credentialIssuance!==false), StorageValidator, LifecycleValidator, BitcoinValidator (if targetLayer==='btco'). Each returns MigrationValidationResult. Collects all errors/warnings. Returns combined result with valid=true only if no errors. Returns cost estimate and duration. (packages/sdk/src/migration/validation/ValidationPipeline.ts:49-176)
Edge Cases: Early exit if input validation fails (sourceDid or targetLayer invalid); Individual validator missing - adds error but continues; credentialIssuance=false skips credential validator; btco migrations require bitcoin validator (adds error if missing); All validators run regardless of partial failures
Notes: Deps: DIDCompatibilityValidator, CredentialValidator, StorageValidator, LifecycleValidator, BitcoinValidator, DIDManager, CredentialManager, BitcoinManager

User Story: As a developer, I want to quickly validate migration options before running async validators so that I can fail fast on obvious errors.
Expected Behaviour: ValidationPipeline.validateQuick(options) runs only input validation (no async calls). Checks sourceDid format, targetLayer in ['peer','webvh','btco'], domain required for webvh, feeRate validation for btco, partialMode.chunkSize validation. Returns array of ValidationError. (packages/sdk/src/migration/validation/ValidationPipeline.ts:246-248, 181-241)
Edge Cases: sourceDid is empty string - error; targetLayer not in allowed list - error with details; feeRate negative - error; domain missing for webvh - error; Multiple errors collected in array
Notes: Deps: ValidationError interface

User Story: As a developer, I want to validate that source DIDs are compatible with target layers so that migrations don't fail mid-process.
Expected Behaviour: DIDCompatibilityValidator.validate(options) resolves sourceDid via DIDManager, validates DID format, checks migration path (peer->webvh->btco, no reverse), validates verification methods (Multikey, Ed25519VerificationKey2020, EcdsaSecp256k1VerificationKey2019), warns if service endpoints on peer layer. Returns MigrationValidationResult with ~100ms estimated duration. (packages/sdk/src/migration/validation/DIDCompatibilityValidator.ts:21-89)
Edge Cases: Source DID cannot be resolved - error SOURCE_DID_NOT_FOUND; Invalid DID format - error INVALID_SOURCE_DID_FORMAT; Reverse migration (webvh->peer) - error INVALID_MIGRATION_PATH; Unknown verification method type - warning only (non-blocking); Service endpoints on peer - warning only
Notes: Deps: DIDManager, MigrationValidationResult interface

User Story: As a developer, I want credential validation to ensure credentials are compatible with migrations so that re-issuance succeeds.
Expected Behaviour: CredentialValidator.validate(options) checks if credentialIssuance=false, skips validation if true. Otherwise returns valid=true (all credential types compatible with all layers). Placeholder for future schema validation. Returns ~50ms estimated duration. (packages/sdk/src/migration/validation/CredentialValidator.ts:21-46)
Edge Cases: credentialIssuance explicitly false - skips validation, returns valid=true; credentialIssuance undefined/true - runs validation (currently always passes)
Notes: Deps: CredentialManager, MigrationValidationResult interface

User Story: As a developer, I want the system to validate storage adapter compatibility and warn about missing chunked upload support so that migrations with large files don't fail.
Expected Behaviour: StorageValidator.validate(options) checks if storageAdapter configured (warning if missing). For partialMode, warns if storageAdapter doesn't support putChunk(). Estimates storage cost: 0.001 sats for webvh, 0 for others. Returns ~50ms estimated duration. (packages/sdk/src/migration/validation/StorageValidator.ts:17-79)
Edge Cases: storageAdapter missing - warning NO_STORAGE_ADAPTER; partialMode specified but putChunk not supported - warning NO_CHUNKED_UPLOAD_SUPPORT; Storage cost varies by target layer
Notes: Deps: OriginalsConfig.storageAdapter, MigrationValidationResult interface

User Story: As a developer, I want the system to validate lifecycle state compatibility so that migrations don't leave assets in inconsistent states.
Expected Behaviour: LifecycleValidator.validate(options) currently returns valid=true (placeholder). Future implementation checks: pending operations on source DID, state machine compatibility, event history preservation capability. Returns ~30ms estimated duration. (packages/sdk/src/migration/validation/LifecycleValidator.ts:17-64)
Edge Cases: Currently always passes (no validation logic implemented); Placeholder for future lifecycle state machine checks
Notes: Deps: OriginalsConfig, MigrationValidationResult interface

User Story: As a developer, I want the system to validate Bitcoin requirements before btco migrations so that network fees are estimated and provider configuration verified.
Expected Behaviour: BitcoinValidator.validate(options) skips for non-btco layers. For btco: checks ordinalsProvider configured (error if missing), estimates fee (estimatedSize=1024 bytes * feeRate, default 10 sat/vB). Warns if feeRate > 1000 sat/vB or network mismatch. Returns ~10 min (600000ms) estimated duration. (packages/sdk/src/migration/validation/BitcoinValidator.ts:21-106)
Edge Cases: ordinalsProvider not configured - error BITCOIN_PROVIDER_REQUIRED; Fee estimation fails - warning, defaults to 10240 sats fallback; High fee rate (>1000) - warning only; Network not mainnet/signet - warning about signet use
Notes: Deps: BitcoinManager, OriginalsConfig.ordinalsProvider, MigrationValidationResult interface

User Story: As a developer, I want the system to track migration state throughout execution so that I can query progress and understand where failures occur.
Expected Behaviour: StateTracker.createMigration(options) generates migrationId (mig_{uuid}), extracts sourceLayer, creates MigrationState (state=PENDING, progress=0, startTime=now). updateState(migrationId, updates) validates state transitions via StateMachine, merges updates, sets endTime for terminal states. getState(migrationId) returns state or null. queryStates(filters) returns array matching all filter criteria. (packages/sdk/src/migration/state/StateTracker.ts:27-116)
Edge Cases: Invalid DID format - throws error; State transition invalid - throws error; Terminal states (COMPLETED/FAILED/ROLLED_BACK/QUARANTINED) auto-set endTime; Query with empty filters - returns all states; queryStates matching only some fields
Notes: Deps: StateMachine, MigrationState interface, MigrationStateEnum

User Story: As a developer, I want old migration states cleaned up automatically so that the state tracker doesn't grow unbounded.
Expected Behaviour: StateTracker.cleanupOldStates(olderThanMs) deletes states completed/rolled_back with endTime older than (now - olderThanMs). Default 7 days. getActiveMigrations() returns states with state=IN_PROGRESS. (packages/sdk/src/migration/state/StateTracker.ts:121-148)
Edge Cases: olderThanMs not provided - uses default 7 days (7*24*60*60*1000 ms); No old states to clean - no-op; Only COMPLETED and ROLLED_BACK cleaned; FAILED and QUARANTINED retained
Notes: Deps: MigrationStateEnum, MigrationState interface

User Story: As a developer, I want to migrate a peer DID to webvh layer so that the DID becomes web-resolvable and achieves higher layer stability.
Expected Behaviour: PeerToWebvhMigration.executeMigration(options, migrationId) resolves sourceDid, validates domain provided, transitions state to IN_PROGRESS (progress 30%), calls didManager.migrateToDIDWebVH(sourceDid, domain), updates progress to 100%, returns targetDid and didDocument. (packages/sdk/src/migration/operations/PeerToWebvhMigration.ts:17-48)
Edge Cases: domain not provided - throws error; Source DID cannot be resolved - throws error; migrateToDIDWebVH fails - error propagates, state updated to FAILED
Notes: Deps: DIDManager.migrateToDIDWebVH, BaseMigration, StateTracker

User Story: As a developer, I want to migrate a webvh DID to Bitcoin (btco) layer so that the DID is anchored to an immutable ledger.
Expected Behaviour: WebvhToBtcoMigration.executeMigration(options, migrationId) resolves sourceDid, creates manifest (didDocument, migrationId, timestamp), serializes as JSON Buffer. Transitions to IN_PROGRESS (progress 30%), calls bitcoinManager.inscribeData(payload, application/json, feeRate). Transitions to ANCHORING (progress 50%). Extracts satoshiId from inscription.satoshi or inscriptionId. Transitions to IN_PROGRESS (progress 80%), calls didManager.migrateToDIDBTCO(sourceDid, satoshiId). Returns targetDid. (packages/sdk/src/migration/operations/WebvhToBtcoMigration.ts:34-88)
Edge Cases: inscribeData fails - error propagates; satoshiId extraction (satoshi field or first part of inscriptionId before 'i'); migrateToDIDBTCO fails after Bitcoin inscription (irrecoverable)
Notes: Deps: BitcoinManager.inscribeData, DIDManager.migrateToDIDBTCO, BaseMigration, StateTracker

Feature Name: Peer to Bitcoin Direct Migration Execution
User Story: As a developer, I want to migrate a peer DID directly to Bitcoin (skipping webvh) so that peer DIDs can achieve blockchain anchoring without intermediate layer.
Expected Behaviour: PeerToBtcoMigration.executeMigration(options, migrationId) identical to WebvhToBtcoMigration but source is peer DID. Resolves peer DID, creates manifest, inscribes to Bitcoin, extracts satoshiId, calls migrateToDIDBTCO(). Returns targetDid. Progress: IN_PROGRESS(30%) -> ANCHORING(50%) -> IN_PROGRESS(80%) -> COMPLETED(100%). (packages/sdk/src/migration/operations/PeerToBtcoMigration.ts:34-88)
Edge Cases: Source DID is peer format; Bitcoin inscription same as webvh->btco path; Satoshi ID extraction same method
Notes: Deps: BitcoinManager.inscribeData, DIDManager.migrateToDIDBTCO, BaseMigration, StateTracker

User Story: As a developer, I want cost estimates per migration operation type so that users can understand fees upfront.
Expected Behaviour: PeerToWebvhMigration.estimateCost() returns {storageCost: 0, networkFees: 0, totalCost: 0, currency: 'sats'}. WebvhToBtcoMigration and PeerToBtcoMigration.estimateCost(options) calculate networkFees = 1024 bytes * feeRate (default 10), totalCost = networkFees. (packages/sdk/src/migration/operations/PeerToWebvhMigration.ts:53-61, WebvhToBtcoMigration.ts:93-104, PeerToBtcoMigration.ts:93-104)
Edge Cases: feeRate not provided - defaults to 10 sat/vB; estimatedSize fixed at 1024 bytes (typical inscription); peer->webvh always 0 cost (web hosting negligible)
Notes: Deps: CostEstimate interface, MigrationOptions interface

User Story: As a server developer, I want to issue cryptographically signed JWT tokens to authenticated users, so that I can maintain secure sessions and pass user context through API requests.
Expected Behaviour: signToken() accepts subOrgId, email, optional sessionToken and options. Validates subOrgId is provided (throws Error if missing). Creates payload with sub=subOrgId, email, optional sessionToken. Adds iat and exp claims. Signs with jsonwebtoken using secret from options or JWT_SECRET env var. Default expiration is 7 days (604800 seconds). Default issuer is 'originals-auth', default audience is 'originals-api'. Returns signed JWT string. See jwt.ts:31-64.
Edge Cases: Missing subOrgId throws validation error; Missing JWT_SECRET env var or config throws error; Custom expiresIn option overrides default 7-day expiration; sessionToken is optional and only included in payload if provided; Custom issuer/audience options override defaults
Notes: Deps: jsonwebtoken npm package

User Story: As a server developer, I want to verify and decode JWT tokens from client requests, so that I can validate user identity and extract authentication context.
Expected Behaviour: verifyToken() accepts token string and optional verification options. Calls jwt.verify() with secret from options or JWT_SECRET env var. Validates issuer (default 'originals-auth') and audience (default 'originals-api'). Returns TokenPayload with sub (subOrgId), email, optional sessionToken, iat, exp. Throws specific error messages: 'Token has expired' for TokenExpiredError, 'Invalid token' for JsonWebTokenError, 'Token missing sub-organization ID' if sub claim is missing. See jwt.ts:73-103.
Edge Cases: Expired token throws TokenExpiredError wrapped as 'Token has expired'; Invalid signature or malformed token throws JsonWebTokenError wrapped as 'Invalid token'; Missing sub claim in valid token throws validation error; Any other jwt error propagates as-is; Custom issuer/audience options override defaults for verification
Notes: Deps: jsonwebtoken npm package

User Story: As a server developer, I want to configure secure HTTP-only cookies for auth tokens, so that tokens are protected from XSS attacks and properly transmitted in requests.
Expected Behaviour: getAuthCookieConfig() accepts token string and optional options (cookieName, maxAge, secure). Returns AuthCookieConfig object with: name (default 'auth_token'), value (the token), options object with httpOnly=true, secure=true in production (NODE_ENV='production'), sameSite='strict', maxAge (default 7 days in milliseconds = 604800000), path='/'. See jwt.ts:111-132.
Edge Cases: NODE_ENV='production' automatically sets secure=true; non-production sets secure=false (can be overridden); Custom cookieName option overrides default 'auth_token'; Custom maxAge option overrides default 7 days; Custom secure option overrides environment-based default; httpOnly is always true for XSS protection; sameSite is always 'strict' for CSRF protection
Notes: Deps: NODE_ENV environment variable (production detection)

User Story: As a server developer, I want to create a logout cookie that clears the auth token, so that I can invalidate user sessions.
Expected Behaviour: getClearAuthCookieConfig() accepts optional cookieName. Returns AuthCookieConfig with: name (default 'auth_token'), empty value string, options object with httpOnly=true, secure=true in production, sameSite='strict', maxAge=0 (expires immediately), path='/'. See jwt.ts:139-153.
Edge Cases: maxAge is always 0 to expire cookie immediately; value is always empty string; NODE_ENV='production' sets secure=true; non-production sets secure=false; Custom cookieName parameter overrides default 'auth_token'
Notes: Deps: NODE_ENV environment variable

User Story: As a server developer, I want to automatically authenticate incoming requests using JWT cookies, so that protected routes can access user context without manual verification.
Expected Behaviour: createAuthMiddleware() accepts AuthMiddlewareOptions with: getUserByTurnkeyId (required async function), createUser (optional async function for auto-registration), cookieName (optional, default 'auth_token'), jwtSecret (optional, defaults to JWT_SECRET env var). Returns middleware function that: (1) extracts JWT from HTTP-only cookie, (2) verifies JWT token, (3) extracts turnkeySubOrgId and email from token payload, (4) looks up user via getUserByTurnkeyId, (5) if user not found and createUser provided, auto-creates user with temporary DID 'temp:turnkey:{subOrgId}', (6) attaches user object to req.user with id, turnkeySubOrgId, email, did, sessionToken, (7) calls next() on success or returns 401 with error message on failure. See middleware.ts:36-92.
Edge Cases: No cookie provided returns 401 'Not authenticated'; Invalid or expired token returns 401 'Invalid or expired token'; User not found and createUser not provided returns 401 'User not found'; User not found and createUser provided creates new user and continues; sessionToken from JWT payload is optional, attached to user object if present; Errors logged to console before returning 401
Notes: Deps: Express framework (Request, Response, NextFunction types), verifyToken() from jwt module, User lookup database or service

User Story: As a server developer, I want to optionally authenticate requests without failing if not authenticated, so that I can serve both authenticated and unauthenticated users.
Expected Behaviour: createOptionalAuthMiddleware() accepts same AuthMiddlewareOptions as required middleware. Returns middleware that: (1) attempts to extract and verify JWT from cookie, (2) if valid, looks up user and attaches to req.user, (3) if any error occurs (no token, invalid token, user not found), silently continues without user object, (4) always calls next() regardless. See middleware.ts:98-135.
Edge Cases: No cookie or empty token doesn't throw, continues with next(); Invalid token doesn't throw, continues with next(); Expired token doesn't throw, continues with next(); User lookup failure silently ignored, continues with next(); createUser is never called in optional middleware; No errors logged on auth failure (silent failure)
Notes: Deps: Express framework, verifyToken() from jwt module

User Story: As a server developer, I want to initiate email-based OTP authentication for users, so that users can log in securely via a code sent to their email.
Expected Behaviour: initiateEmailAuth() accepts email, Turnkey client, and optional SessionStorage. Validates email format with regex /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/. Calls getOrCreateTurnkeySubOrg() to get/create sub-org ID. Calls turnkeyClient.apiClient().initOtp() with otpType='OTP_TYPE_EMAIL', contact=email, userIdentifier=sha256(email) hex string, appName='Originals', otpLength=6, alphanumeric=false. Throws if no otpId returned. Generates unique sessionId. Stores EmailAuthSession in SessionStorage with email, subOrgId, otpId, timestamp, verified=false. Returns InitiateAuthResult with sessionId and message. Session timeout is 15 minutes. See email-auth.ts:79-142.
Edge Cases: Invalid email format throws 'Invalid email format' error; OTP API failure throws 'Failed to initiate OTP - no OTP ID returned'; SessionStorage uses in-memory Map by default with automatic cleanup every 60 seconds; Cleanup interval is unref() to not prevent process exit; User identifier is SHA256 hash of email for Turnkey rate limiting
Notes: Deps: Turnkey SDK (@turnkey/sdk-server), getOrCreateTurnkeySubOrg() function, SHA256 hashing (@noble/hashes/sha2.js), SessionStorage interface (pluggable, defaults to in-memory)

User Story: As a server developer, I want to verify the OTP code users received in their email, so that I can complete the authentication flow and confirm user identity.
Expected Behaviour: verifyEmailAuth() accepts sessionId, code (OTP string), Turnkey client, and optional SessionStorage. Retrieves session from storage, throws 'Invalid or expired session' if not found. Checks session.timestamp against 15-minute timeout, deletes and throws 'Session expired. Please request a new code.' if expired. Validates otpId and subOrgId are present in session. Calls turnkeyClient.apiClient().verifyOtp() with otpId, otpCode=code, expirationSeconds='900' (15 min), organizationId=subOrgId. Throws if no verificationToken returned. Marks session.verified=true and updates storage. Returns VerifyAuthResult with verified=true, email, subOrgId. Logs errors but wraps them in 'Invalid verification code: {error.message}'. See email-auth.ts:147-205.
Edge Cases: Session not found throws 'Invalid or expired session'; Session expired (timestamp > 15 min) deletes session and throws 'Session expired...'; Missing otpId or subOrgId in session throws descriptive error; Turnkey OTP verification failure throws wrapped error with original message; Code can be any string format (validation delegated to Turnkey API)
Notes: Deps: Turnkey SDK (@turnkey/sdk-server), SessionStorage interface

Feature Name: Email Auth Session Verification Status Check
User Story: As a server developer, I want to check if an email auth session has been verified, so that I can determine if a user can proceed to the next step.
Expected Behaviour: isSessionVerified() accepts sessionId and optional SessionStorage. Retrieves session from storage, returns false if not found. Checks if session.timestamp exceeds 15-minute timeout, deletes session and returns false if expired. Returns session.verified boolean. See email-auth.ts:210-225.
Edge Cases: Session not found returns false; Session expired (> 15 min) deletes session and returns false; Session exists and verified=false returns false; Session exists and verified=true returns true
Notes: Deps: SessionStorage interface

User Story: As a server developer, I want to clean up auth sessions after successful login, so that OTP codes cannot be reused.
Expected Behaviour: cleanupSession() accepts sessionId and optional SessionStorage. Calls storage.delete(sessionId) to remove session. No return value. See email-auth.ts:230-236.
Edge Cases: Cleanup can be called on non-existent session (no-op); SessionStorage.delete() is called regardless of session existence
Notes: Deps: SessionStorage interface

User Story: As a server developer, I want to retrieve auth session data, so that I can access session details like email and OTP ID.
Expected Behaviour: getSession() accepts sessionId and optional SessionStorage. Retrieves session from storage, returns undefined if not found. Checks if session.timestamp exceeds 15-minute timeout, deletes session and returns undefined if expired. Returns EmailAuthSession object with email, subOrgId, otpId, timestamp, verified. See email-auth.ts:241-257.
Edge Cases: Session not found returns undefined; Session expired (> 15 min) deletes session and returns undefined; Valid session returns full EmailAuthSession object
Notes: Deps: SessionStorage interface

User Story: As a developer implementing the auth system, I want a pluggable session storage interface, so that I can swap between in-memory storage (dev) and persistent storage (prod).
Expected Behaviour: createInMemorySessionStorage() returns SessionStorage implementation using Map<string, EmailAuthSession>. Implements: get(sessionId) returns session or undefined, set(sessionId, session) stores session, delete(sessionId) removes session, cleanup() clears Map and stops interval. Starts background cleanup interval every 60 seconds that removes sessions older than 15 minutes. Calls cleanupInterval.unref() to prevent blocking process exit. See email-auth.ts:29-56.
Edge Cases: cleanup() stops interval and clears all sessions; Cleanup interval runs even if no sessions exist; unref() is called if available, but try-catch prevents errors if unavailable; Interval is unref() so it doesn't prevent Node.js process exit
Notes: Deps: Node.js setInterval/clearInterval (with optional unref)

User Story: As a server developer, I want to automatically create or retrieve Turnkey sub-organizations for users, so that each user gets their own secure key management workspace.
Expected Behaviour: getOrCreateTurnkeySubOrg() accepts email and Turnkey client. Generates baseSubOrgName by replacing non-alphanumeric characters with hyphens and lowercasing. Calls turnkeyClient.apiClient().getSubOrgIds() with filterType='EMAIL', filterValue=email to check for existing sub-org. If found and has wallets, returns existing subOrgId. If found but no wallets, logs warning and creates new sub-org. If not found, creates new sub-org. For creation: generates unique subOrgName='${baseSubOrgName}-${Date.now()}', calls createSubOrganization() with rootUsers (email-based), rootQuorumThreshold=1, wallet with 3 accounts (SECP256K1 for auth-key, 2x ED25519 for assertion/update keys). Returns subOrgId or throws error if none returned. See server/turnkey-client.ts:48-149.
Edge Cases: Multiple sub-orgs with same email returns first one (index 0); Existing sub-org with no wallet creates new one with wallet; getSubOrgIds() call failure silently logs and proceeds to create; createSubOrganization() failure throws descriptive error; Timestamp-based uniqueness prevents name collisions
Notes: Deps: Turnkey SDK (@turnkey/sdk-server), TURNKEY_ORGANIZATION_ID environment variable

User Story: As a server developer, I want to initialize a Turnkey client with server credentials, so that I can call Turnkey APIs securely on behalf of users.
Expected Behaviour: createTurnkeyClient() accepts optional TurnkeyClientConfig. Reads apiPublicKey, apiPrivateKey, organizationId from config or environment variables (TURNKEY_API_PUBLIC_KEY, TURNKEY_API_PRIVATE_KEY, TURNKEY_ORGANIZATION_ID). Throws error if any required value is missing. Creates and returns new Turnkey client instance with apiBaseUrl (default 'https://api.turnkey.com'), apiPublicKey, apiPrivateKey, defaultOrganizationId. See server/turnkey-client.ts:21-42.
Edge Cases: Missing TURNKEY_API_PUBLIC_KEY throws descriptive error; Missing TURNKEY_API_PRIVATE_KEY throws descriptive error; Missing TURNKEY_ORGANIZATION_ID throws descriptive error; Config parameters override environment variables
Notes: Deps: Turnkey SDK (@turnkey/sdk-server), Environment variables or config object

User Story: As a server developer, I want to sign DID documents using keys stored in Turnkey, so that I can create verifiable credentials tied to user identity.
Expected Behaviour: TurnkeyWebVHSigner class accepts subOrgId, keyId, publicKeyMultibase, turnkeyClient, verificationMethodId in constructor. sign() method accepts document and proof objects. Calls OriginalsSDK.prepareDIDDataForSigning() to get canonical bytes. Converts bytes to '0x' prefixed hex string. Calls turnkeyClient.apiClient().signRawPayload() with organizationId=subOrgId, signWith=keyId, payload=hex, encoding='PAYLOAD_ENCODING_HEXADECIMAL', hashFunction='HASH_FUNCTION_NO_OP'. Expects response with r and s components. Concatenates r+s (removing '0x' prefix if present). Validates signature is exactly 64 bytes (Ed25519). Encodes as multibase using multikey.encodeMultibase(). Returns {proofValue: multibaseString}. verify() method validates Ed25519 signature given signature bytes, message, publicKey. Returns boolean. getVerificationMethodId() returns verificationMethodId. getPublicKeyMultibase() returns publicKeyMultibase. See server/turnkey-signer.ts:37-150.
Edge Cases: No r or s in Turnkey response throws error; Signature length is 65 bytes (extra byte) trims to 64; Signature length != 64 throws validation error; Signature starting with '0x' is cleaned; Public key is 33 bytes (compressed) trimmed to 32 for Ed25519; Public key != 32 bytes after trim returns false from verify; Ed25519 verification failure returns false (not thrown); SHA512 configuration is set at module load time
Notes: Deps: Turnkey SDK (@turnkey/sdk-server), OriginalsSDK (prepareDIDDataForSigning, multikey), @noble/ed25519 for verification, @noble/hashes/sha2 for SHA512

User Story: As a client-side developer, I want to initialize a Turnkey client for API calls, so that I can interact with Turnkey's OTP and wallet endpoints.
Expected Behaviour: initializeTurnkeyClient() accepts optional config with apiBaseUrl, apiPublicKey, apiPrivateKey, organizationId. Reads from config or environment variables (TURNKEY_API_PUBLIC_KEY, TURNKEY_API_PRIVATE_KEY, TURNKEY_ORGANIZATION_ID). Throws error if any required value missing. Returns Turnkey client instance. See client/turnkey-client.ts:49-75.
Edge Cases: Same validation as server-side client creation; Config parameters override environment variables
Notes: Deps: Turnkey SDK (@turnkey/sdk-server), Environment variables

User Story: As a client-side developer, I want to initiate OTP authentication via Turnkey's API, so that users can receive verification codes.
Expected Behaviour: initOtp() accepts Turnkey client, email, optional subOrgId. Calls turnkeyClient.apiClient().initOtp() with otpType='OTP_TYPE_EMAIL', contact=email, appName='Originals'. Optionally passes organizationId=subOrgId if provided. Throws if no otpId returned. Returns otpId string. Errors wrapped as 'Failed to send OTP: {error.message}'. See client/turnkey-client.ts:80-105.
Edge Cases: No otpId in response throws error; Turnkey API failure throws wrapped error; subOrgId is optional parameter
Notes: Deps: Turnkey SDK

User Story: As a client-side developer, I want to complete OTP verification flow, so that I can obtain a verification token.
Expected Behaviour: completeOtp() accepts Turnkey client, otpId, otpCode (string), subOrgId. Calls turnkeyClient.apiClient().verifyOtp() with otpId, otpCode, expirationSeconds='900' (15 min), organizationId=subOrgId. Throws if no verificationToken returned. Returns {verificationToken, subOrgId}. Errors wrapped as 'Failed to complete OTP: {error.message}'. See client/turnkey-client.ts:111-139.
Edge Cases: No verificationToken in response throws error; OTP verification failure throws wrapped error
Notes: Deps: Turnkey SDK

User Story: As a client-side developer, I want to fetch user details from a Turnkey sub-organization, so that I can access user profile information.
Expected Behaviour: fetchUser() accepts Turnkey client, subOrgId, optional onExpired callback. Wraps with withTokenExpiration() error handling. Calls turnkeyClient.apiClient().getUsers() with organizationId=subOrgId. Returns first user from response (users[0]) or null if empty. Errors wrapped as 'Failed to fetch user: {error.message}'. If API key expired error detected, calls onExpired callback and throws TurnkeySessionExpiredError. See client/turnkey-client.ts:144-163.
Edge Cases: Empty users array returns null; API key expired triggers onExpired callback and throws TurnkeySessionExpiredError; API key expired detection checks for 'api_key_expired', 'expired api key', or code 16
Notes: Deps: Turnkey SDK, withTokenExpiration() wrapper

User Story: As a client-side developer, I want to fetch user's wallets and accounts, so that I can access signing keys for DID operations.
Expected Behaviour: fetchWallets() accepts Turnkey client, subOrgId, optional onExpired callback. Calls getWallets() API to get list of wallets. For each wallet, calls getWalletAccounts() to fetch accounts. Maps each account to TurnkeyWalletAccount with address, curve, path, addressFormat. Returns array of TurnkeyWallet objects with walletId, walletName, accounts array. Wraps with withTokenExpiration() and TurnkeySessionExpiredError on expired keys. Errors wrapped as 'Failed to fetch wallets: {error.message}'. See client/turnkey-client.ts:168-209.
Edge Cases: No wallets returns empty array; Wallet has no accounts (empty array) is valid; API key expiration triggers onExpired callback; Each account fetch is a separate API call
Notes: Deps: Turnkey SDK, withTokenExpiration() wrapper

User Story: As a client-side developer, I want to find a key with a specific curve type from wallets, so that I can select the right key for signing.
Expected Behaviour: getKeyByCurve() accepts wallets array and curve type ('CURVE_SECP256K1' or 'CURVE_ED25519'). Iterates through wallets and accounts, returns first account matching curve. Returns null if no match found. See client/turnkey-client.ts:214-226.
Edge Cases: Empty wallets array returns null; No matching curve returns null; Returns first matching account (doesn't check for duplicates); Curve comparison is exact string match
Notes: Deps: None

User Story: As a client-side developer, I want to create a new wallet with specific account types, so that I can set up signing keys for DID operations.
Expected Behaviour: createWalletWithAccounts() accepts Turnkey client, subOrgId, optional onExpired callback. Calls createWallet() API with walletName='default-wallet', accounts=[SECP256K1 path m/44'/0'/0'/0/0, ED25519 path m/44'/501'/0'/0', ED25519 path m/44'/501'/1'/0']. Gets walletId from response. Waits 500ms for wallet to be created. Calls fetchWallets() to retrieve created wallet. Returns the created wallet object. Wraps with withTokenExpiration() error handling. Errors wrapped as 'Failed to create wallet: {error.message}'. See client/turnkey-client.ts:231-286.
Edge Cases: No walletId returned throws error; 500ms wait is hardcoded delay (may be insufficient for slow APIs); fetchWallets() may return wallet not in original list (handles async); Failed to find created wallet throws error
Notes: Deps: Turnkey SDK, fetchWallets() function, withTokenExpiration() wrapper

User Story: As a client-side developer, I want to ensure a user has a wallet with all required accounts, so that I can guarantee signing keys are available for DID creation.
Expected Behaviour: ensureWalletWithAccounts() accepts Turnkey client, subOrgId, optional onExpired callback. Fetches existing wallets. If no wallets, creates new wallet via createWalletWithAccounts(). If wallets exist, checks if they have required accounts: >=1 SECP256K1 and >=2 ED25519. If missing accounts, calls createWalletAccounts() API to add missing accounts. Refetches wallets after creation. Returns array of TurnkeyWallet objects. Wraps with withTokenExpiration() error handling. Errors wrapped as 'Failed to ensure wallet with accounts: {error.message}'. See client/turnkey-client.ts:291-364.
Edge Cases: No wallets creates new wallet and returns it; Existing wallet with all accounts returns unchanged; Existing wallet with missing SECP256K1 creates it; Existing wallet with missing ED25519 accounts creates up to 2; Path indices for ED25519: 0->m/44'/501'/0'/0', 1->m/44'/501'/1'/0'; Multiple account creation in single API call if needed
Notes: Deps: Turnkey SDK, fetchWallets() function, createWalletWithAccounts() function, withTokenExpiration() wrapper

User Story: As a client-side developer, I want to detect and handle Turnkey API key expiration, so that I can trigger re-authentication flows.
Expected Behaviour: withTokenExpiration() accepts async function fn and optional onExpired callback. Calls fn() and returns result. On error, checks if error JSON contains 'api_key_expired', 'expired api key', or code 16. If expired pattern found, calls onExpired() callback if provided, throws new TurnkeySessionExpiredError(). Otherwise rethrows original error. Used to wrap all Turnkey API calls. See client/turnkey-client.ts:22-43.
Edge Cases: Non-expired error passes through unchanged; JSON.stringify(error) may fail for circular refs, but message is checked; onExpired callback is optional, called only if provided; Error detection is case-insensitive toLowerCase(); Code '16' detection for numeric error codes
Notes: Deps: TurnkeySessionExpiredError class

User Story: As a client-side developer, I want to distinguish API key expiration errors, so that I can trigger appropriate re-authentication flows.
Expected Behaviour: TurnkeySessionExpiredError extends Error. Constructor accepts optional message (default 'Your Turnkey session has expired. Please log in again.'). Sets name='TurnkeySessionExpiredError'. Used to indicate Turnkey session/API key expiration vs other errors. See client/turnkey-client.ts:12-17.
Edge Cases: Custom message can be provided on construction; Error is catchable as Error or TurnkeySessionExpiredError
Notes: Deps: None

User Story: As a client-side developer, I want to send OTP requests through my server (which holds Turnkey credentials), so that I can use email authentication securely.
Expected Behaviour: sendOtp() accepts email, optional endpoint (default '/api/auth/send-otp'), optional ServerAuthOptions with fetch function. Makes POST request to endpoint with JSON body {email}. If response not ok, extracts error.message from response JSON or returns 'Failed to send OTP'. Throws error with message. On success, returns response JSON as InitiateAuthResult {sessionId, message}. Errors wrapped as 'Failed to send OTP' if no server message provided. See client/server-auth.ts:43-61.
Edge Cases: response.ok=false throws error; response.json() failure on error path returns {message: 'Failed to send OTP'}; Custom fetch function can be provided for testing; Default fetch is global browser/Node.js fetch
Notes: Deps: Fetch API (or custom fetch function)

User Story: As a client-side developer, I want to verify OTP codes through my server, so that I can complete email authentication securely.
Expected Behaviour: verifyOtp() accepts sessionId, code (OTP string), optional endpoint (default '/api/auth/verify-otp'), optional ServerAuthOptions with fetch function. Makes POST request to endpoint with JSON body {sessionId, code}. If response not ok, extracts error.message from response JSON or returns 'Verification failed'. Throws error with message. On success, returns response JSON as VerifyAuthResult {verified, email, subOrgId}. Errors wrapped as 'Verification failed' if no server message provided. See client/server-auth.ts:82-101.
Edge Cases: response.ok=false throws error; response.json() failure on error path returns {message: 'Verification failed'}; Custom fetch function can be provided for testing; verified must be boolean in response
Notes: Deps: Fetch API (or custom fetch function)

User Story: As a client-side developer, I want to sign DID documents using Turnkey keys, so that I can create verifiable DIDs tied to user identity.
Expected Behaviour: TurnkeyDIDSigner class accepts turnkeyClient, signWith (key address), subOrgId, publicKeyMultibase, optional onExpired callback. sign() method accepts {document, proof} input. Calls OriginalsSDK.prepareDIDDataForSigning(document, proof) to get bytes. Calls turnkeyClient.apiClient().signRawPayload() with organizationId=subOrgId, signWith, payload=Buffer hex string, encoding='PAYLOAD_ENCODING_HEXADECIMAL', hashFunction='HASH_FUNCTION_NO_OP'. Extracts r and s from result. Combines cleanR + cleanS into 64-byte signature. Validates signature length is exactly 64 bytes. Encodes as multibase using encoding.multibase.encode(signatureBytes, 'base58btc'). Returns {proofValue}. Wraps with withTokenExpiration() error handling. verify() calls OriginalsSDK.verifyDIDSignature() with signature, message, publicKey. Returns boolean. getVerificationMethodId() returns 'did:key:${publicKeyMultibase}'. See client/turnkey-did-signer.ts:25-130.
Edge Cases: r or s missing in Turnkey response throws error; Invalid signature length throws validation error; Signature cleanup removes '0x' prefix if present; API key expiration detected in sign() calls onExpired and throws TurnkeySessionExpiredError; Expiration detection checks for 'api_key_expired', 'expired api key', or code 16; verify() catches verification errors and returns false instead of throwing
Notes: Deps: Turnkey SDK, OriginalsSDK (prepareDIDDataForSigning, verifyDIDSignature, encoding.multibase), withTokenExpiration() wrapper

User Story: As a client-side developer, I want to create a DID:WebVH document with Turnkey signing, so that I can establish user identity on the Originals Protocol.
Expected Behaviour: createDIDWithTurnkey() accepts params object with: turnkeyClient, updateKeyAccount (TurnkeyWalletAccount), subOrgId, authKeyPublic (multibase), assertionKeyPublic (multibase), updateKeyPublic (multibase), domain, slug, optional onExpired callback. Creates TurnkeyDIDSigner with updateKeyAccount.address as signWith, subOrgId, updateKeyPublic. Calls OriginalsSDK.createDIDOriginal() with type='did', domain, signer, verifier (both = TurnkeyDIDSigner), updateKeys=[signer.getVerificationMethodId()], verificationMethods=[{id: '#key-0', type: 'Multikey', controller: '', publicKeyMultibase: authKeyPublic}, {id: '#key-1', type: 'Multikey', controller: '', publicKeyMultibase: assertionKeyPublic}], paths=[slug], portable=false, authentication=['#key-0'], assertionMethod=['#key-1']. Returns {did, didDocument: result.doc, didLog: result.log}. See client/turnkey-did-signer.ts:135-203.
Edge Cases: Two verification methods: #key-0 (auth), #key-1 (assertion); TurnkeyDIDSigner is both signer and verifier; updateKeyAccount.address is used as signWith key ID; portable=false indicates domain-bound DID; updateKeys contains signer verification method ID; onExpired callback passed through to signer
Notes: Deps: Turnkey SDK, OriginalsSDK.createDIDOriginal(), TurnkeyDIDSigner class

User Story: As a developer, I want to emit and handle structured errors with error codes and details so that I can programmatically distinguish between failure types
Expected Behaviour: StructuredError (telemetry.ts) extends Error with `code: string` and optional `details: Record<string, unknown>`. Integrates with TelemetryHooks via `emitError()` which invokes hooks.onError if defined (telemetry.ts:35-42)
Edge Cases: emitError and emitTelemetry silently catch and ignore exceptions from hook callbacks (telemetry.ts:29, 40); hooks parameter may be undefined; functions handle gracefully; Error details object may contain nested structures
Notes: Deps: Error (built-in), TelemetryHooks interface

User Story: As an operator, I want to emit telemetry events with optional attributes and severity levels so that I can observe SDK operations
Expected Behaviour: TelemetryEvent emitted via emitTelemetry() (telemetry.ts:25-32) which calls hooks.onEvent() if defined. Events include name, optional level (defaults to 'info'), and optional attributes Record<string, unknown>
Edge Cases: Hooks.onEvent may be undefined; function returns silently; Hook callback exceptions are caught and ignored to avoid disrupting main flow; Level defaults to 'info' if not specified
Notes: Deps: TelemetryHooks interface

User Story: As an application operator, I want to log messages at different levels with hierarchical context, multiple outputs, and sanitization so that I can debug operations and monitor production systems
Expected Behaviour: Logger (Logger.ts) provides debug/info/warn/error methods. Supports ConsoleLogOutput and FileLogOutput implementations. Child loggers nest context (Logger.ts:212-221). Entries include timestamp, level, context, message, optional data and duration. Sanitization redacts keys containing 'private', 'key', 'secret', 'password', 'token', 'credential' (Logger.ts:284-322). FileLogOutput batches writes with 1s flush interval using Bun file API (Logger.ts:94-120)
Edge Cases: FileLogOutput catches file write errors and falls back to console (Logger.ts:116-119); Async log outputs (returning Promise) don't block logging, errors logged to console (Logger.ts:264-270); Level filtering prevents below-threshold messages from being logged (Logger.ts:242-244); Child logger inherits all parent settings including outputs and thresholds; Sanitize data recursively processes arrays and nested objects (Logger.ts:289-322)
Notes: Deps: OriginalsConfig interface, LogOutput interface, Bun file API (for FileLogOutput), performance.now() (for timing)

User Story: As an operator, I want to collect and export operation metrics, asset lifecycle counts, and error statistics in JSON or Prometheus format so that I can monitor SDK performance and health
Expected Behaviour: MetricsCollector (MetricsCollector.ts) tracks: asset creation count (recordAssetCreated:138-140), layer migrations with transition keys (recordMigration:145-153), asset transfers (recordTransfer:158-160), operation performance by name with min/max/avg/total times (recordOperation:101-121), error counts by code (recordError:165-174), cache hits/misses (recordCacheHit/Miss:179-188). Exports via export() method in JSON (exportJSON:285-287) or Prometheus (exportPrometheus:292-373) format. getMetrics() returns snapshot (Metrics interface, 30-52). track() wraps async operations for automatic timing and error recording (85-96). getOperationMetrics() retrieves single operation stats (239-254). reset() clears all metrics (259-267)
Edge Cases: Migration keys use format 'shortname→shortname' derived from layer names by splitting on ':' (recordMigration:147-149); Operation metrics with count=0 compute avgTime as 0, minTime/maxTime as 0 (exportJSON data computation:200-204); Cache stats only included if totalCacheRequests > 0 (getMetrics:217-222); Prometheus export sanitizes operation names replacing non-alphanumeric with '_' (exportPrometheus:318); track() catches error, records failure, then re-throws (85-96)
Notes: Deps: LayerType interface, Date API, performance.now() (for timing)

User Story: As a developer working with Bitcoin assets, I want to validate Bitcoin addresses for specific networks (mainnet/regtest/signet) so that I can ensure addresses are properly formatted and checksummed before use
Expected Behaviour: validateBitcoinAddress() (bitcoin-address.ts:55-116) validates address format and checksum using bitcoinjs-lib's address.toOutputScript(). Supports 'mainnet', 'regtest', 'signet' networks. Trims input, checks length (26-90 chars), rejects mock/test addresses (regex /^(mock-|test-)/i). For regtest, falls back to testnet validation. Throws descriptive Error on failure; isValidBitcoinAddress() wrapper returns boolean. getNetwork() maps network names to bitcoinjs-lib configs (11-28)
Edge Cases: Empty or whitespace-only input throws Error (57-65); regtest network attempts testnet validation as fallback (88-94); Input must be string; non-string throws (57-59); Address length must be 26-90 chars (72-75); Mock/test addresses explicitly rejected (68-70)
Notes: Deps: bitcoinjs-lib library (bitcoin.networks, bitcoin.address.toOutputScript)

User Story: As a developer working with Bitcoin ordinals, I want to validate satoshi identifiers and parse them from multiple formats (plain numbers, did:btco DIDs) so that I can ensure valid ordinal identifiers
Expected Behaviour: validateSatoshiNumber() (satoshi-validation.ts:31-95) checks for null/undefined/empty, validates numeric format (/^[0-9]+$/), checks if finite, rejects decimals, rejects negatives, and validates range (0 to MAX_SATOSHI_SUPPLY = 2,100,000,000,000,000). parseSatoshiIdentifier() (115-176) supports plain satoshi '123456', did:btco:123456 (mainnet), did:btco:test:123456 (testnet), did:btco:sig:123456 (signet). assertValidSatoshi() (191-196) throws StructuredError if invalid
Edge Cases: Whitespace-only strings rejected after trim (44-48); String-to-number conversion via Number() may lose precision for very large numbers; mitigation checks Number.isFinite() (63-68); Number() followed by Math.floor comparison catches attempted decimal strings (71-76); Negative zero (-0) treated as 0 (79-84); did:btco format requires exactly 3 parts (mainnet) or 4 parts (network-specific) (142-160); Network parameter in did:btco must be 'test' or 'sig'; others rejected (148-153)
Notes: Deps: StructuredError from telemetry module, MAX_SATOSHI_SUPPLY constant

User Story: As a credential verifier, I want to compute a canonical digest of a credential and proof so that I can verify signatures remain valid across credential serialization
Expected Behaviour: computeCredentialDigest() (credential-digest.ts:18-43) computes sha256(canonicalized_proof) || sha256(canonicalized_credential). Creates proofInput by copying proofBase and deleting proofValue and publicKeyMultibase (22-24). Injects credential @context into proofInput if absent (26-29). Creates unsigned copy of credential by deleting proof (31-32). Canonicalizes both via canonicalizeDocument (34-35), hashes each with sha256, concatenates hashes into Uint8Array (36-42)
Edge Cases: If credential lacks @context, proofInput won't have @context injected (26-29); publicKeyMultibase deliberately excluded from hash input (24); If proof or credential missing properties, Object.entries works on remaining keys; Uint8Array concatenation via set() at offset (39-41)
Notes: Deps: canonicalizeDocument from serialization module, sha256 from @noble/hashes/sha2, Buffer

Feature Name: Encoding Utilities (Multibase, Multikey, Base64, Base58, UTF-8)
User Story: As a developer working with cryptographic keys and identifiers, I want to encode/decode data in various standard formats (multibase, multikey, base64url, base58) so that I can interoperate with W3C standards
Expected Behaviour: encoding.ts provides: encodeBase64UrlMultibase() returns 'z' + base64url (3-5), decodeBase64UrlMultibase() validates 'z' prefix and decodes (7-11), hexToBytes() parses hex strings with optional '0x' prefix (14-29). Exports base64, utf8, base64url, base58, multibase, multikey objects with encode/decode methods. multibase.encode() adds header 'z' for base58btc or 'u' for base64url (100-108); multibase.decode() parses header (109-117). multikey.encode() prepends header + val and multibase-encodes (120-125); multikey.decode() validates header match and returns key bytes (126-135). Multicodec headers defined as constants (MULTICODEC_ED25519_PUB_HEADER, etc., 36-50)
Edge Cases: hexToBytes rejects odd-length hex strings (16-18); hexToBytes accepts both '0x' prefixed and non-prefixed hex (14-16); base64url manually implements padding and re-padding logic (71-80); multibase.decode expects 'z' or 'u' header; throws on missing header (109-115); multikey.decode validates header byte-by-byte and throws if mismatch (128-131); b58 library requires type assertion due to loose types (82-88)
Notes: Deps: b58 library, @noble/hashes, Buffer, TextEncoder/TextDecoder

User Story: As a credential issuer/verifier, I want to serialize, deserialize, and canonicalize JSON-LD documents so that I can ensure consistent hashing and round-trip fidelity
Expected Behaviour: serialization.ts provides: serializeDIDDocument() JSON.stringifies with 2-space indent (47-50), deserializeDIDDocument() parses JSON as DIDDocument type (52-60), serializeCredential() JSON.stringifies VC (62-65), deserializeCredential() parses JSON as VerifiableCredential (67-75). canonicalizeDocument() (77-102) uses jsonld.canonize with URDNA2015 algorithm, preloaded contexts, safe mode (errors on undefined terms), and rdfDirection='i18n-datatype'. PRELOADED_CONTEXTS (20-36) includes W3C standard contexts and Originals network contexts for all three networks (pichu/production, cleffa/staging, magby/development). defaultDocumentLoader resolves URLs from PRELOADED_CONTEXTS (39-45)
Edge Cases: JSON.parse errors catch and throw 'Invalid DID Document JSON' or equivalent (54-60, 68-75); Canonicalization fails if document references unknown context not in PRELOADED_CONTEXTS (44); safe=true mode throws on undefined JSON-LD terms (prevents silent exclusion); All three Originals networks reference identical originalsContext object (32-35); canonicalizeDocument provides documentLoader override via options (79-80)
Notes: Deps: jsonld library, DIDDocument and VerifiableCredential types, Context JSON files (credentials-v1, credentials-v2, data-integrity-v2, dids, ed25519-2020, ordinals-plus, originals)

Feature Name: DID, Credential, and DID Document Validation
User Story: As a developer, I want to validate DIDs, credentials, and DID documents against W3C specifications so that I can reject malformed or incomplete data early
Expected Behaviour: validation.ts provides: validateDID() checks regex /^did:([a-z0-9]+):(.*)/ and verifies method is in ['peer', 'webvh', 'btco'] (5-26). validateCredential() (28-76) checks @context (array with 'https://www.w3.org/2018/credentials/v1'), type (array including 'VerifiableCredential'), issuer (DID string or object.id as DID), issuanceDate (valid ISO timestamp), credentialSubject (present). validateDIDDocument() (78-115) validates @context, id (valid DID), verificationMethod array (each has id/type/controller/publicKeyMultibase with 'z' prefix), optional controller array (all valid DIDs). hashResource() computes SHA-256 of Uint8Array content and returns hex string (117-122)
Edge Cases: validateCredential issuer accepts string DID or {id: string} with valid DID (54-62); issuanceDate must parse as valid Date (Date.parse must not return NaN) (67-69); publicKeyMultibase must start with 'z' (base58 multibase indicator) (99-101); controller array is optional; if present, must contain all DIDs (108-112); validateDID returns false without throwing; used internally by other validators
Notes: Deps: DIDDocument and VerifiableCredential types, sha256 from @noble/hashes/sha2, bytesToHex from @noble/hashes/utils

User Story: As a developer, I want to encode and decode data in CBOR format so that I can serialize event logs and other binary-compatible structures
Expected Behaviour: cbor.ts provides: encode() (8-12) calls cbor.encode(input) and wraps ArrayBuffer in Uint8Array. decode() (14-30) accepts Uint8Array, ArrayBuffer, or Buffer; converts to ArrayBuffer handling byteOffset and byteLength for slicing (17-28); calls cbor.decode() and returns typed result
Edge Cases: decode handles Uint8Array.buffer offset and length via slice (19-20); decode explicitly handles Buffer type with byteOffset/byteLength (23-27); decode returns generic T type; caller responsible for correctness; encode wraps ArrayBuffer (not stored directly) in new Uint8Array
Notes: Deps: cbor-js library

User Story: As an operator, I want to subscribe to SDK events and log them at configurable levels while automatically extracting metrics so that I can monitor asset lifecycle and batch operations
Expected Behaviour: EventLogger (EventLogger.ts) integrates Logger and MetricsCollector. subscribeToEvents() (85-107) subscribes to event types via eventEmitter.on() and calls handleEvent() for each. configureEventLogging() (112-114) merges event logging config (DEFAULT_EVENT_CONFIG:45-66). handleEvent() (129-145) extracts metrics, then logs if not disabled (logLevel !== false). logEvent() (150-271) formats event message and data by type (asset:created, asset:migrated, asset:transferred, resource:published, credential:issued, resource:version:created, verification:completed, batch:started/completed/failed). extractMetrics() (276-296) calls metricsCollector.recordAssetCreated/Migration/Transfer for lifecycle events. unsubscribe() (119-124) removes all event listeners
Edge Cases: Metrics are extracted regardless of logging level (134-135); logLevel false disables logging for that event type but metrics still recorded (137-139); Unknown event types silently return from logEvent (253-254); Unsubscribe clears unsubscribeFns array after calling all functions (119-124); Event data extraction accesses nested properties (e.g., event.asset.id, event.resource.url)
Notes: Deps: Logger class, MetricsCollector class, EventEmitter interface, OriginalsEvent and EventTypeMap types

User Story: As a developer, I want to wrap provider calls with a circuit breaker so that cascading failures stop being attempted after threshold and the system can recover gracefully
Expected Behaviour: CircuitBreaker (circuit-breaker.ts) manages state transitions (CLOSED→OPEN→HALF_OPEN). execute() (47-75) throws CIRCUIT_OPEN StructuredError if open and reset timeout not elapsed (52-64); attempts function and calls onSuccess/onFailure. onSuccess() (86-91) resets failures to 0, transitions HALF_OPEN→CLOSED. onFailure() (93-103) increments consecutiveFailures, updates lastFailureTime; in HALF_OPEN any failure goes to OPEN (99); in CLOSED at threshold transitions to OPEN (100-102).
