Callbacks: onStateChange (105-109). reset() (77-84) clears state. withCircuitBreaker() (116-132) wraps target object via Proxy, intercepts method calls to route through breaker and exposes circuitBreaker property
Edge Cases: State transitions only if transitioning to different state (88-91, 100-102); lastFailureTime used to measure reset timeout; Date.now() - lastFailureTime >= resetTimeoutMs (49-50); HALF_OPEN→CLOSED only on success; any failure goes to OPEN (97-99); withCircuitBreaker creates Proxy; non-function properties pass through (123-126); execute() catches error, calls onFailure, then re-throws (67-75)
Notes: Deps: StructuredError from telemetry, Date.now(), Proxy (for withCircuitBreaker)

User Story: As a developer, I want to retry failed operations with exponential backoff and jitter so that I can handle transient failures gracefully
Expected Behaviour: withRetry() (retry.ts:31-46) loops up to maxRetries attempts. Computes delay via computeDelay() (25-29): baseDelayMs * backoffFactor^attempt + random jitter, clamped to maxDelayMs. Sleeps via sleep() (21-23). Calls onRetry callback before each delay (41). Throws lastError if all attempts exhausted or isRetriable returns false (39, 45). Defaults: maxRetries=3, baseDelayMs=300, maxDelayMs=10000, backoffFactor=2, jitterFactor=0.1, isRetriable=()=>true, onRetry=()=>{}
Edge Cases: attempt starts at 0; attempt + 1 passed to onRetry for 1-indexed display (41); jitter is ±(jitterFactor * exponential_delay); computed as (Math.random()*2-1)*factor*exp (27-28); Delay clamped to [0, maxDelayMs] after jitter (28); lastError preserved across all attempts; final throw uses lastError not current (33, 45); isRetriable checked after failure; false breaks loop immediately (39); maxRetries=3 means up to 4 total attempts (0, 1, 2, 3)
Notes: Deps: setTimeout (for sleep), Math.random(), Math.pow(), Math.floor(), Math.min(), Math.max()

User Story: As a developer, I want to compute SHA-256 hashes of strings or binary data so that I can generate content digests
Expected Behaviour: sha256Bytes() (hash.ts:1-11) accepts string or Uint8Array input. Converts string to Uint8Array via TextEncoder. Accesses globalThis.crypto.subtle (SubtleCrypto interface). Calls subtle.digest('SHA-256', data) and returns Uint8Array result
Edge Cases: crypto.subtle may be undefined; function throws 'SubtleCrypto not available' (5-7); Uint8Array must be cast to ArrayBuffer for SubtleCrypto compatibility (9); TextEncoder always available in modern environments
Notes: Deps: globalThis.crypto.subtle (Web Crypto API), TextEncoder

User Story: As an SDK user, I want to provide custom storage implementations so that I can store assets in my preferred backend (S3, GCS, etc.)
Expected Behaviour: StorageAdapter (adapters/types.ts:11-15) defines interface: put(objectKey, data, options?) returns Promise<string> (upload location/URL), get(objectKey) returns Promise<StorageGetResult | null> with content Buffer and contentType string, delete(objectKey)? returns Promise<boolean>. StoragePutOptions (1-4) includes optional contentType and cacheControl headers
Edge Cases: delete is optional (?) method for adapters not supporting deletion; get returns null if object not found; caller must handle; put returns string (location/URL); exact format adapter-specific
Notes: Deps: Buffer type

User Story: As an SDK user, I want to provide custom fee rate estimation so that I can integrate with my preferred blockchain fee estimation service
Expected Behaviour: FeeOracleAdapter (adapters/types.ts:17-20) defines single method: estimateFeeRate(targetBlocks?) returns Promise<number> with sats/vB (or network-appropriate feerate unit). FeeOracleMock (FeeOracleMock.ts:3-8) implements with default feeRate=7; returns Math.max(1, feeRate - (targetBlocks - 1))
Edge Cases: targetBlocks defaults to 1 if not provided; FeeOracleMock returns minimum 1 sats/vB (Math.max(1, ...)); Mock fee decreases linearly with increased targetBlocks
Notes: Deps: None (pure interface)

User Story: As an SDK user, I want to provide custom ordinals/inscriptions implementations so that I can integrate with my preferred indexer or blockchain node
Expected Behaviour: OrdinalsProvider (adapters/types.ts:22-65) defines methods: getInscriptionById(id) returns inscription with content Buffer, contentType, txid, vout, optional satoshi/blockHeight. getInscriptionsBySatoshi(satoshi) returns array of {inscriptionId}. broadcastTransaction(txHexOrObj) returns txid string. getTransactionStatus(txid) returns {confirmed, blockHeight?, confirmations?}. estimateFee(blocks?) returns Promise<number>. createInscription(params) returns {inscriptionId, revealTxId, commitTxId?, satoshi?, txid?, vout?, blockHeight?, content?, contentType?, feeRate?}. transferInscription(inscriptionId, toAddress, options?) returns {txid, vin[], vout[], fee, blockHeight?, confirmations?, satoshi?}
Edge Cases: getInscriptionById returns null if not found; getInscriptionsBySatoshi returns empty array if none found; Multiple inscriptions may reference same satoshi; createInscription response fields are mostly optional; transferInscription.vin and vout are complex nested structures
Notes: Deps: Buffer type

User Story: As a developer, I want to test ordinals operations without hitting live APIs so that I can mock inscriptions and transactions
Expected Behaviour: OrdMockProvider (adapters/providers/OrdMockProvider.ts) implements OrdinalsProvider. Maintains in-memory state with inscriptionsById Map, inscriptionsBySatoshi Map, feeRate. getInscriptionById copies stored record or returns null (29-32). getInscriptionsBySatoshi returns array of {inscriptionId} (34-37). broadcastTransaction returns 'mock-broadcast-txid' (39-41). getTransactionStatus returns {confirmed: true, blockHeight: 1, confirmations: 1} (43-45). estimateFee returns Math.max(1, feeRate - (blocks - 1)) (47-49). createInscription generates random IDs, satoshi (as numeric string), stores in state, returns inscription object (51-82). transferInscription looks up inscription, generates new txid, returns transfer tx shape (84-99)
Edge Cases: createInscription satoshi generated as string of random digits (55); createInscription stores in both inscriptionsById and inscriptionsBySatoshi (66-69); transferInscription throws if inscription not found (86-88); Constructor merges partial state via spread operator (21-26)
Notes: Deps: OrdinalsProvider interface

User Story: As a developer, I want to connect to a remote ordinals indexer API so that I can fetch live inscription data
Expected Behaviour: OrdHttpProvider (adapters/providers/OrdHttpProvider.ts) implements OrdinalsProvider with HTTP requests. Constructor requires baseUrl option (26-31). getInscriptionById() (33-64) fetches /inscription/{id}, parses owner_output for txid:vout, fetches content, returns inscription object. getInscriptionsBySatoshi() (66-71) fetches /sat/{satoshi}, returns inscription_ids array. broadcastTransaction() returns 'broadcast-txid' placeholder (73-76). getTransactionStatus() returns {confirmed: false} placeholder (78-80). estimateFee() returns 5 * Math.max(1, blocks) (82-85). createInscription() returns placeholder result (87-102). transferInscription() returns placeholder tx structure (104-114). createOrdinalsProviderFromEnv() (117-125) checks USE_LIVE_ORD_PROVIDER env var; returns OrdHttpProvider if true with ORD_PROVIDER_BASE_URL, else OrdMockProvider
Edge Cases: fetchJson returns null on non-200 response (14-21); buildUrl handles missing trailing slash and leading slash (8-11); Fetch globalThis requires polyfill in Node.js; Buffer handling for content response (51-53); owner_output parsing assumes 'txid:vout' format (41-45)
Notes: Deps: globalThis.fetch API, globalThis.Buffer

User Story: As a developer, I want a single verify() entry point that automatically detects document type and delegates to the appropriate verifier so that I can handle heterogeneous verification tasks
Expected Behaviour: UnifiedVerifier (UnifiedVerifier.ts) is PoC (not yet shipped). classifyDocument() (31-44) heuristically discriminates: checks if doc.events is array (eventLog); checks if doc.type includes 'VerifiableCredential' (credential); else unknown. UnifiedVerifier.verify() (54-75) classifies, then: for credential delegates to Verifier.verifyCredential() (59-61); for eventLog delegates to verifyEventLog() (64-65); for unknown returns error result. Returns normalized UnifiedVerificationResult (kind, verified, errors, details)
Edge Cases: classifyDocument returns 'unknown' for non-object input (32); Type can be string or array; normalized to array for checking (40-41); Details field may contain verifier-specific result structure; Error array always present, may be empty if verified=true
Notes: Deps: Verifier class (from vc module), verifyEventLog function (from cel module), DIDManager, VerifiableCredential and EventLog types
